active.title: Design handoff — surface map from Figma V1
active.created: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.updated: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.body:
## Context

PROGRAMME-001 requires a final Figma-designed UX (V1) before any shell implementation work begins. Enzo is reworking the design in Figma after Stitch was flagged for consistency issues by Codex. Once Figma V1 is complete, this ticket governs the handoff into WS-SHELL. The handoff must produce a surface map (which pages are visible now / hidden / future) and a component extraction plan, so that engineering can start WS-SHELL without design ambiguity. This is the Phase 1 gate ticket.

## Current State

- Figma rework in progress. Expected complete within days of 2026-04-07.
- Prior design research: `01_Repos/zo/app/docs/stitch-import/` contains design-phasing.md, inventory.md, route-mapping.md, component-extraction-plan.md, shared-shell-normalization.md, stitch-vs-current-platform-diff.md — written during the Stitch pass. These are useful reference but not the final design.
- Current surface phasing decisions (from stitch-import/design-phasing.md): Home, Assistant, Search, Companies, Company Workspace, Sourcing, Settings, Admin/Data Health → implement now. Analysis → design now, wire later. People directory, Person profile, Portfolio, Reconnect → future/hidden.
- Current movecapital-platform shell: Next.js at port 3100. Auth: Keycloak. DB: Supabase (port 54322).

## Task

This ticket activates when Enzo completes the Figma V1 design. At that point:

1. Review the Figma V1 design against the current stitch-import surface phasing decisions.
2. Produce a final surface map document at `01_Repos/zo/app/docs/v1-surface-map.md` with:
   - Every surface (page + major panel) classified as: `IMPLEMENT-NOW` | `DESIGN-NOW-WIRE-LATER` | `FUTURE-HIDDEN`
   - For each `IMPLEMENT-NOW` surface: the real data source it connects to (project-ninpo table / deal-rag endpoint / transcript-rag endpoint / etc.)
   - For each `FUTURE-HIDDEN` surface: the prerequisite that would unlock it (e.g. "People directory: unlocks when person_identity populated and WS-CRM Phase 4 complete")
3. Produce a component extraction plan at `01_Repos/zo/app/docs/v1-component-plan.md` with:
   - Figma components → Next.js component mapping
   - Which components can be ported from MoveCapital_UX and which must be built new
4. Get Enzo sign-off on the surface map (this is the Phase 1 gate).
5. Update the design-phasing.md in stitch-import/ to reflect the final decisions, or note that v1-surface-map.md supersedes it.

## Execution Notes

- Do not start building components until Enzo approves the surface map. The surface map is the gate.
- When reviewing Figma, check alignment with the data currently available in Supabase (TKT-0002 results). Do not design surfaces around data that does not yet exist unless they are FUTURE-HIDDEN.
- The stitch-import/ docs are research artifacts — they inform but do not lock the design.
- Do not modify any running service or database during this ticket.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Figma V1 design received from Enzo
- [ ] `docs/v1-surface-map.md` written and approved by Enzo
- [ ] `docs/v1-component-plan.md` written
- [ ] Every surface classified with its data source or prerequisite
- [ ] design-phasing.md updated or superseded note added
- [ ] TKT-0006 (WS-SHELL) unblocked — engineering can start

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-07 | claude-code | Ticket created. Current state: Figma rework in progress (ETA days). Prior Stitch design research exists in stitch-import/ but not the final design. This ticket is waiting on Enzo completing Figma V1. Next: activate when Figma V1 is delivered.

active.title: Replace P0 misleading surfaces in platform shell
active.created: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.updated: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.body:
## Context

PROGRAMME-001 requires a demo-ready, honest platform shell (Phase 2 gate). Currently, several P0 surfaces in movecapital-platform are seeded with mock data, are shallower than they appear, or actively mislead users about what is real. This ticket covers the replacement pass for all P0 surfaces. It is blocked on TKT-0005 (Figma V1 surface map) because the exact target UX must be locked before building. Once unblocked, this is the largest single implementation ticket in the programme and will require multiple handoff sessions.

## Current State

App runs at `http://127.0.0.1:3100`. Auth: Keycloak at `docker-compose.auth.yml`. DB: Supabase on port 54322. Ollama: local. Meilisearch: port 7700.

P0 surfaces and their current truth status (from `docs/page-audit.md` and `TODO.md`):

- **Home** (`components/DashboardHome.tsx`): static "Today" cards and seeded watchlist cues. Needs real user work state from sessions, threads, pinned companies, sourcing runs.
- **Assistant** (`components/AssistantChat.tsx`): multi-turn real, but underdelivers on explicit attachment selection, governed routing posture. Port from `MoveCapital_UX/components/AssistantChat.tsx`, `AssistantDrawer.tsx`, `lib/api.ts`.
- **Company workspace**: mixed summary board. Fix demo-slug precedence in `lib/company-data.ts`. Mine `MoveCapital_UX/components/DealsExplorer.tsx`, `zo/services/deal-rag/deal-rag-api/main.py`, `zo/services/transcript-rag-api/main.py`.
- **Search**: live multi-backend results exist but seeded Persons results shown alongside real backends. Add real transcript/call/document destinations.
- **Persons page**: powered by `lib/platform-data.ts` (mock). Decision: hide or demote until real.
- **Sourcing** (`fundraise-intel` wired): real backend, but only overview level. Port event detail and evidence drilldown from `zo/services/fundraise-intel/src/fundraise_intel/app.py`.
- **Analysis**: worker trigger real, but page is mostly a control panel. Connect evidence selection and outputs.

Live sidecars:
- `DEAL_RAG_API_URL` — deal document search and chunk retrieval
- `TRANSCRIPT_RAG_API_URL` — transcript search and call lists
- `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_URL` — sourcing events
- `COMPS_WORKER_URL` — comps pipeline

## Task

Execute P0 replacement pass in this order (break into separate handoff sessions as needed):

**Session 1 — Hide misleading surfaces and fix company data**
1. Hide Persons page from nav (not delete — just gate it as future).
2. Fix `lib/company-data.ts` demo-slug precedence so real company routes don't yield demo workspaces.
3. Remove seeded watchlist cues from `components/DashboardHome.tsx`. Replace with real recent threads + pinned companies from DB.

**Session 2 — Assistant depth**
4. Port attachment selection model from `MoveCapital_UX/components/AssistantChat.tsx` and `AssistantDrawer.tsx`.
5. Wire attachment list to `lib/api.ts`. Keep message persistence and replay.
6. Remove/downgrade provider-route panel if it is still seeded theater.

**Session 3 — Company workspace**
7. Replace mixed summary board with real evidence workspace using deal files and transcript excerpts.
8. Port patterns from `MoveCapital_UX/components/DealsExplorer.tsx`.
9. Verify deal path helpers and external file links work.

**Session 4 — Search and Sourcing**
10. Remove seeded Persons results from search surface.
11. Add transcript and document destinations to search.
12. Port event detail and evidence drilldown for Sourcing from fundraise-intel templates.

## Execution Notes

- Run `npm run build` after each session to verify no compile errors before committing.
- The app runs standalone with `npm run start` from `scripts/start-standalone.cjs`. Do not break this path.
- Do not modify `deal-rag`, `transcript-rag-api`, or `fundraise-intel` directly — only the adapters in `zo/app/lib/`.
- Each session must produce a Handoff Log entry listing exactly what changed and what the current build/runtime state is.
- `usage_checkpoint: HIGH` — this ticket will span multiple sessions. Write Handoff Log at the end of each session even if continuing.
- Do not start Session 2+ without confirming `npm run build` passes from Session 1.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Persons page hidden from main nav (not deleted)
- [ ] Home shows real threads and pinned companies, not seeded cues
- [ ] Assistant has working attachment selection
- [ ] Company workspace shows real deal + transcript evidence
- [ ] Search returns no seeded Persons results
- [ ] Sourcing shows event detail and evidence drilldown
- [ ] `npm run build` passes
- [ ] Reality Checker agent sign-off
- [ ] page-audit.md updated — no P0 surface marked `misleading` or `mock`

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-07 | claude-code | Ticket created. Current state: all P0 surfaces identified with truth status. Blocked on TKT-0005 (surface map from Figma V1). Next: activate after TKT-0005 is resolved and Enzo approves surface map. Start with Session 1 (hide Persons, fix company-data.ts, clean Home).

active.title: Deploy Twenty in compose sandbox
active.created: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.updated: Mon Apr 13 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.body:
## Context

PROGRAMME-001 plans to replace Attio with Twenty (self-hosted CRM) as part of the long-term Attio-free target state. Before any production migration, Twenty must be validated in a sandbox against MoveCapital's actual data model. This ticket deploys Twenty in a non-production compose profile on T418, loads a sample of companies, and validates that the data model and API work as expected. This is a sandbox only — no production changes. Blocked on TKT-0002 (Supabase state confirmed) and TKT-0004 (open questions answered, especially OQ-01 and OQ-02 which affect Twenty scope).

## Current State

- Twenty not yet deployed anywhere in the gordon-stack or local stack.
- Research dossier: `01_Repos/movecapital-platform-transition/docs/future-transition/twenty-evaluation.md` — full Twenty data model, API, self-hosting, and fit assessment.
- Twenty version: v1.20.0. Docker Compose self-hosting documented. REST + GraphQL API.
- gordon-stack: `01_Repos/gordon-stack/` — the orchestration repo for the live T418 stack. Any compose additions must be in a non-production profile and must not interfere with live services.
- T418 change control: any compose file modification requires runtime change control checklist (`00_Standards/runtime-change-control-checklist.md`).
- Data to load: sample of 50–100 companies from `project-ninpo.company_identity` (name, primary_domain, hq_country, hq_city).

## Task

1. Read `01_Repos/movecapital-platform-transition/docs/future-transition/twenty-evaluation.md` in full before starting.
2. Add a `twenty-sandbox` profile to gordon-stack compose (or create a separate `docker-compose.twenty-sandbox.yml`) that runs Twenty in isolation. Do not add it to the production `docker-compose.yml` or any active profile.
3. Start Twenty sandbox and verify it is accessible (default Twenty port is 3000 — check for port conflict with existing services on T418).
4. Use the Twenty REST or GraphQL API to load a sample of 50–100 companies. Source the company data from project-ninpo Supabase: `SELECT id, display_name, primary_domain, hq_country, hq_city FROM company_identity LIMIT 100`.
5. Validate:
   - Company records created with correct field mapping
   - Twenty REST API responds to GET /companies
   - A custom number field (placeholder for future `deeptech_score`) can be created via API
   - `PATCH /companies/:id` updates a field value correctly
6. Document findings: what worked, what didn't, what will need custom field setup for the production migration.

## Execution Notes

- T418 runtime change control: fill out `00_Standards/runtime-change-control-checklist.md` before modifying any compose file.
- Check port 3000 is not in use by another service before starting Twenty. Use `netstat -ano | findstr :3000` or equivalent.
- This is a sandbox only. Do not point any production adapter at Twenty. Do not modify project-ninpo's Attio connector.
- If Twenty's Postgres conflicts with Supabase (port 54322) or Keycloak (port 54325), Twenty must use a different internal port.
- Do not commit live `.env` secrets. Only commit the compose file structure.
- Document the exact Twenty API version and compose image tag used, for reproducibility.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Twenty sandbox running and accessible (non-production profile)
- [ ] No conflict with live services on T418
- [ ] 50–100 sample companies loaded via Twenty API
- [ ] GET /companies returns correct records
- [ ] Custom number field (score placeholder) created and patchable via API
- [ ] Findings documented in Handoff Log (data model fit, API behaviour, field gaps)
- [ ] SRE awareness of the new sandbox service

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-07 | claude-code | Ticket created. Current state: Twenty not yet deployed. Research dossier complete. Blocked on TKT-0002 (shadow import confirmation) and TKT-0004 (open questions, especially OQ-01 enrichment and OQ-02 list-scoped import). Next: after blockers resolved, read evaluation doc, add sandbox compose profile, validate API.

- 2026-04-13 | claude-code | **Session: compose scaffold created.** Phase 0 confirmed complete (OQ-01–06 answered); blocker TKT-0004 is DONE — ticket is now unblocked.

  **Change control scoped and executed:**
  - Target file: `01_Repos/gordon-stack/docker-compose.twenty-sandbox.yml` (new, additive — no touch to main `docker-compose.yml`)
  - Also created: `01_Repos/gordon-stack/.env.twenty-sandbox.example` (template for Enzo to copy to `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/`)
  - Port: `127.0.0.1:3002:3000` — host 3000 is occupied by Grafana; 3002 is free
  - Network: isolated `twenty_sandbox_net` bridge — no contact with production networks
  - Image: `twentycrm/twenty:latest` (server + worker), postgres:16-alpine, redis:7-alpine
  - Blast radius: zero on production (separate compose file, separate volumes, separate network)
  - Rollback: `docker compose -f docker-compose.twenty-sandbox.yml down -v` + delete files

  **State: `edited only`** — compose file written, NOT yet deployed. Deployment must be done by Enzo or in a follow-up session with Docker access.

  **Key finding from evaluation doc:** Twenty image tag in Docker Hub is `twentycrm/twenty:latest` (not tied to the v1.20.0 product version). Self-hosted instances have no rate limits — hardware-bound only. Port 3000 conflict with Grafana is documented and resolved.

  **What remains for Enzo to do before validation can continue:**
  1. Copy `.env.twenty-sandbox.example` → `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/.env.twenty-sandbox` (optional — defaults work for throwaway sandbox)
  2. Run: `cd 01_Repos/gordon-stack && docker compose -f docker-compose.twenty-sandbox.yml up -d`
  3. Wait ~90s for server startup (healthcheck start_period), then: `curl http://localhost:3002/healthz`
  4. Open `http://localhost:3002` in browser — complete the Twenty workspace setup wizard
  5. Create an API key in Settings → APIs & Webhooks
  6. Report back: API key created, workspace setup complete → unblocks the data load step

  **Remaining sub-tasks after Enzo confirms Twenty is running:**
  - Load 50–100 sample companies via Twenty REST API (source: project-ninpo Supabase `company_identity`)
  - Validate GET /companies, PATCH, custom field creation
  - Document findings in Handoff Log (data model fit, API quirks, pagination model)
  - Mark acceptance criteria checkboxes

  **Next session**: Resume here. Read this Handoff Log, confirm Twenty is up (`docker compose -f docker-compose.twenty-sandbox.yml ps`), then proceed with data load and API validation.

active.title: Deploy Vexa in sandbox and validate call platforms
active.created: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.updated: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.body:
## Context

PROGRAMME-001 plans to use Vexa for automated call capture (meeting bot + transcript) to replace the current ad-hoc recording + Attio company-lookup workflow in transcript-rag-api. Vexa is the higher-risk of the two CRM transition components (pre-1.0, no Zoom support yet). It must be validated before any production commitment. Critical unknown: which call platforms (Google Meet / Teams / Zoom) does the MoveCapital team actually use? If Zoom is primary and Vexa can't cover it, Vexa may not be viable. Blocked on TKT-0004 (OQ-03 — call platform question must be answered first).

## Current State

- Vexa not yet deployed anywhere.
- Research dossier: `01_Repos/movecapital-platform-transition/docs/future-transition/vexa-evaluation.md` — full Vexa platform, API, transcript model, and fit assessment.
- Vexa version: v0.6 / pre-1.0. GitHub: Vexa-ai/vexa. ~1,500 stars.
- Known limitations: Zoom support not production-ready. Google Meet and Teams: supported.
- Current call capture: n8n workflows in transcript-rag-api associate recordings with Attio company records. These are live and must not be disrupted.

## Task

1. Read `01_Repos/movecapital-platform-transition/docs/future-transition/vexa-evaluation.md` in full before starting.
2. Confirm answer to OQ-03 (from TKT-0004): which call platforms does MoveCapital actually use?
   - If Zoom only: Vexa is not viable. Document and escalate to Enzo. Do not proceed with deployment.
   - If Meet/Teams (with or without Zoom): proceed.
3. Deploy Vexa Lite in a non-production compose profile on T418 (separate from the live transcript-rag-api workflow).
4. Run a test meeting on the confirmed call platform. Inspect the webhook payload.
5. Validate:
   - Meeting bot joins successfully
   - Transcript output is structured JSON with speaker labels
   - Webhook payload is inspectable and matches expected format
   - Company association via post-processing is feasible (custom step, not built-in)
6. Document findings: call platform coverage confirmed, transcript quality, webhook shape, gaps vs. current workflow.

## Execution Notes

- Do NOT modify the live transcript-rag-api n8n workflows. This is a parallel sandbox test only.
- T418 runtime change control applies to any compose file modification.
- Vexa is pre-1.0 — treat this as an evaluation, not a production deployment. Document version and any breaking change risks.
- If OQ-03 reveals Zoom as primary platform: escalate immediately. Vexa ticket becomes BLOCKED pending an alternative evaluation.
- Do not expose Vexa to the public internet. Run in a sandboxed local network only.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] OQ-03 confirmed (call platforms known)
- [ ] If Meet/Teams viable: Vexa sandbox deployed and running
- [ ] Test meeting run and webhook payload inspected
- [ ] Transcript output confirmed (structured JSON with speaker labels)
- [ ] Company association feasibility documented
- [ ] Findings documented in Handoff Log (platform coverage, quality, webhook shape, go/no-go recommendation)
- [ ] If Zoom-only: escalation report to Enzo with alternative options

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-07 | claude-code | Ticket created. Current state: Vexa not deployed. Evaluation dossier complete. Critical blocker: OQ-03 (call platform question) must be answered before deployment. Next: after TKT-0004 resolves OQ-03, read evaluation doc, deploy sandbox, run test meeting.

active.title: Auth security audit — Keycloak, JWT, RLS, session handling
active.created: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.updated: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.body:
## Context

The platform must be safe to run with real investment data before it goes live. The current auth layer (Keycloak + next-auth + Supabase RLS) was stood up quickly during the local activation pass and has not had a security review. Before Phase 2 can gate as production-ready, the auth and data access model must be audited. This ticket covers the full auth security surface: JWT handling, session expiry, role propagation, Supabase RLS policies, API route guards, and secrets posture. This blocks the Phase 2 gate — the platform cannot go live without this passing.

## Current State

- Auth: Keycloak at `docker-compose.auth.yml`. Realm: dev import. next-auth wired via `/auth/signin`.
- Role mapping: `platform-admin` Keycloak realm role maps to `admin` in platform.
- DB: Supabase on port 54322. RLS enabled on all project-ninpo tables (migrations 0000–0012 all use `ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY`). Platform app-local tables also have RLS.
- API routes: `middleware.ts` handles route protection. Admin guard exists.
- Dev reset script: `npm run auth:reset-dev-user`.
- No security audit has been done.

## Task

Audit these surfaces in order:

1. **Keycloak configuration**: is the dev realm import safe for production use? Check token expiry, PKCE, redirect URIs, client secrets vs public client, and whether Keycloak admin console is exposed.

2. **JWT handling in next-auth**: check that JWTs are validated server-side on every protected API route, not just at the middleware layer. Confirm token expiry is enforced, not just checked at login.

3. **Role propagation**: confirm `platform-admin` → `admin` mapping is correctly enforced on all admin-only routes and server actions. Check for privilege escalation paths.

4. **Supabase RLS**: review RLS policies on all tables. Confirm `service_role` policies are only used server-side. Check that no client-side query can bypass RLS by using the service role key.

5. **API route guards**: audit `middleware.ts` and all API route files for missing auth checks. Look for routes that accept unauthenticated POST/PATCH/DELETE.

6. **Secrets posture**: confirm that no API keys, JWT secrets, or service role keys appear in client-side bundle. Check next.config.mjs for accidental `NEXT_PUBLIC_` exposure.

7. **Session handling**: confirm sessions expire correctly. Check that sign-out invalidates the server-side session, not just the client cookie.

Produce: a security findings report at `zo/app/docs/security/auth-audit-YYYY-MM-DD.md` with findings categorised as CRITICAL / HIGH / MEDIUM / LOW, plus a remediation plan.

## Execution Notes

- Do not modify auth configuration without understanding the current live session state. A broken auth config locks out all users.
- Do not print JWT secrets or Keycloak client secrets. Document that they exist and whether they are appropriately scoped.
- If CRITICAL findings are found: set ticket to `WAITING_INPUT`, surface to Enzo before proceeding with remediation.
- Run `npm run build` after any code changes to verify no compile errors.
- T418 runtime change control applies if touching Keycloak Docker config.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 7 surfaces audited
- [ ] Security findings report written with CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW categorisation
- [ ] No CRITICAL findings unresolved
- [ ] All HIGH findings have a remediation ticket created (spawn TKT-XXXX as needed)
- [ ] Supabase RLS confirmed to block client-side bypass
- [ ] No secrets exposed in client-side bundle
- [ ] Security Engineer sign-off

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-07 | claude-code | Ticket created. Current state: auth layer stood up but not audited. Keycloak dev realm import in place. Supabase RLS enabled on all tables. No security review done. Blocked on TKT-0006 (platform must be closer to complete before a meaningful auth audit). Next: activate after Phase 2 implementation is underway.

active.title: Platform security review — OWASP top 10, data handling, secrets posture
active.created: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.updated: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.body:
## Context

The platform handles confidential investment data: deal documents, call transcripts, company intelligence, scoring data, and team notes. Before going live, a full application security review is required beyond the auth audit (TKT-0010). This ticket covers OWASP Top 10 surface, data handling compliance (investment data sensitivity), and the broader secrets posture across all repos. It also involves the Legal Compliance Checker because of the financial data context (AMF/DORA adjacent). Blocked on TKT-0006 (implementation must be far enough along to be meaningful to audit) and TKT-0010 (auth audit must be done first).

## Current State

- No application-level security review has been done.
- Investment data categories in scope: deal PDFs, call transcripts (audio/text), company scoring data, analyst notes, pipeline stage information.
- Repos involved: movecapital-platform (frontend + API routes), deal-rag (deal document retrieval), transcript-rag-api (transcript retrieval).
- Secrets management: secrets live in `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/.env` (untracked). Service-to-service calls use env vars.

## Task

1. **OWASP Top 10 scan**: check for injection (SQL injection in Supabase queries, prompt injection in LLM routes), broken access control, security misconfiguration, XSS in rendered content, insecure direct object references in file/document endpoints.

2. **Data handling review**: confirm that deal documents and transcripts are never returned to unauthenticated requests. Confirm that LLM calls (Ollama) do not log or persist sensitive content. Confirm that search indices (Meilisearch) are not publicly accessible.

3. **Secrets posture**: audit all repos for accidentally committed secrets, API keys, or tokens (use `git log -S "sk-" --all` and similar). Confirm `.gitignore` patterns cover all secret file types.

4. **Inter-service trust**: confirm that calls from movecapital-platform to deal-rag, transcript-rag-api, and fundraise-intel are authenticated or network-isolated. Check if these sidecars accept unauthenticated requests from any source.

5. **LLM prompt injection**: review the assistant routes for prompt injection vectors — specifically whether user-supplied text can override system prompts or exfiltrate retrieved evidence.

6. **Legal Compliance Checker input**: confirm that the data handling model is consistent with French investment fund regulatory expectations (AMF) for internal tooling. This is not a full DORA compliance audit — just a sanity check.

Produce: security review report at `zo/app/docs/security/app-security-review-YYYY-MM-DD.md`.

## Execution Notes

- Do not print secret values. Document their presence and scope only.
- If CRITICAL findings are found: set ticket to WAITING_INPUT, surface to Enzo before continuing.
- Spawn new tickets for any HIGH+ finding that requires remediation work.
- Legal Compliance Checker should be briefed on the data categories, not given access to actual data.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] OWASP Top 10 surfaces reviewed
- [ ] No unauthenticated access to deal documents or transcripts confirmed
- [ ] No secrets committed to any tracked repo
- [ ] Inter-service calls are authenticated or network-isolated
- [ ] LLM prompt injection risk assessed and mitigated if found
- [ ] Legal Compliance Checker sanity check complete
- [ ] Security review report written with CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW findings
- [ ] All CRITICAL and HIGH findings have remediation tickets spawned
- [ ] Threat Detection Engineer sign-off

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-07 | claude-code | Ticket created. Current state: no security review done. Blocked on TKT-0006 (implementation) and TKT-0010 (auth audit). Next: activate once auth audit is complete and Shell V1 implementation is sufficiently advanced.

active.title: Test strategy and smoke test suite — Phase 2 acceptance
active.created: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.updated: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.body:
## Context

The platform has no automated tests and no formal acceptance test process. Before Phase 2 can gate as demo-ready, there must be a minimum test suite that proves the main surfaces work end-to-end with real sidecars, and a reality check that no surface is still misleading. This ticket creates the test strategy and executes the Phase 2 acceptance pass. It is the formal gate that closes Phase 2. Blocked on TKT-0006 (implementation must be done before testing it).

## Current State

- No existing test suite (no Jest, Playwright, or similar).
- `npm run build` is the only current automated check.
- Reality Checker agent exists in the org but has not been used yet.
- The `docs/demo-readiness.md` file in movecapital-platform has a page scoring table — this is the closest thing to an acceptance checklist currently.

## Task

**Part 1 — Test strategy** (first session):

Define the minimum viable test approach for the platform. Produce `zo/app/docs/testing/test-strategy.md` covering:
- Scope: what is tested (smoke, integration, API, E2E) and what is explicitly out of scope for now
- Tool choice: recommend one lightweight test runner (likely Playwright for E2E, given Next.js, or Vitest for unit)
- Coverage targets: minimum for Phase 2 gate (not 100% — just critical paths)
- CI gate: what command must pass before any PR is merged

**Part 2 — Smoke test suite** (second session):

Implement the Phase 2 minimum smoke suite:
- App starts and `/` returns 200
- `/auth/signin` renders without error
- Authenticated session can reach `/companies`, `/assistant`, `/search`, `/sourcing`, `/analysis`
- Assistant returns a response (even if sidecars are down — graceful fallback)
- Company workspace loads for a real company slug (not demo slug)
- Search returns results or an empty state (never a crash)

**Part 3 — Reality check pass** (third session, by Reality Checker):

Execute the Reality Checker agent against the current live app. Update `docs/demo-readiness.md` with current scores. Certify that:
- No P0 surface is marked `misleading` or `mock` in page-audit.md
- No seeded content is visible in any surface Enzo might show externally

## Execution Notes

- Run all tests against the local runtime at `http://127.0.0.1:3100`. Do not test against production.
- Smoke tests should be runnable with a single command: `npm run test:smoke` or similar. Document the command.
- Do not aim for full coverage — this is a Phase 2 gate, not a production test suite.
- Reality Checker must access the live running app, not a static build. Confirm the app is running before starting Part 3.
- If any test reveals a regression from TKT-0006 work: spawn a FIX ticket and link it here.

active.title: Analysis surface — evidence selection and output review
active.created: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.updated: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.body:
## Context

The Analysis surface in movecapital-platform is currently a control panel for the comps worker — it has a trigger button and run log but no evidence-selection model or output review. PROGRAMME-001 Phase 2 requires all P0 surfaces to be honest and demo-ready. The design direction (design-phasing.md) for Analysis is "design now, wire later" — the route exists and should improve now, but richer orchestration depth is a later phase. This ticket covers the Phase 2 posture: honest evidence selection from real data sources, real run state from the comps worker, and a simplified output review. The full Analysis orchestration depth is Phase 3+. This ticket is separate from TKT-0006 (which covers the other P0 surfaces) because Analysis requires a distinct evidence-selection interaction model not covered in TKT-0006's session plan.

## Current State

- Route: `/analysis` in movecapital-platform
- Current implementation: comps worker trigger (`POST /run`, `POST /run/dry`, `POST /run/diagnose`), live run log from `GET /runs/latest`. No evidence selection. No output review surface. Described as "mostly a control panel" in TKT-0006.
- Comps worker: `COMPS_WORKER_URL` (port 8084 → 8080 container, `comps-pipeline-worker`). API contract documented at `docs/service-contracts/comps-worker.md`.
- Design direction from `docs/stitch/design-rules.md` and `docs/stitch/keep-hide-demote.md`: "Analysis — simplify worker framing and reduce console feel." Keep rough vs polish: "Can stay more functional / rough."
- Source components to mine: `MoveCapital_UX/components/SwarmWorkspace.tsx` — has analysis packaging patterns.
- deal-rag search (`POST /search`) can be used to pull evidence by company or deal. transcript-rag-api can pull relevant calls.

## Task

1. **Evidence selection panel**: Add a simple pre-run evidence selection step. User can select which companies or deals to include in the analysis run. Pull available companies from Supabase (project-ninpo `company_identity` table via existing API routes). Keep the UI compact and functional — not a hero surface.

2. **Run state honesty**: Replace any seeded or hardcoded run state with live data from `GET /runs/latest`. Show real status, timestamps, duration, return code. If no prior run: show "No runs yet" rather than fake state.

3. **Output preview**: After a run completes, show a simple output preview — stdout tail from the run result. No need to build a full output browser. Just surface what the comps worker already returns in `stdout_tail`.

4. **Remove console theater**: Remove any fake model/provider control panels, fake verbosity sliders, or fake configuration surfaces that are not wired to real behaviour. Follow the "Remove If Fake" list in `docs/stitch/keep-hide-demote.md`.

5. **Verify comps worker integration**: Confirm the trigger buttons (`/run`, `/run/dry`, `/run/diagnose`) call the real comps worker via `adjacent-services.ts`. Check for silent failures — surface errors to the user rather than swallowing them.

## Execution Notes

- Run `npm run build` after changes. Port 3100 dev server must be stopped first (build-safe.cjs guard).
- Do not modify the comps worker (`MoveCapital_CompsPipeline`) — adapter only in `zo/app/lib/`.
- The Analysis surface is allowed to remain functional/rough per design direction. Do not over-polish.
- Evidence selection only needs company-level granularity for Phase 2. Deal-level selection is Phase 3.
- `usage_checkpoint: MEDIUM` — this is a single session ticket but requires reading the current analysis page code first.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Evidence selection panel present — user can select companies before triggering a run
- [ ] Run state pulled from live `GET /runs/latest`, not seeded
- [ ] Output preview shows `stdout_tail` from the most recent run
- [ ] No fake control panels, fake sliders, or fake configuration UI
- [ ] Comps worker trigger confirmed to call real endpoint (not mocked)
- [ ] `npm run build` passes after changes
- [ ] page-audit.md updated — Analysis surface no longer marked misleading

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-07 | claude-code | Ticket created. Current state: Analysis is a control panel with real worker trigger but no evidence selection or output review. Blocked on TKT-0005 (Figma V1) and TKT-0006 (P0 surface pass must complete first). Source patterns in MoveCapital_UX/components/SwarmWorkspace.tsx. Next: activate after TKT-0006 is complete and Figma direction confirmed.

## Context

PROGRAMME-001 requires a Code Reviewer sign-off and Reality Checker certification before Phase 2 can gate. This ticket is the formal review gate that closes Phase 2 once all implementation, security, and test tickets are complete. It exists as a separate ticket so the gate is explicit and traceable. The Phase 2 gate condition (from PROGRAMME-001): demo session with no misleading surfaces, Reality Checker sign-off, no surfaces marked misleading or mock in page-audit.md.

## Current State

All Phase 2 implementation tickets must be DONE before this activates:
- TKT-0006: Replace P0 misleading surfaces (OPEN, blocked on TKT-0005)
- TKT-0016: Analysis surface — evidence selection and output review (OPEN, blocked on TKT-0006)
- TKT-0010: Auth security audit (OPEN, blocked on TKT-0006)
- TKT-0011: Platform security review (OPEN, blocked on TKT-0006)
- TKT-0012: Test strategy and smoke test suite (OPEN, blocked on TKT-0006)

## Task

1. **Code review pass**: Read all code changes from TKT-0006 and TKT-0016. Verify: no regressions in existing functionality, no new silent fallbacks, no hardcoded/seeded data introduced, TypeScript clean, `npm run build` passes.

2. **Reality Checker pass**: Run the Reality Checker agent against the live app at `http://127.0.0.1:3100`. Check every P0 surface:
   - Home: real threads and pinned companies visible
   - Assistant: real multi-turn, real attachment selection
   - Search: no seeded Persons results, real multi-backend results
   - Companies: real company list from Supabase
   - Company workspace: real deal + transcript evidence (not demo slug)
   - Sourcing: real events from fundraise-intel with evidence drilldown
   - Analysis: honest run state, no fake controls

3. **page-audit.md certification**: Update `docs/page-audit.md` with current scores for every surface. No P0 surface may be marked `misleading` or `mock`.

4. **Gate sign-off**: Produce a written certification: "Phase 2 implementation review complete — [date]. All P0 surfaces verified honest. No CRITICAL or HIGH security findings unresolved. Smoke tests passing. Phase 2 gate approved."

5. **Update PROGRAMME-001**: Mark Phase 2 gate as CLOSED.

## Execution Notes

- Do not modify implementation code during this ticket. Only read, verify, and document.
- If you find a regression or misleading surface: spawn a FIX ticket (TKT-XXXX), mark this ticket BLOCKED, and link the fix ticket as blocked_by.
- This ticket can only be started when all blocked_by tickets are DONE.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All Phase 2 implementation tickets confirmed DONE
- [ ] Code review pass complete — no regressions, no silent fallbacks
- [ ] `npm run build` passes
- [ ] page-audit.md updated — no P0 surface marked misleading or mock
- [ ] Reality Checker certification document written
- [ ] Code Reviewer sign-off recorded in Handoff Log
- [ ] PROGRAMME-001 Phase 2 gate marked CLOSED

## Handoff Log

Activates when TKT-0006, TKT-0016, TKT-0010, TKT-0011, TKT-0012 are all DONE. Next: wait for blockers to clear, then execute all 5 review steps in a single session.

active.title: Security audit — Phase 3 data and CRM surfaces
active.created: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.updated: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.body:
## Context

Phase 3 introduces real CRM data (Attio shadow import) and a new CRM backend (Twenty sandbox). Before any of this data is surfaced in the platform or used for dual-import, a security audit of the data access layer is required. This covers: RLS policy correctness for shadow tables, Twenty API auth model, and any n8n workflow that will touch shadow or CRM data. This is separate from TKT-0010/TKT-0011 (which cover the platform shell auth layer). This ticket covers the data and CRM-specific security surface introduced in Phase 3.

## Current State

- Attio shadow tables: RLS enabled (migration 0012 uses `ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY`). Specific policies not yet reviewed for correctness.
- Twenty sandbox: not yet deployed (TKT-0008). Auth model unknown until deployed.
- n8n workflows: currently write to Attio. In Phase 4 they will dual-write to Twenty. Workflow auth (API keys) needs to be reviewed before CRM transition.
- fundraise-intel and comps worker: emit data to Supabase. Verify they use service role correctly and no data leaks to client.

## Task

1. **Shadow table RLS audit**: Review all RLS policies on `attio_*_shadow` tables. Verify that:
   - No shadow table is readable by unauthenticated requests
   - Service role access is only from server-side code
   - No client-side Supabase query can read raw Attio person/deal data

2. **Twenty auth model review** (after TKT-0008): Document Twenty's API auth. Verify API keys are stored in `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/.env` (not in any repo). Confirm the twenty connector in project-ninpo uses the service role, not a client-facing key.

3. **n8n workflow credential audit**: Review the n8n workflows that write to Attio (and eventually Twenty). Confirm API keys are stored as n8n credentials (not hardcoded in workflow JSON). Flag any workflow that stores tokens in plaintext.

4. **Data exposure check**: Grep `movecapital-platform` for any NEXT_PUBLIC_ variable that references shadow table data, Attio API keys, or CRM credentials. None should be present.

5. **Produce findings report**: Write `zo/app/docs/security/phase3-data-audit-YYYY-MM-DD.md` with findings categorised as CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW.

## Execution Notes

- Do not print API keys, database passwords, or Attio credentials. Confirm existence and location only.
- Do not modify Twenty or n8n configs without change control.
- If CRITICAL findings: set ticket to WAITING_INPUT and surface to Enzo before proceeding.
- TKT-0008 must be DONE before the Twenty auth model review can be completed. Run other steps first.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Shadow table RLS policies reviewed and confirmed correct
- [ ] Twenty API auth model documented (after TKT-0008)
- [ ] n8n workflow credentials confirmed stored as n8n credentials, not hardcoded
- [ ] No CRM credentials or shadow data exposed via NEXT_PUBLIC_ vars
- [ ] Phase 3 security findings report written
- [ ] No CRITICAL findings unresolved

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-07 | claude-code | Ticket created. Blocked on TKT-0018 (shadow import must exist before auditing its RLS) and TKT-0008 (Twenty must be deployed before reviewing its auth). Other steps (n8n credential audit, NEXT_PUBLIC_ check) can proceed earlier. Next: activate after TKT-0018 DONE, start with shadow table RLS audit and data exposure check while waiting for TKT-0008.
- 2026-04-10 | Security Engineer + Threat Detection Engineer | Partial audit executed (Steps 1, 3, 4 of task — Twenty auth review skipped, TKT-0008 not deployed). Reviewed migrations 0002 + 0012 (RLS on all shadow tables: PASS). Reviewed 10 n8n workflows in 02_Runtime bind-mounts (all credentials stored as named n8n references: PASS). Scanned movecapital-platform src/ and .env.example for NEXT_PUBLIC_ vars (none found: PASS). One LOW observation: hardcoded Qdrant URLs in transcript_rag_step3_drive_to_qdrant.json — maintenance issue only, not a security concern. | Findings report written: zo/app/docs/security/phase3-data-audit-2026-04-10.md. Status updated to IN_REVIEW. | Next: re-open for Twenty auth review when TKT-0008 DONE; update acceptance criteria to mark Twenty review as pending. | No CRITICAL or HIGH findings — phase can proceed from a shadow/n8n/client-exposure perspective.

active.title: Build deal-analysis-api — FastAPI wrapper service for Deal-analysis-swarm
active.created: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.updated: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.body:
## Context

TKT-0024 (Deal-analysis-swarm design thinking) completed and identified that the swarm is a Codex-native skill package — it has no HTTP interface, no LLM provider abstraction, and its extraction scripts are PowerShell-only. None of the platform integration described in TKT-0016 (Analysis surface) can be built until a `deal-analysis-api` wrapper service exists. This ticket covers building that wrapper. PROG-001, WS-INTELLIGENCE, Phase 2.

## Resolved Decisions (answered by Enzo 2026-04-07)

| # | Question | Answer |
|---|---|---|
| OQ-A | Repo or sub-directory? | **Sub-directory**: `zo/services/deal-analysis-swarm/api/` |
| OQ-B | Deploy target? | **T418 sidecar** — add to gordon-stack compose |
| OQ-C | Model strategy? | **Haiku as default** for everyday cost efficiency. Per-analysis-type DB config allows Sonnet/Opus overrides for high-stakes runs. 04_Agents/ agent model settings are separate and not managed by this service. `ANALYSIS_DEFAULT_MODEL` env var = `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`. |
| OQ-D | ZO polling model? | **Platform polling** — `GET /runs/{id}/status`. Webhooks deferred to post-TKT-0025 once ZO architecture is defined. |
| OQ-E | PS→Python port? | **Separate TKT-0032** (blocks prompt execution step in this ticket). |

**Note on OQ-C**: The architecture spec in `analysis-swarm-architecture.md` (TKT-0024 output) specifies `claude-sonnet-4-6` as default — this is superseded by the above. Implementing agent must use Haiku as default and treat Sonnet/Opus as per-type opt-in via `analysis_type_config`. The doc should be amended before TKT-0016 starts.

## Current State

- `Deal-analysis-swarm` repo: Codex skill package, no HTTP server, prompts in `/skills/`, extraction scripts in PowerShell.
- Architecture design spec: `zo/app/docs/design/analysis-swarm-architecture.md` (TKT-0024 output) — read this before starting, but apply OQ-C model override above.
- No `deal-analysis-api` service or `zo/services/deal-analysis-swarm/api/` directory exists yet.
- `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` presence in `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/.env` not yet confirmed — verify before deploy.
- TKT-0032 (PS→Python port) is a hard prerequisite for the prompt execution step.

## Task

Implement the `deal-analysis-api` FastAPI service at `zo/services/deal-analysis-swarm/api/`:

1. **Service scaffold**: FastAPI app, Dockerfile (Linux container), `.env.example`. Endpoints:
   - `POST /api/analysis/run` — accepts `AnalysisRunRequest`, queues run, returns `run_id`
   - `GET /api/analysis/runs/{id}` — run status + output when complete
   - `GET /api/analysis/runs` — run history (paginated, filterable by company_id)
   - `GET /api/analysis/templates` — available analysis types + prompt metadata
2. **LLM routing layer**: four-layer routing (user override → per-type DB config → `ANALYSIS_DEFAULT_MODEL` env var → provider fallback chain). **Default: `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001` via Anthropic**, not Sonnet.
3. **Prompt execution**: invoke ported Python scripts from TKT-0032 — do not start this step until TKT-0032 is DONE.
4. **Config table**: `analysis_type_config` DB migration (platform Supabase) — `analysis_type`, `default_model`, `default_provider`, `max_tokens`. Pre-populate with Haiku defaults; mark Sonnet-override rows for analysis types that warrant it (e.g. full deal memo).

**Additional migration requirement (TKT-0040):** The initial DB migration must also create an `agent_config` table with at minimum: `id uuid primary key`, `agent_type text not null`, `default_model text`, `model_override_allowed boolean default true`, `skill_packs jsonb default '[]'`. The `skill_packs` column must be present from day one (empty array default) per TKT-0029 design note — do not add it in a later migration. See `zo/app/docs/roadmap/agent-skill-packs.md` Section 3.1 for the full TypeScript interface and usage pattern. The `agent_config` table follows the same storage pattern as `analysis_type_config` — DB-seeded via migration, allowing config changes without code deploys.
5. **Compose wiring**: add service to gordon-stack with change control. Assign port, health check, env injection.

## Execution Notes

- Read `zo/app/docs/design/analysis-swarm-architecture.md` before starting — but apply the OQ-C model override: Haiku default, not Sonnet.
- Do not hardcode model IDs — `ANALYSIS_DEFAULT_MODEL` env var, per-type overrides in DB.
- Docker container is Linux regardless of T418 host OS — Python scripts required (TKT-0032).
- Do not touch live gordon-stack services without the runtime change control checklist.
- Confirm `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` exists in `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/` before wiring Anthropic calls.
- Do not start prompt execution step until TKT-0032 is DONE.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] Open questions OQ-A through OQ-E answered by Enzo
- [ ] `zo/services/deal-analysis-swarm/api/` scaffold created (FastAPI, Dockerfile, .env.example)
- [ ] All four API endpoints implemented and responding
- [ ] LLM routing layer implemented — Haiku default, per-type DB overrides
- [ ] `analysis_type_config` migration created with correct defaults
- [ ] `curl POST /api/analysis/run` with a real company_id returns a run_id
- [ ] `curl GET /api/analysis/runs/{id}` returns output for a completed run
- [ ] Compose wiring confirmed — service accessible from platform adapter
- [ ] `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` confirmed present in runtime env

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-07 | COO | Ticket created — DISCOVERED during TKT-0024 design thinking. Deal-analysis-swarm has no HTTP interface; platform cannot integrate the swarm without this wrapper service. → spawned from TKT-0024. Status set to WAITING_INPUT pending Enzo answers on OQ-A through OQ-E. | No implementation started. Architecture spec at zo/app/docs/design/analysis-swarm-architecture.md. | Enzo must answer the 5 open questions before execution begins. | —
- 2026-04-07 | COO | All 5 OQs answered by Enzo. Status → OPEN. Key override on OQ-C: default model is Haiku not Sonnet — architecture doc (TKT-0024 output) is superseded on this point. blocked_by updated to include TKT-0032 (PS→Python port). New programme OQ added: T418 platform VM OS (Windows vs Linux) — Docker containers are Linux either way so not a deploy blocker, but affects dev experience for TKT-0032. | OQ answers recorded above. | TKT-0032 must complete before prompt execution step. Amend analysis-swarm-architecture.md model section before TKT-0016 begins. | Do not hardcode Sonnet — Haiku is the default.
- 2026-04-08 | AI-Engineer | Task spec amended by TKT-0040: `agent_config` table with `skill_packs jsonb default '[]'` column added as a required output of step 4's DB migration. TKT-0029 design doc (`agent-skill-packs.md`) explicitly requires this column from day one to avoid a breaking schema migration later. Column will be empty array initially; populated when Skill Pack agents are implemented post-Phase 6. | Task spec updated above. | Implementing agent must create both `analysis_type_config` AND `agent_config` tables in the same migration pass. | Do not omit `skill_packs` column even though it starts empty.

active.title: Deploy fundraise-intel to T418 as a live gordon-stack sidecar

active.body:
## Context

`fundraise-intel` is an ACTIVE-CORE repo (per PROGRAMME-001 repo inventory) but it is not deployed on T418 — it is a local MVP under active development with no entry in `gordon-stack` compose. This is a gap because: (1) the ZO assistant architecture (TKT-0025 output, zo-assistant-architecture.md Section 6.1) explicitly flags "fundraise-intel — LOCAL MVP — NOT DEPLOYED — ZO cannot call this until deployed"; (2) TKT-0006 Session 4 (Sourcing surface drilldown) requires a live fundraise-intel endpoint; (3) the company-to-fundraise-intel identity join (TKT-0043) cannot be implemented until the service is reachable. PROGRAMME-001 WS-SERVICES goal requires all platform sidecars to have stable, clean API contracts — not achievable while the service is running only locally. Phase P3, WS-SERVICES.

## Current State

- `01_Repos/zo/services/fundraise-intel/` — local MVP codebase, not deployed.

- `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_URL` expected unset in `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/` — confirmed in service-contracts doc.

- Port assignment: check existing port map in gordon-stack before choosing — do not collide with deal-rag (6333/6334 Qdrant, 8000 deal-rag API), transcript-rag-api (8001), comps-pipeline-worker (8002 or per its config).

## Task

1. Read `01_Repos/zo/services/fundraise-intel/` in full — understand its startup requirements, env vars, and any external dependencies.
2. Read `gordon-stack` compose file — identify current port assignments and service naming convention.

7. Smoke test: `curl http://localhost:<port>/health` or equivalent healthcheck endpoint — attach output to Handoff Log.

## Execution Notes

- Do not change fundraise-intel's code in this ticket — this is a deployment ticket only. Code changes belong in a separate ticket.
- Do not hardcode ports. Use the env var `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_PORT` or match the gordon-stack convention.
- Gordon-stack is local-only on T418. No push to GitHub.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] fundraise-intel service entry in gordon-stack compose
- [ ] Service starts cleanly: `docker compose ps` shows it running
- [ ] Healthcheck passes: curl output in Handoff Log
- [ ] `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_URL` wired in `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/.env`
- [ ] Platform service status adapter recognises the service
- [ ] Any Qdrant dependency noted in Handoff Log for TKT-0037 tracking
- [ ] No other services disrupted (smoke test all existing sidecars)

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-08 | COO | Ticket created. Grounded in zo-assistant-architecture.md Section 6.1 (ZO cannot call fundraise-intel until deployed) and PROGRAMME-001 WS-SERVICES scope. No implementation started. | Service not deployed. | Begin by reading fundraise-intel repo structure and gordon-stack compose. | T418 change control mandatory before any gordon-stack edit.

- 2026-04-10 | Engineering Core (Backend Architect + DevOps Automator) | Change-control prep phase. Full read of fundraise-intel repo, gordon-stack compose, service-port-registry.md, and service contract. No files written to compose yet. All output below is preparatory — DO NOT deploy until Enzo answers open questions. | Service not deployed — no compose entry yet. | See pre-deployment questions section below before proceeding. | **BLOCKER: No Dockerfile exists in fundraise-intel repo — cannot build a container image until one is authored. This is the primary gate for deployment.**

---

### Change Control Prep Output — 2026-04-10

#### 1. Port Analysis

**Assigned port: 8010 (internal to Docker network, no host port binding)**

Rationale:
- Port registry (`gordon-stack/docs/service-port-registry.md`) already reserved port 8010 for fundraise-intel (entry marked PLANNED, logged in TKT-0069 changelog 2026-04-09).
- fundraise-intel falls in the "Deal pipeline services" band (8010–8019 per the allocation scheme).
- fundraise-intel's standalone CLI default is port 8000 — that conflicts with `rag-api` (transcript-rag-api) which is also 8000 internal. Registering at 8010 avoids ambiguity when both run in gordon-stack.
- No host port binding needed at this stage — internal Docker DNS (`fundraise-intel:8010`) is sufficient for n8n and movecapital-platform to reach it via `app_internal` network. If direct browser access is needed, a host bind of `127.0.0.1:8010:8010` can be added later.
- Existing host port map (confirmed from docker-compose.yml): 80, 443, 11434, 6333, 8084 (comps-pipeline-worker), 3001 (uptime-kuma), 8085 (open-webui), 8086 (infisical), 3100 (loki), 3000 (grafana), 9090 (prometheus), 8083 (cadvisor), 9100 (node-exporter). **8010 is clean — no conflict.**

**Health endpoint confirmed:** `GET /api/health` → returns `{"status": "ok"}` (found in `src/fundraise_intel/app.py` line 187–189). Note: this is `/api/health`, not `/health` — differs from other gordon-stack sidecars. Service contract (fundraise-intel.md) explicitly documents this difference.

---

#### 2. Env Vars Required

All env vars sourced from `src/fundraise_intel/settings.py` and `.env.example`.

| Env Var | Required? | Source | Known in gordon-prod/.env? | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_APP_NAME` | No | `.env.example` | No | Defaults to `fundraise-intel` — safe to omit or set explicitly |
| `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_ENV` | No | `.env.example` | No | Defaults to `development`; should be set to `production` for T418 deploy |
| `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_STATE_DIR` | **YES** | `settings.py` | No | **NEW — needs Enzo input.** Must be a path inside the container, backed by a Docker volume or bind-mount. Suggested: `/data/state`. Without this, defaults to `$LOCALAPPDATA/fundraise-intel` which resolves to nothing meaningful in a Linux container. |
| `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_DATABASE_URL` | No | `settings.py` | No | Defaults to SQLite at `$FUNDRAISE_INTEL_STATE_DIR/db/fundraise_intel.sqlite3`. If state dir is a mounted volume, SQLite is sufficient for MVP. Postgres upgrade is a future concern. |
| `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_SOURCE_CONFIG` | **YES** | `settings.py` | No | **NEW — needs Enzo input.** Path to the `sources.yml` config file inside the container. Without this it falls back to `config/sources.example.yml` in the repo root, which may work if the repo is COPY'd into the image, but should be wired explicitly. Suggested: `/config/sources.yml` backed by a bind-mount from `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/bind-mounts/fundraise-intel/`. |
| `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_IMAP_PASSWORD` | No | `settings.py` | No | Only needed if IMAP is enabled in sources config. IMAP is disabled in the example config — omit until needed. |
| `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_OLLAMA_URL` | No | `settings.py` | Partially — `OLLAMA_URL` exists in other services | **NEW name for fundraise-intel.** If Ollama integration is enabled, set to `http://ollama:11434`. Not required for baseline deploy — fundraise-intel uses Ollama optionally (no hard dependency found in startup path). |
| `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_OLLAMA_MODEL` | No | `settings.py` | No | Optional. Only used if Ollama is wired in. |
| `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_TIMEZONE` | No | `settings.py` | No | Defaults to `Europe/Paris`. Match Enzo's timezone preference or omit. |
| `LIGHTPANDA_CDP_URL` | No | `.env.example` | No | Only required for watchlist sources with `render: true`. Not in the example sources config by default. Omit for initial deploy. |
| `RSSHUB_BASE_URL` | No | `.env.example` | No | Only required if sources.yml references `${RSSHUB_BASE_URL}` feeds. The example sources.yml includes many RSSHub feeds. **NEW — needs Enzo input**: does Enzo want RSSHub deployed alongside this? If yes, raises TKT for rsshub sidecar. For initial deploy, if sources config is simplified to native RSS feeds only, this is not required. |

**Env vars that must also be added to `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/.env`:**
- `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_URL=http://fundraise-intel:8010` — this is the variable that movecapital-platform's adapter reads (per service contract). Must be added after the service is running.

**Summary: 2 vars definitely needed before deploy (STATE_DIR, SOURCE_CONFIG), 1 optional-but-important (RSSHUB_BASE_URL decision).**

---

#### 3. Qdrant Dependency — NO

fundraise-intel does **not** use Qdrant. Confirmed by:
- `settings.py`: no Qdrant URL or collection vars.
- `.env.example`: no Qdrant reference.
- `db.py`: uses SQLAlchemy — defaults to SQLite, can be configured for Postgres.
- `pyproject.toml` dependencies: no qdrant-client.
- No `qdrant` import anywhere in `src/`.

**TKT-0037 flag: NOT required.** fundraise-intel is not affected by the Qdrant → pgvector migration.

---

#### 4. CRITICAL BLOCKER — No Dockerfile

`01_Repos/zo/services/fundraise-intel/` has **no Dockerfile**. The repo is a Python package (setuptools, pyproject.toml) designed to be installed in a venv and run via the `fundraise-intel` CLI. There is no Docker image build configuration.

Other gordon-stack services with custom images (`rag-api`, `deal-rag-api`, `metadata-generator`, `deal-ingest-worker`, `comps-pipeline-worker`) all have Dockerfiles in their respective repos. fundraise-intel is missing one.

**A Dockerfile must be authored before any compose entry can be written and deployed.** Suggested minimum Dockerfile (for Enzo/Engineering to validate before authoring):
- Base: `python:3.13-slim`
- Install: `pip install -e .` from the repo root
- Entrypoint: run DB migrations then start uvicorn via `fundraise-intel runserver --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8010`
- Note: `pywin32` dep has `sys_platform == 'win32'` guard — will be skipped on Linux container, which is correct.

This Dockerfile authoring should be a sub-task of this ticket or a child ticket. **No deployment can proceed without it.**

---

#### 5. Proposed Compose Service Entry

This is **draft only** — do not apply to `docker-compose.yml` until Enzo confirms open questions and Dockerfile is authored.

```yaml
  # ── fundraise-intel — EU company intelligence pipeline (TKT-0035) ──────────
  # FastAPI/uvicorn service tracking companies raising capital from public sources.
  # Health: GET /api/health (note: /api/health not /health — see service contract)
  # Port: 8010 (internal docker network only — no host port bind at MVP stage)
  # State: persisted to fundraise-intel-state volume (SQLite DB + raw + exports + logs)
  # Config: sources.yml bind-mounted from 02_Runtime/gordon-prod/bind-mounts/fundraise-intel/
  fundraise-intel:
    build: ../fundraise-intel
    container_name: fundraise-intel
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      FUNDRAISE_INTEL_ENV: "production"
      FUNDRAISE_INTEL_STATE_DIR: "/data/state"
      FUNDRAISE_INTEL_SOURCE_CONFIG: "/config/sources.yml"
      FUNDRAISE_INTEL_TIMEZONE: "Europe/Paris"
      FUNDRAISE_INTEL_OLLAMA_URL: "http://ollama:11434"
      FUNDRAISE_INTEL_OLLAMA_MODEL: "${FUNDRAISE_INTEL_OLLAMA_MODEL}"
      RSSHUB_BASE_URL: "${RSSHUB_BASE_URL}"
    volumes:
      - fundraise-intel-state:/data/state
      - ../../02_Runtime/gordon-prod/bind-mounts/fundraise-intel/sources.yml:/config/sources.yml:ro
    networks:
      - app_internal
    logging:
      driver: json-file
      options:
        max-size: 10m
        max-file: "5"
    # Resource profile: Core app (API + scheduler — not inference-heavy)
    mem_limit: 512m
    memswap_limit: 512m
    cpu_shares: 256
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 512m
          pids: 200
        reservations:
          memory: 64m
    # Security hardening (TKT-0100 pattern)
    cap_drop:
      - ALL
    security_opt:
      - no-new-privileges:true
    # Health check — note /api/health path (differs from other sidecars)
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:8010/api/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 60s
```

**Volume entry to add to the `volumes:` block:**
```yaml
  fundraise-intel-state:
```

**Network rationale:** `app_internal` only — no Qdrant (data_internal not needed), no postgres/redis dependency. If Ollama is used, Ollama is already on `app_internal`. If RSSHub sidecar is added later, it would also be on `app_internal`.

**Notes on draft:**
- `start_period: 60s` — longer than other services because fundraise-intel runs `alembic upgrade head` on startup (DB migration), which adds latency.
- Ollama env vars included but will be no-ops if Ollama integration is not configured in sources.yml.
- `RSSHUB_BASE_URL` passed from `.env` — if Enzo decides not to deploy RSSHub, set to empty string or remove.

---

#### 6. Pre-Deployment Questions for Enzo

1. **Dockerfile**: A Dockerfile does not exist. Should Engineering author one in this ticket, or should it be a child ticket? Suggested approach: author in fundraise-intel repo, commit and push before proceeding with compose work.

2. **State directory and volume**: The proposed entry uses a named Docker volume `fundraise-intel-state`. Is this acceptable, or should state be bind-mounted to a specific path under `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/`? Using a named volume is simpler but less transparent for backup.

3. **Sources config**: The `sources.example.yml` has RSSHub-generated feeds referencing `${RSSHUB_BASE_URL}`. Should the live sources.yml be simplified to native RSS feeds only (removing RSSHub dependencies), or should a RSSHub sidecar be deployed? If RSSHub: raise a new TKT for rsshub sidecar — it is an additional container.

4. **RSSHub sidecar decision**: If RSSHub is needed, it should be a separate sidecar added to gordon-stack in a follow-on ticket. The runbook shows the compose snippet needed. This is out of scope for TKT-0035 but should be tracked.

5. **Ollama integration**: Should fundraise-intel use Ollama for any processing? The settings accept `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_OLLAMA_URL` and `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_OLLAMA_MODEL` but there is no hard dependency found in startup code. Is this wiring active or dead config?

6. **Database**: SQLite (the default) is sufficient for MVP. Confirm Enzo is comfortable with SQLite in a Docker volume for this service. Postgres upgrade path exists (set `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_DATABASE_URL` to a postgres DSN) but is not needed for initial deploy.

7. **Scheduler**: The CLI has both `runserver` (FastAPI/uvicorn) and `run-scheduler` (APScheduler blocking). Should the container run both, or just the API server? If scheduler is needed, the entrypoint must run both processes (e.g., supervisor or two containers). For MVP, recommend API-only first, scheduler as a follow-on.

8. **Host port binding**: Internal-only (no host port) is proposed for MVP. Do you need direct browser access to the fundraise-intel UI (dashboard at `/`, companies at `/companies`, etc.) from the Windows host? If yes, add `127.0.0.1:8010:8010` to ports.

---

#### 7. What Remains Before Actual Deployment

- [x] **DONE (TKT-0108, 2026-04-10)**: Author `Dockerfile` in `01_Repos/zo/services/fundraise-intel/` — committed and pushed (168a70f). Build and smoke test PASSED.
- [ ] Enzo answers questions 1–8 above.
- [ ] Create `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/bind-mounts/fundraise-intel/sources.yml` (live sources config, not committed to git).
- [ ] Add `fundraise-intel-state` volume to gordon-stack `docker-compose.yml` volumes block.
- [ ] Add compose service entry (draft above) to `gordon-stack/docker-compose.yml` after Enzo sign-off.
- [ ] Add `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_URL=http://fundraise-intel:8010` to `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/.env`.
- [ ] Add `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_OLLAMA_MODEL` and `RSSHUB_BASE_URL` to `.env` if used.
- [ ] Update `gordon-stack/docs/service-port-registry.md` — change fundraise-intel status from PLANNED to LIVE/DEPLOYED.
- [ ] Run `docker compose up -d fundraise-intel` (with change control checklist completed).
- [ ] Smoke test: `curl http://localhost:8010/api/health` from host (if host port bound) or `docker exec fundraise-intel curl -f http://localhost:8010/api/health`.
- [ ] Verify `FUNDRAISE_INTEL_URL` is picked up by movecapital-platform service status adapter.
- [ ] Add Uptime Kuma monitor for fundraise-intel (per port registry convention).
- [ ] Confirm no other services disrupted after compose up.

active.title: Design thinking — n8n workflow re-wiring plan for Twenty + Vexa dual-import
active.created: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.updated: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.body:
## Context

PROGRAMME-001 Phase 4 lists "n8n workflows re-wired in staging to point at Twenty + Vexa" as a required work item. The WS-CRM workstream lists "n8n workflows re-wireable" as a dependency for the dual-import phase. Currently n8n workflows in gordon-stack/n8n-git-sync point at Attio. When Twenty and Vexa are validated in sandbox (TKT-0008, TKT-0009), the re-wiring must be designed before Phase 4 begins. Discovering the complexity at Phase 4 start is a known risk — this design ticket front-loads that work so Phase 4 has a ready-to-execute re-wiring playbook. No ticket existed for this. PROG-001, WS-CRM, Phase P3.

## Current State

- n8n runs in gordon-stack; workflows are git-synced via `n8n-git-sync`.
- Current workflows integrate with Attio (exact workflows unknown — must audit).
- TKT-0008 (Twenty sandbox deploy) and TKT-0009 (Vexa sandbox deploy): both OPEN, not yet started.
- Dual-import means Attio connector stays live while Twenty connector runs in parallel — n8n must feed both during Phase 4 parity window.
- OQ-02 (answered 2026-04-07): list-scoped import is required for the final Attio handoff sync. Twenty connector must support list-scoped filtering.

## Task

After TKT-0008 and TKT-0009 complete, produce a re-wiring plan document at `zo/app/docs/migration/n8n-rewiring-plan.md` covering:

1. **Workflow audit**: List every n8n workflow that touches Attio. For each: what it does, what Attio API it calls, what data it moves.

2. **Vexa integration points**: Which workflows currently handle call/meeting data? How will these be re-pointed to Vexa's API?

3. **Twenty integration points**: Which workflows import company/person/deal data from or to Attio? How will these run in parallel to Twenty during dual-import?

4. **Dual-import configuration**: Design the staging setup — both Attio and Twenty connectors live simultaneously. How does n8n decide which system of record to write to? Is it parallel writes or conditional routing?

5. **Credential management**: Attio tokens, Twenty API keys, Vexa API keys — where do they live in the n8n credential store? Are they in `.env` or in n8n's internal credential storage?

6. **Staging vs production path**: How does the re-wiring get validated in staging before being applied to the live n8n instance?

7. **Rollback plan**: If the re-wiring introduces a regression, what is the rollback path? (n8n-git-sync should make this a git revert.)

## Execution Notes

- Design only — no workflow edits until TKT-0008 and TKT-0009 are complete and sandbox validated.
- The n8n-git-sync repo is an automation exception — it is GitHub-backed and T418 can push. But do not push workflow changes during this design phase.
- Do not stop or restart the live n8n instance while reading/auditing workflows.
- TKT-0022 (Phase 3 security audit) covers n8n workflow credential audit — this ticket covers re-wiring design only, not the security audit.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All Attio-touching n8n workflows inventoried
- [ ] Vexa integration points identified
- [ ] Twenty integration points identified
- [ ] Dual-import configuration designed (parallel write vs conditional routing decision)
- [ ] Credential management approach documented
- [ ] Staging vs production migration path defined
- [ ] Rollback plan documented
- [ ] Plan doc at `zo/app/docs/migration/n8n-rewiring-plan.md`

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-08 | COO | Ticket created. PROGRAMME-001 Phase 4 requires n8n re-wiring; no design ticket existed. Blocked by TKT-0008 and TKT-0009 (need sandbox API behaviour before designing re-wiring). Outline can be started now; full execution after sandbox results. | No investigation started. | Activate after TKT-0008 and TKT-0009 complete. | Do not edit live n8n workflows during design phase.

active.title: Configure Keycloak production realm — replace dev import with hardened settings
active.created: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.updated: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.body:
## Context

ADR-002 (in `zo/app/docs/`) states explicitly: "Not completed in this pass: live Keycloak realm configuration." TKT-0010 (auth security audit) will identify whether the current dev realm import is safe for production and what settings must change. This ticket is the implementation follow-on: when TKT-0010 produces its findings, this ticket executes the production realm configuration. Without it, there is no scoped ticket to receive TKT-0010's remediation items, and the auth configuration work would either be done inline in TKT-0010 (wrong scope — TKT-0010 is audit only) or fall through the cracks. The platform cannot handle real investment data on a dev-configured Keycloak realm. PROG-001, WS-SHELL, Phase P2.

## Current State

- Keycloak is running in gordon-stack.
- Current realm: imported from a dev realm JSON. Token expiry, PKCE enforcement, redirect URIs, client type (public vs confidential), session limits, and brute-force protection settings are all unverified for production use.
- TKT-0010 is OPEN and will audit these settings. Its findings will populate this ticket's task list.
- The platform's auth flow is documented in ADR-002 and the TKT-0010 ticket.

## Task

**Wait for TKT-0010 to complete and produce its findings before executing this ticket.**

When TKT-0010 produces its findings, update this ticket's task list with the specific configuration changes required. Typical items expected (to be confirmed by TKT-0010):

1. Set appropriate access token expiry (e.g. 15 minutes for investment platform use).
2. Set refresh token expiry and rotation policy.
3. Enforce PKCE for all clients.
4. Lock redirect URIs to known platform domains only — remove wildcards.
5. Set client type to confidential (not public) for server-side clients.
6. Enable brute-force protection (lockout policy).
7. Disable account registration if Keycloak self-registration is open.
8. Configure SSL required: `all` (not `external` only).
9. Remove or disable unused identity providers and clients from dev import.
10. Export the production realm JSON and commit it to gordon-stack (no secrets inline — use env var references).
11. **Enable TOTP (2FA) as a required authentication flow** — Enzo's requirement (confirmed 2026-04-08). Configure the authentication flow to require OTP after password. TOTP is the correct mechanism (self-hosted, no SMS dependency). All platform users must enrol on first login. This is non-negotiable for an investment platform handling real deal data.

Each setting must be verified with a curl or Keycloak admin API call — do not rely on UI screenshots.

## Execution Notes

- Blocked on TKT-0010 findings — do not pre-empt the audit.
- Keycloak is a live service on T418. Every config change is a live service change. Use `00_Standards/runtime-change-control-checklist.md`.
- Export the final production realm as JSON and commit to gordon-stack for reproducibility. Do not commit realm JSON with embedded client secrets — use `${env.SECRET_NAME}` substitution in the export.
- Test the platform login flow after each major change — do not batch-apply all changes without intermediate verification.
- If a setting change breaks the login flow: roll back via Keycloak admin UI immediately and document the exact failure before retrying.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] TKT-0010 findings reviewed and task list updated in this ticket
- [ ] All CRITICAL and HIGH findings from TKT-0010 remediated
- [ ] TOTP (2FA) required authentication flow configured and verified — all users prompted on first login
- [ ] Platform login flow verified after each change
- [ ] Production realm JSON committed to gordon-stack (no embedded secrets)
- [ ] Keycloak admin API confirms final settings match production requirements
- [ ] All changes logged in Handoff Log with before/after state

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-08 | COO | Ticket created as implementation follow-on for TKT-0010 (auth audit). ADR-002 confirms Keycloak realm configuration was not completed. Blocked on TKT-0010 — task list will be populated from audit findings. | No configuration started. | Activate after TKT-0010 is DONE. Read its findings first. | Every Keycloak change is a live service change — use change control.
- 2026-04-08 | COO | Amended: TOTP (2FA) added as explicit required task (#11). Enzo confirmed 2026-04-08: goal is self-hosted auth with 2FA for all platform users. Keycloak TOTP is the correct mechanism — native, no SMS, no third-party dependency. Keycloak already supports TOTP via its built-in OTP policy. TKT-0053 (Keycloak → GoTrue federation) is spawned as a follow-on to this ticket — once the realm is hardened and 2FA is configured, GoTrue federation can be wired. | Task #11 and acceptance criteria updated. | — | —

active.title: Link fundraise-intel company records to canonical company_identity (resolution logic)
active.created: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.updated: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
active.body:
## Context

`fundraise-intel` surfaces sourcing signals (funding events, company news, market signals). These signals refer to companies by name or domain — but the platform's canonical identity for a company is the `company_identity` record in Supabase (project-ninpo schema). Without a join/resolution layer, sourcing events from fundraise-intel cannot route to company workspaces in the platform, comps linked-peer rows have no match logic, and ZO cannot resolve a company name from a sourcing signal to a `company_id` when constructing analysis payloads. This is a recurring glue problem referenced in `backend-gap-map.md`, `comps-surface.md` (TKT-0023 output, cross-reference logic noted as follow-on), and `zo-assistant-architecture.md` Section 2.3 (fundraise-intel as ZO-triggerable service, requiring `company_id` resolution). This ticket designs and implements the resolution logic. PROG-001, WS-DATA, Phase P3.

## Current State

- `fundraise-intel`: local MVP — sourcing signals stored with company name and/or domain. No `company_identity_id` foreign key.
- `project-ninpo` `company_identity` table: has `name` and `domain` fields (confirmed from migration schema). `attio_id` as current primary external key.
- Shadow import completed (TKT-0018/0019): 18 Attio lists, company records populated.
- No resolution/join logic exists between fundraise-intel and company_identity.
- TKT-0035 (deploy fundraise-intel) must complete before this ticket can implement and test.

## Task

1. **Schema**: Assess whether fundraise-intel company records have a domain or name field reliable enough for matching. If not, document the gap.

2. **Resolution strategy**: Design and implement a resolver that maps a fundraise-intel company record to a `company_identity` record. Strategy (in order of preference):
   - **Domain match**: `fundraise_intel.domain = company_identity.domain` — exact match, highest confidence.
   - **Name fuzzy match**: normalised string comparison (lowercase, stripped legal suffixes like Ltd/Inc/GmbH) — use only when domain is absent.
   - **Manual override**: `fundraise_intel_id` → `company_identity_id` mapping table for cases where auto-resolution fails or produces incorrect results.

3. **Implementation options** (choose based on volume and query patterns):
   - Option A: a lookup function in project-ninpo called by the platform API before surfacing a sourcing event.
   - Option B: a background job that enriches fundraise-intel records with `company_identity_id` at ingest time.
   - Option C: a view/join in Supabase combining both tables. Document the chosen approach and rationale.

4. **Confidence scoring**: Record match confidence (exact / fuzzy / manual) alongside each resolved link.

5. **Platform integration**: The platform's sourcing event drilldown (TKT-0006 Session 4) must be able to call a single endpoint that returns the sourcing event plus its resolved `company_id` and `company_identity_name`. Update or create the appropriate API route.

6. **ZO integration note**: Document how ZO should use the resolver — ZO will call `GET /api/companies/resolve?name=<name>&domain=<domain>` (or equivalent) to get `company_id` before constructing an analysis payload.

## Execution Notes

- fundraise-intel must be deployed (TKT-0035) before implementation and testing.
- The `company_identity` table is in Supabase — all queries must go through the platform's Supabase client, not direct DB access.
- Do not write to `company_identity` in this ticket — read only. The resolver links existing records; it does not create new ones.
- If domain matching produces <80% coverage on the real fundraise-intel dataset, surface the gap to Enzo before shipping fuzzy matching logic.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Resolution strategy documented (domain / fuzzy / manual override)
- [ ] Resolver implemented (chosen option A, B, or C with rationale)
- [ ] Confidence score stored with each resolved link
- [ ] Platform API endpoint returns sourcing event + resolved `company_id`
- [ ] ZO resolution endpoint documented in `zo-assistant-architecture.md` (or ADR addendum)
- [ ] Domain match coverage rate measured against real fundraise-intel data and reported in Handoff Log
- [ ] Manual override mapping table created for unresolved cases

## Handoff Log
