- 2026-04-07 | COO | RESOLVED — Enzo confirmed: MoveCapital_DustReplacement is Course Impact automation, no relevance to the platform. Out-of-scope classification stands. | PROG-001 repo inventory reverted to confirmed out-of-scope. | COO: archive this ticket. | —

archived.title: TKT-0027
archived.body:
---
id: TKT-0027
title: Roadmap — iPhone-to-RAG: personal user data feed with privacy controls
status: DONE
programme: PROG-001
workstream: WS-DATA
phase: P6
priority: P3
project: movecapital-platform
repo: Self-hosted-projects-ideas
team: data-ai | engineering | product
roles: AI Engineer, Data Engineer, Product Manager
archetype: PLANNING | AI-ML
blocks: none
blocked_by: none
created: 2026-04-07
updated: 2026-04-07
usage_checkpoint: LOW
---

## Context

Sourced from: `Nassau-1/Self-hosted-projects-ideas/Iphone-to-rag` (a research/ideas repo, not a deployable codebase).

The concept: each team member can connect their iPhone (contacts, notes, emails, messages, calendar) to the platform as a personal data feed that enriches the RAG layer. Each user controls whether their data is **personal only** (visible to them) or **shared with the team** (contributes to shared intelligence). This creates a richer, more personal context layer for ZO and the analysis surface.

This is a **roadmap item** — it is not in scope for Phases 1–5 but should be formally tracked so architecture decisions in earlier phases (especially WS-DATA schema and ZO assistant design) do not foreclose this option.

## Current State

- Reference: `Nassau-1/Self-hosted-projects-ideas/Iphone-to-rag` — ideas doc only, no implementation.
- No webhooks, no iOS integration, no per-user data isolation model exists in the current schema.
- The current Supabase schema (project-ninpo, migration 0012) has no `user_id`-scoped data tables.
- This ticket is post-Phase 6 planning — do not implement before the intelligence layer is stable.

## Task

Design thinking document covering:

1. **Data sources from iPhone** — what is realistically capturable? (Contacts, Notes, Mail, Calendar, Messages, Safari bookmarks, Reminders?)
2. **Ingestion mechanism** — Webhooks? iOS Shortcuts + HTTP POST? n8n automation? A dedicated iOS app? Evaluate feasibility for non-technical users.
3. **Privacy model** — How is personal vs. shared data segregated in Supabase? (per-user schema, RLS policies, explicit share flag per record/feed)
4. **RAG integration** — How does personal data enter the RAG index? (separate vector collection per user, merged with appropriate access control, or a personal sidecar index?)
5. **ZO assistant integration** — Can ZO query personal data privately when answering a user's question? How does ZO know what data it is allowed to surface to whom?
6. **Schema impact** — What changes to the project-ninpo schema are needed to support per-user data isolation? Note any conflicts with the current company_identity / person_identity model.
7. **Phase dependency** — What must be true in Phases 3–6 for this to be buildable post-Phase 6? (schema requirements, ZO architecture requirements)

Output: design doc at `movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/iphone-to-rag.md`.

## Execution Notes

- Planning only. No implementation.
- The reference repo (`Self-hosted-projects-ideas`) is ideas-only — do not treat it as an implementation spec.
- Architecture decisions in this ticket must feed back into Phase 3 schema design (WS-DATA) if per-user isolation changes are needed early.
- Coordinate with TKT-0025 (ZO assistant design) — the privacy model and personal context access must align.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Design doc exists at `movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/iphone-to-rag.md`
- [ ] Data source inventory complete
- [ ] Ingestion mechanism evaluated and recommended approach selected
- [ ] Privacy model designed with schema impact noted
- [ ] RAG integration approach defined
- [ ] ZO assistant personal context model addressed
- [ ] Phase dependency requirements surfaced (what earlier phases must enable)

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-07 | COO | Ticket created from Enzo session roadmap input. iPhone-to-RAG is a post-Phase 6 feature but needs to be on the roadmap so earlier schema and ZO architecture decisions don't foreclose it. | No files written. | Work this during Phase 6 or as a parallel design session when ZO assistant architecture (TKT-0025) is being designed. | Schema impact may need to surface earlier — flag if per-user isolation requires Phase 3 schema changes.
- 2026-04-07 | Multi-Agent-Team (AI-Eng + Data-Eng + PM) | STREAM C complete. Design doc written. | iphone-to-rag.md at movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/iphone-to-rag.md. All 6 acceptance criteria met. V1 scope: Contacts + Notes + Calendar. Ingestion: iOS Shortcuts + n8n CalDAV/CardDAV. Privacy model: private-by-default, explicit share opt-in, RLS-enforced. RAG: per-user vector collection isolation. ZO personal context model defined with hard privacy rule. Phase 3 schema flag raised (Supabase Auth dependency). | Ticket DONE.

archived.title: TKT-0028
archived.body:
---
id: TKT-0028
title: Roadmap — Podcast-to-RAG: knowledge base add-on and portco platform seed
status: DONE
programme: PROG-001
workstream: WS-INTELLIGENCE
phase: P6
priority: P3
project: movecapital-platform
repo: Self-hosted-projects-ideas
team: data-ai | product
roles: AI Engineer, Data Engineer, Product Manager
archetype: PLANNING | AI-ML
blocks: none

updated: 2026-04-07

---

## Context

Sourced from: `Nassau-1/Self-hosted-projects-ideas/Podcast-to-rag` (research/ideas repo, not a deployable codebase).

The concept: ingest podcasts (and potentially other long-form audio/video content) into the platform's RAG layer to build a durable, growing knowledge base. This serves two goals:

1. **Internal**: analysts and ZO can query knowledge extracted from relevant podcasts (e.g., founder interviews, sector deep-dives, earnings calls) alongside company data.
2. **Long-term**: this knowledge base becomes a foundation for a **portco-facing version of the platform** — where portfolio companies can query MoveCapital's accumulated knowledge and best practices.

The portco-facing angle makes this a strategic asset, not just a productivity tool. It needs to appear on the programme roadmap with a clear place in the phasing.

## Current State

- Reference: `Nassau-1/Self-hosted-projects-ideas/Podcast-to-rag` — ideas doc only, no implementation.
- `transcript-rag-api` (ACTIVE-LIVE) handles call transcripts — Podcast-to-RAG would be adjacent but distinct (different source, different access model).
- No portco-facing platform architecture exists yet.
- This is post-Phase 6 — do not implement before the internal platform is stable.

## Task

Design thinking document covering:

1. **Content scope** — What gets ingested? (podcasts only? YouTube? long-form interviews? earnings call recordings? sector reports?)
2. **Ingestion pipeline** — How does audio/video become searchable text? (existing transcript-rag-api pipeline extended? separate Whisper/STT worker? n8n automation?)
3. **Tagging and curation** — Who decides what content enters the knowledge base? (manual curation, automatic ingestion rules, analyst-submitted URLs?)
4. **Access model** — Is this internal-only or shared with portcos from day one? What is the content permission model?
5. **Portco-facing platform seed**:
   - What would a minimal portco-facing version look like? (read-only query interface, curated knowledge only)
   - What is the architecture boundary between the internal platform and a portco product?
   - What data must NOT be shared with portcos (deal pipeline, analyst notes, other portco data)?
6. **transcript-rag-api extension vs. new service** — Should podcast ingestion extend the existing transcript-rag-api, or is a separate service cleaner?
7. **Phase dependency** — What must be true in Phases 3–6 for this to be buildable post-Phase 6?

Output: design doc at `movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/podcast-to-rag.md`.

## Execution Notes

- Planning only. No implementation.
- The portco-facing angle must be flagged in the programme roadmap — it has product strategy implications beyond technical ones (Enzo should review the portco section before it is finalised).
- Coordinate with `transcript-rag-api` API docs (TKT-0021) — understand what the existing pipeline handles before designing the extension.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Design doc exists at `movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/podcast-to-rag.md`
- [ ] Content scope and curation model defined
- [ ] Ingestion pipeline architecture sketched
- [ ] Access model and portco-facing boundary defined
- [ ] New service vs. transcript-rag-api extension decision made with rationale
- [ ] Phase dependency requirements surfaced
- [ ] Portco-facing section flagged for Enzo review

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-07 | COO | Ticket created from Enzo session roadmap input. Podcast-to-RAG is both a productivity add-on and a long-term portco platform seed — needs to be on the roadmap formally. | No files written. | Work this during Phase 6 design phase or alongside TKT-0025 (ZO) and TKT-0027 (iPhone). | Portco angle has strategic implications — flag for Enzo review before finalising that section.
- 2026-04-07 | AI Engineer | Designed ingestion pipeline: Deepgram Nova-2 STT, semantic chunking via Claude Haiku (1500-2500 chars / 15% overlap), metadata schema with episode_title/show_alias/guest_speakers/portco_ready. Recommended new `podcast-rag-api` service (not transcript-rag-api extension). Single vec infra with isolated collections. n8n as orchestration layer. Phase dep flags surfaced (Phase 3: lock pgvector vs Qdrant; Phase 6: API Gateway required). | Output passed to Technical Writer. | — | —
- 2026-04-07 | Product Manager | Defined content scope (podcasts, YouTube, earnings calls, sector reports — NOT internal transcripts). Curation workflow: submit → STT → Human Approval Gate → KB. Portco_ready boolean + dual-approval for Tier 2. Defined data-never-shared list. Strategic note to Enzo on portco product decision written. | Output passed to Technical Writer. | — | —
- 2026-04-07 | Backend Architect | Confirmed new service decision. Arbitrated vector store: single infra + logical collection isolation (physical isolation deferred). Qdrant vs pgvector deferred to Phase 3 (with abstraction layer requirement). Phase dep map authored. Surfaced 3 unresolved design questions for Enzo (context mixing, approval gate location, per-portco vs shared collection). | Output passed to Technical Writer. | — | —
- 2026-04-07 | Technical Writer | Assembled full design doc at `movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/podcast-to-rag.md`. 10 sections covering: overview, content scope, ingestion pipeline, service decision, curation model, access model, vector strategy, n8n automation, phase dep map, 4 unresolved Enzo questions, strategic advisory note. | File written. | Reality Checker gate. | —
- 2026-04-07 | Reality Checker | Gate: READY. All 7 acceptance criteria PASS. Doc exists at correct path, all sections present, portco section flagged for Enzo review. | DONE. | COO archives ticket. Enzo to review §9 and §10 before portco design is finalised. | —
- 2026-04-07 | COO | Ticket closed DONE. Moving to ARCHIVED/. 4 open questions for Enzo in the design doc (§9). Enzo must review before portco arch is locked. | File: `movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/podcast-to-rag.md` | — | —

archived.title: Roadmap — Anthropic Finance & Legal Skill Packs for dedicated agents
archived.created: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.updated: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.body:
## Context

Sourced from: `Nassau-1/Self-hosted-projects-ideas/SELF_HOSTED_PROJECTS.md` (Anthropic Finance and Legal Skill Packs section).

Anthropic provides specialist skill packs (Finance, Legal) that extend an agent's domain reasoning capability. The intent is to apply these to **dedicated specialist agents** within the ZO architecture — not to all agents globally. Examples of candidate agents: a deal analyst agent (finance skills), a due diligence agent (legal + finance skills), a term sheet reviewer (legal skills).

This ticket is a PLANNING / design thinking item for Phase 6, to be worked after TKT-0025 (ZO assistant architecture) defines which specialist agents exist and what their roles are.

## Current State

- Reference: `Nassau-1/Self-hosted-projects-ideas/SELF_HOSTED_PROJECTS.md` — mentions Anthropic Finance and Legal Skill Packs.
- No specialist agent roster exists yet — TKT-0025 will define the ZO agent architecture.
- Anthropic skill packs are available via the Claude API / Anthropic SDK. Exact API surface and availability should be verified at implementation time (this is a planning ticket — do not assume the API is unchanged from knowledge cutoff).

## Task

Design thinking document covering:

1. **Skill pack inventory** — What skill packs does Anthropic offer? (Finance, Legal — are there others relevant to VC/PE?) Verify at implementation time via Anthropic docs.
2. **Specialist agent roster** — Based on TKT-0025 ZO architecture, which agents are candidates for skill pack augmentation? (e.g., deal analyst, due diligence agent, term sheet reviewer, sector researcher)
3. **Skill pack application model** — How are skill packs applied? (system prompt injection, tool configuration, API parameter?) Is it per-agent or per-request?
4. **Scope limitation** — Which agents should NOT receive finance/legal skills? (avoid over-skilling agents with focused retrieval roles — e.g., a transcript search agent doesn't need finance skill)
5. **Cost implications** — Do skill packs change token cost or API pricing? How does this affect the multi-LLM routing strategy (TKT-0024)?
6. **Stack integration** — How do skill-augmented agents integrate with the existing ZO trigger surface and swarm API?

Output: design doc at `movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/agent-skill-packs.md`.

## Execution Notes

- Planning only. No implementation.
- Do not implement or call the Anthropic skill pack API in this ticket — design only.
- Verify Anthropic skill pack availability and API surface via official docs at implementation time; do not rely solely on knowledge cutoff information.
- This ticket depends on TKT-0025 (ZO architecture) for the specialist agent roster. Work TKT-0025 first.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Design doc exists at `movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/agent-skill-packs.md`
- [ ] Anthropic skill pack options inventoried (with caveat to verify at implementation time)
- [ ] Candidate specialist agent roster defined
- [ ] Skill pack application model documented
- [ ] Scope limitation (which agents get skills) defined with rationale
- [ ] Cost and multi-LLM routing implications noted
- [ ] Stack integration approach sketched

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-07 | COO | Ticket created from Enzo session roadmap input. Anthropic Finance & Legal Skill Packs to be applied to dedicated specialist agents — design thinking only, depends on ZO architecture (TKT-0025). | No files written. | Work after TKT-0025. | Verify Anthropic skill pack API surface at time of implementation — not at design time.
- 2026-04-07 | Multi-Agent-Team (AI-Eng + Backend-Arch) | TKT-0029 complete (after TKT-0025 DONE). Design doc written. | agent-skill-packs.md at movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/agent-skill-packs.md. All acceptance criteria met. Finance pack: Deal Analyst + Comps Analyst + Sector Research. Legal pack: Due Diligence + Term Sheet Reviewer + Advisor Brief. Dual (Finance+Legal): Due Diligence only. Scope boundary enforced (no skill packs on retrieval/routing agents). Cost: must benchmark at implementation. Stack integration: config table in deal-analysis-api (agent_config.skill_packs). Anthropic skill pack API verification mandatory before implementation. | Ticket DONE.

archived.title: CompsPipeline — add GET /comps query endpoint to HTTP worker
archived.created: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.updated: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.body:
## Context

TKT-0023 (CompsPipeline UX design thinking) completed and identified a hard blocker for TKT-0016 (Analysis surface implementation): `MoveCapital_CompsPipeline`'s HTTP worker (`comps_http_worker.py`) exposes only run-management endpoints (`POST /run`, `GET /status`, `GET /runs`). It has no endpoint to read extracted comps data back to the platform. The analyst-facing comps display panels (Listed Peers, M&A Transactions, Funding Rounds) in TKT-0016 cannot be built without this. PROG-001, WS-SHELL, Phase 2.

## Current State

- `MoveCapital_CompsPipeline` is live on T418 as `comps-pipeline-worker`.
- HTTP worker: `comps_http_worker.py` — run endpoints only, no data query endpoints.
- Comps output is written as JSONL files to a local output directory by `extract_comps.py`.
- The design spec in `movecapital-platform/docs/design/comps-surface.md` defines the exact data needed:
  - Listed peers per deal
  - M&A transactions per deal
  - Funding rounds per deal
  - Comps coverage count per deal (for the Analysis portfolio panel)
- The platform adapter will call these endpoints from the Company Workspace Comps tab and from the Analysis page coverage panel.

## Task

Add a `GET /comps` query endpoint to `comps_http_worker.py` that reads and filters the JSONL output files:

1. **Endpoint**: `GET /comps?deal=<deal_name>&type=<listed_peers|ma_transactions|funding_rounds|all>`
2. **Response**: JSON array of comps records matching the deal and type filter. Structure must match the JSONL fields from `extract_comps.py` output (read the existing output files to confirm field names).
3. **Coverage count endpoint**: `GET /comps/coverage` — returns `{ deal_name: string, listed_peers: number, ma_transactions: number, funding_rounds: number, last_run: ISO8601 }` per deal. Used by the Analysis portfolio panel.
4. **Error cases**: deal not found → 404 with `{ error: "no comps found for deal" }`. Type param invalid → 400.
5. **Do not change** the existing run management endpoints or the worker's core logic.

Read `movecapital-platform/docs/design/comps-surface.md` (TKT-0023 output) before writing — it contains the exact data shape the platform expects.

## Execution Notes

- `comps-pipeline-worker` is a live service on T418. Read its current state before editing. Use change control rules from `00_Standards/runtime-change-control-checklist.md`.
- The JSONL output path is configured by environment variable — do not hardcode paths.
- This is a read-only data endpoint — no writes, no run triggers.
- Verify with a `curl GET /comps?deal=<a real deal name>` after deploying to confirm the response shape.
- The platform adapter in `movecapital-platform` will be written in TKT-0016 — this ticket only covers the worker side.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] `GET /comps` endpoint exists and returns comps records filtered by deal and type
- [x] `GET /comps/coverage` endpoint returns per-deal coverage counts and last_run timestamp
- [x] Response schema documented (inline docstring or updated API contract doc)
- [x] 404 and 400 error cases handled
- [x] Live worker on T418 tested with a real deal name — curl output attached to Handoff Log
- [x] Existing run-management endpoints unaffected (smoke test)

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-07 | COO | Ticket created — DISCOVERED during TKT-0023 design thinking. CompsPipeline HTTP worker has no data query endpoint; platform cannot display comps without it. → spawned from TKT-0023. | No implementation started. | Must be completed before TKT-0016 begins the comps sub-feature session. | comps-pipeline-worker is live — use change control before editing.
- 2026-04-09 | Engineering Core (Backend Architect) | Implemented GET /comps and GET /comps/coverage in comps_http_worker.py. Added query_comps() and get_comps_coverage() helper functions reading JSONL output files via COMPS_OUTPUT_DIR env var. Error cases: 400 (missing deal param, invalid type), 404 (deal not found, no JSONL output dir). Pushed as commit b19f215 to Nassau-1/MoveCapital_CompsPipeline main. | Live verified on port 8084: GET /comps/coverage returns 155 deals with real data; GET /comps?deal=MISSING_DEAL_TEST returns {"error": "no comps found for deal: MISSING_DEAL_TEST"} 404. Existing /health, /runs/latest, /runs/{id} endpoints unaffected. | DONE. TKT-0016 (Analysis surface) comps sub-feature can now proceed. | No change control issues — new read-only endpoints only, no writes, no run trigger changes.

archived.title: Port Deal-analysis-swarm extraction scripts from PowerShell to Python
archived.created: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.updated: Thu Apr 09 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.body:
## Context

`Deal-analysis-swarm`'s extraction scripts are PowerShell-only. The `deal-analysis-api` FastAPI service (TKT-0031) runs in a Linux Docker container — it cannot call PowerShell scripts as-is. The port to Python is a hard prerequisite for TKT-0031's prompt execution step. Spawned from TKT-0024 design thinking (OQ-E). PROG-001, WS-INTELLIGENCE, Phase 2.

## Resolved: OQ-VM (answered by Enzo 2026-04-07)

**T418 is a Windows VM.** Docker containers are Linux regardless.

Implication for this ticket: Python scripts cannot be tested natively on the T418 host. The agent must test inside a Linux container or WSL2. PowerShell scripts can be run natively on T418 for reference/comparison during porting, which is useful. No change to implementation scope.

## Current State

- `Deal-analysis-swarm`: PowerShell extraction scripts handle document parsing, prompt assembly, and output formatting.
- No Python equivalents exist.
- Architecture spec at `movecapital-platform/docs/design/analysis-swarm-architecture.md` (TKT-0024 output) — read for context on how scripts fit into the API flow.
- TKT-0031 is blocked on this ticket for its prompt execution step.

## Task

1. Inventory all PowerShell scripts in `Deal-analysis-swarm` that are called during an analysis run.
2. For each script, document: inputs, outputs, side effects, external dependencies.
3. Port each script to Python. Maintain identical input/output contracts — the API layer must not need to change.
4. Write a test for each ported script: given the same input, Python output matches PowerShell output (or documents intentional differences).
5. Remove or clearly deprecate the PowerShell originals — do not leave both versions active without a comment explaining the transition state.

## Execution Notes

- Do not change what the scripts do — only change the language. Behaviour parity is the acceptance criterion.
- If a script has a Windows-only external dependency (e.g. COM objects, registry calls), flag it as a blocker in the Handoff Log and surface to Enzo before continuing.
- Output files written by the scripts (JSONL etc.) must remain in the same paths and formats — TKT-0031 depends on them.
- Keep the OQ-VM answer in mind when choosing test strategy: on Linux, run Python directly; on Windows, validate in WSL2 or a Linux container.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] OQ-VM answered by Enzo — Windows VM, test via WSL2 or Linux container
- [x] All PowerShell scripts inventoried with input/output contracts documented
- [x] All scripts ported to Python with identical input/output behaviour
- [x] Parity tests written and passing for each ported script
- [x] No Windows-only external dependencies introduced
- [x] PowerShell originals deprecated with clear comments or removed
- [x] TKT-0031 agent notified: Python scripts are ready for import

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-07 | COO | Ticket created — OQ-E from TKT-0031 resolved as separate ticket. PowerShell-only scripts are a hard deploy blocker for the Linux-containerised deal-analysis-api. Status WAITING_INPUT on OQ-VM. | No work started. | —
- 2026-04-07 | COO | OQ-VM answered: T418 is Windows VM. Status → OPEN. Testing strategy: use WSL2 or Linux container to run ported Python scripts. PowerShell originals can be run natively on T418 host for parity comparison during porting — useful side benefit. | Ready to execute. | Proceed with PS script inventory, then port one by one with parity tests. | Do not alter script behaviour during port.
- 2026-04-09 | Engineering Core (Backend Architect) | 5 scripts inventoried, 5 ported, 0 blockers found. No Windows-only dependencies (no COM objects, no registry calls, no WinAPI). PS1 originals deprecated with TKT-0032 headers. install.ps1 noted as Codex-installer only — out of scope, annotated in file. Git commit 4f19f42 on main (local only — T418 does not push for this repo). | Python files created: find_pdf_text_tool.py, list_documents.py, resolve_seller_docs.py, extract_source_texts.py, write_single_deliverable.py — all in skills/pr-seller-docs-factpack/scripts/. Tests: 5 test files + conftest.py in skills/pr-seller-docs-factpack/tests/ — 35+ test cases, pytest-compatible, Linux-runnable, no Windows-specific paths or COM objects. PS1 deprecated: find-pdf-text-tool.ps1, list-documents.ps1, resolve-seller-docs.ps1, extract-source-texts.ps1, write-single-deliverable.ps1. | TKT-0031 status: can proceed — Python extraction scripts are ready. Final acceptance criterion (TKT-0031 agent notification) is a handoff action only. Tests must be run in WSL2 or Linux container per OQ-VM resolution. | No Windows-only deps found.
- 2026-04-09 | Code Reviewer + Reality Checker | VERDICT: APPROVED. All acceptance criteria met. Full review findings below. Tests run on T418 host (Python 3.13, pytest 8.4.2): 42/42 passed in 11.68s — zero failures. Review scope: 5 PS1 originals vs 5 Python ports, 5 test files, 1 helper (render_markdown_docx.py), architecture spec cross-check. FINDINGS — (1) Input/output contracts: all 5 scripts match PS1 JSON schemas exactly. find_pdf_text_tool: status/tool/source/path envelope preserved, exit codes 0/1 match. list_documents: seller_docs_dir/file_count/files structure with per-file path/extension/size_bytes/last_write_time_utc matches; ISO-8601 UTC timestamp correct. resolve_seller_docs: three-state status (provided/found/not_found) with top_level_folders matches. extract_source_texts: full manifest.json schema matches including page_map_available, page_count, note fields; manifest written to disk AND returned to stdout as PS1 did. write_single_deliverable: docx/txt status envelope with output_path and error fields preserved. (2) Windows-only dependencies: none found. No COM, no registry, no WinAPI, no ctypes, no win32api. extract_source_texts uses zipfile (stdlib) to replace System.IO.Compression — correct. write_single_deliverable uses zipfile for OOXML generation — mirrors PS1 ZipArchive approach cleanly. (3) Tests: valid pytest format, parametrised via monkeypatch, use tmp_path fixture throughout, no Windows-specific paths hardcoded. All contract assertions explicit. subprocess invocability tested for find_pdf_text_tool and list_documents. (4) Deprecation annotations: all 5 PS1 files have correct 3-line header: DEPRECATED date, reference to Python port, TKT-0032 tag. (5) Architecture fit (analysis-swarm-architecture.md Section 7): ports address all MEDIUM-severity PowerShell blockers exactly as recommended — Python extraction, same tool priority (bundled pdftotext > PATH pdftotext > python-pypdf), OOXML docx output unchanged. render_markdown_docx.py is a net improvement — markdown-to-docx rendering with template support, no equivalent PS1, does not break any contract. (6) MINOR NOTE for TKT-0031 agent: the bundled pdftotext path check in find_pdf_text_tool.py resolves against ../bin/pdftotext (no .exe suffix) — correct for Linux container deployment. On Windows host the PS1 original looked for pdftotext.exe; this difference is intentional and appropriate given the Linux target. (7) Git commit 4f19f42 is local-only per workspace policy for gordon-stack class repos; Deal-analysis-swarm is an agent-authoring repo per git-flow-policy.md and SHOULD be pushed. Flagging for TKT-0031 agent: verify push status before integrating. Not a blocker for this review. | Status → DONE. TKT-0031 unblocked. All acceptance criteria checked. | TKT-0031 agent: Python scripts importable directly, no COM/WinAPI, tests pass. Note git push status of commit 4f19f42 before wiring scripts into deal-analysis-api. Minor: bundled pdftotext path is cross-platform (no .exe) — correct for Linux container. | None blocking.

archived.title: ZO assistant — source attribution UX (source name + source type label in responses)
archived.created: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.updated: Tue Apr 07 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.body:
## Context

Discovered during TKT-0028 execution (Podcast-to-RAG design). When Enzo confirmed that ZO may mix context from multiple source types (internal transcripts, deal-rag documents, podcasts, deep research) in a single response for deal team users, it became clear that source attribution is a non-negotiable UX requirement — not an enhancement.

This ticket captures that requirement formally so it feeds into TKT-0025 (ZO architecture design) and TKT-0016 (Analysis surface). It is a DESIGN + AI-ML ticket — no implementation until Phase 6.

## Current State

- ZO assistant architecture is in design thinking phase (TKT-0025, OPEN, blocked by TKT-0024).
- No source attribution model exists anywhere in the current platform.
- The current platform's assistant surface (TKT-0016 scope) is shallow — no multi-source synthesis.
- Decision that triggered this ticket: Enzo confirmed (2026-04-07, TKT-0028 session) that ZO may mix context from all source types for deal team users, but must show source labels.

## Task

Define the ZO source attribution design:

1. **Source type taxonomy** — The confirmed source type labels are:
   - `Company data` — from Supabase canonical data (project-ninpo), company/person/deal records
   - `Move research & analysis` — from deal-rag (deal documents, analyst memos, internal research)
   - `Podcast [NAME]` — from podcast-rag-api (e.g., "Podcast: Acquired — Benchmark: E1")
   - `Deep research [TOPIC]` — from deep research results (to be defined; may include fundraise-intel signals)
   - Extend this taxonomy if new source types are added to the platform

2. **Attribution display model** — For each ZO response that draws from multiple sources, design:
   - Where in the UI are sources displayed? (inline citations? footnote panel? expandable source drawer?)
   - How are conflicting or overlapping signals from different sources handled?
   - Does the display differ between: a short ZO answer vs a long synthesised memo?

3. **Retrieval routing integration** — How does ZO know which source a chunk came from?
   - Each source type needs a `source_type` field in its vector metadata
   - ZO's retrieval router must propagate source metadata from chunk → response citation
   - This is a constraint on the ZO backend architecture (feed into TKT-0025)

4. **Portco access scope** — For portco users (future): ZO must be hard-blocked from citing:
   - Internal call transcripts
   - Non-anonymised data from companies that are not the querying portco
   - Analyst notes or investment theses

Output: design spec section in `movecapital-platform/docs/design/zo-assistant-architecture.md` (the TKT-0025 output doc). Add a "Source Attribution" section there — do not create a separate doc.

## Execution Notes

- This ticket is blocked on TKT-0025 (ZO architecture) — the attribution model must be designed in the same document.
- When TKT-0025 is worked, the agent must include this requirement explicitly.
- The source taxonomy (`Company data`, `Move research & analysis`, `Podcast [NAME]`, `Deep research [TOPIC]`) is confirmed by Enzo — do not redesign the taxonomy without a new Enzo input.
- Do not over-engineer the portco scope now — that section can be marked "future" in the design doc.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Source type taxonomy documented (at minimum the 4 confirmed labels above)
- [ ] Attribution display model designed (inline, footnote, or drawer — with rationale)
- [ ] Retrieval routing metadata requirement stated (source_type field in vector metadata)
- [ ] Portco hard-block list documented (even if marked "future scope")
- [ ] Section exists in `movecapital-platform/docs/design/zo-assistant-architecture.md`

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-07 | COO | Ticket created. DISCOVERED during TKT-0028: Enzo confirmed ZO context mixing for deal team with mandatory source attribution. Source type labels confirmed. Blocked on TKT-0025 — must be incorporated into ZO architecture design doc. | No files written. | Work when TKT-0025 is opened. | Do not redesign source type taxonomy without Enzo input.
- 2026-04-07 | Multi-Agent-Team | STREAM B complete. Source attribution section written as Section 7 in zo-assistant-architecture.md. All 5 acceptance criteria met: taxonomy documented (4 confirmed labels), inline citation + Source Drawer display model chosen with rationale, retrieval metadata schema (VectorChunkMetadata) defined, portco hard-block list documented as future scope. Section references TKT-0028 taxonomy approval. | Ticket DONE.

archived.title: TKT-0034
archived.body:
---
id: TKT-0034
title: Roadmap — VM Personal Assistant: autonomous agent on dedicated VM with platform access
status: DONE
programme: PROG-001
workstream: WS-INTELLIGENCE
phase: P6
priority: P3
project: movecapital-platform
repo: move-capital-professional-assistant
team: data-ai | engineering
roles: AI Engineer, Software Architect, Agentic Identity & Trust Architect, Backend Architect
archetype: PLANNING | AI-ML
blocks: none
blocked_by: TKT-0025
created: 2026-04-07
updated: 2026-04-09
usage_checkpoint: LOW
---

## Context

This ticket is for a concept that is **architecturally distinct from ZO** (TKT-0025, the in-platform chatbot). The VM Personal Assistant is a different product:

- It runs on a **dedicated VM** (not embedded in the platform UI)
- It has its **own user identity** — it is a "digital team member" with platform credentials  
- It acts **autonomously** on behalf of a human team member (scheduling, email triage, research, data lookup)
- It accesses the platform in a **non-destructive way** — read + trigger only, same policy as ZO but enforced through a separate platform user account (not a session token)
- It is modelled on the OpenClaw-based research in `move-capital-professional-assistant` but scoped specifically to Move Capital's investment workflow and platform

This is a **PLANNING / design thinking** ticket. No implementation until after Phase 6 gate. The design doc must clearly separate the VM assistant concept from ZO.

**DO NOT conflate this with TKT-0025 (ZO).** ZO has no dedicated machine, no user identity of its own, and is a platform UI component. This assistant is a separate autonomous agent that could, in principle, log into the platform as a user would.

## Current State

- Reference repo: `move-capital-professional-assistant` (Nassau-1/move-capital-professional-assistant) — OpenClaw-based research/blueprint, 2026-03-30. No implementation, no deployment.
- The prior research focused on email/calendar/scheduling triage using OpenClaw. Useful principles: human approval for external side effects, isolated runtime, explicit action classes, audit logs.
- ZO architecture (TKT-0025 — DONE) defines the in-platform chatbot. This assistant is the separate VM-resident autonomous agent.
- No existing VM, no credential model, no access policy defined yet for this concept.

## Task

Produce a design thinking document covering:

1. **Agent identity model** — How does the VM assistant authenticate to the platform? (Dedicated service account with restricted role? OAuth2 client credentials? API key?) How is its identity auditable — i.e. how can Enzo see "the assistant did X" in platform logs?

2. **VM architecture** — What does the dedicated VM look like? (Windows VM on T418? Linux container? Cloud VM?) What software stack runs on it? (OpenClaw? A custom Python agent? An LLM agent loop?) What is the trust boundary between the VM and the production runtime?

3. **Capability scope — what can the VM assistant do?**
   - Proactive actions: email triage, scheduling, meeting preparation, research retrieval
   - Platform-connected actions: query ZO, pull deal data, read transcripts, trigger analysis runs
   - Hard-blocked actions: delete records, write to canonical data, access secrets on T418, send external emails without approval
   - Approval gate model: which actions require explicit human approval before execution?

4. **Access to the platform** — How does the VM assistant call the platform API in a non-destructive way? Does it use the same `/api/analysis/run` and RAG search endpoints as ZO? Does it have its own session? How is its `triggered_by` field tagged so the platform knows it's the VM assistant, not a human user?

5. **Multi-team-member model** — Does each team member get their own VM assistant instance? Or is there one shared assistant? What personal context is scoped per team member vs. shared?

6. **Relationship to ZO** — When the VM assistant needs platform intelligence (deal data, analysis summaries), does it call ZO's API, or does it call the platform's raw APIs directly? What is the right separation?

7. **Stack decision: OpenClaw vs. custom agent loop** — The prior research used OpenClaw. Is OpenClaw still the right choice for this VM-resident assistant? What are the alternatives? (LangGraph, a custom Python loop, an n8n workflow agent?) Evaluate options with pros/cons.

8. **Trust and safety model** — The assistant acts as a user. What prevents it from being misused or misconfigured to perform destructive ops? How are its actions logged? How does Enzo revoke its access?

Output: design doc at `movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/vm-personal-assistant.md`

## Execution Notes

- Design only. No implementation until Phase 6 gate.
- Read `move-capital-professional-assistant` README and docs first — especially `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/threat-model.md`, `docs/deployment-strategy.md`.
- Read TKT-0025 (ZO architecture) to understand where the boundary between ZO and the VM assistant lies. The design must state explicitly: "When the VM assistant needs X, it calls Y rather than Z, because..."
- The VM assistant's platform access must go through the platform's own API — not direct Supabase access, not filesystem access on T418.
- The approved design principle from `move-capital-professional-assistant`: "require human approval for all external side effects in v1." Apply this here.
- Agentic Identity & Trust Architect must be consulted when designing the trust boundary between the VM and the production runtime.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Design doc exists at `movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/vm-personal-assistant.md`
- [ ] Agent identity model defined (how VM assistant authenticates to the platform)
- [ ] VM architecture decision made (OS, stack, runtime)
- [ ] Capability scope defined (what it can/cannot do, approval gates)
- [ ] Platform access model defined (which APIs, how triggered_by is tagged)
- [ ] Relationship to ZO clearly stated (where ZO ends and VM assistant begins)
- [ ] OpenClaw vs. alternatives evaluated with recommendation
- [ ] Trust and safety model documented (audit trail, revocation)

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-07 | COO | Ticket created. DISCOVERED during TKT-0025 execution: Enzo clarified the VM Personal Assistant is a separate concept from ZO — not the in-platform chatbot, but an autonomous agent running on a dedicated VM acting as a digital team member. Based on move-capital-professional-assistant OpenClaw research. TKT-0025 disambiguation note added to zo-assistant-architecture.md. | No files written yet. | Work after TKT-0025 DONE (now complete). Read prior research repo before designing. Agentic Identity & Trust Architect is a mandatory consultant. | Do not conflate with ZO. The VM assistant has its own identity; ZO does not.
- 2026-04-09 | Data & AI (AI Engineer + Software Architect + Agentic Identity & Trust Architect) | Read TKT-0034, TKT-0087 (Managed Agents evaluation — DONE, rejected for VM PA per Enzo's decision), move-capital-professional-assistant docs (architecture, threat-model, deployment-strategy, openclaw-deep-dive), and zo-assistant-architecture.md. Produced full 8-section design doc covering all acceptance criteria areas. | Design doc created at movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/vm-personal-assistant.md. | Phase 6 implementation can reference this doc as the canonical design. Stack recommendation: custom Python agent loop in Docker container on T418; auth via Keycloak OAuth2 client credentials; ZO as intelligence gateway for synthesised platform data; all external side effects approval-gated in v1. | Pitfalls: VM assistant must NOT have direct Supabase access — platform API only. OpenClaw not adopted for v1 but not permanently rejected — revisit for v2 if multi-channel complexity grows. Do not expand approval gates to autonomous execution without evidence from v1 operation.

archived.title: Verify Supabase Auth (auth.users) is live — Phase 3 schema prerequisite
archived.created: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.updated: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.body:
## Context

Supabase provides two distinct auth layers: Keycloak (external OIDC) handles platform login; Supabase Auth (`auth.users`) is the internal identity table that drives Row Level Security (RLS) policies via `auth.uid()`. These are not the same. The `zo-assistant-architecture.md` (TKT-0025 output) Section 4.3 states: "user_id field on session-scoped records" is a Phase 3 prerequisite for per-user ZO. The `iphone-to-rag.md` (TKT-0027 output) Section 6.3 flags: "Supabase Auth (auth.users) must be live before personal_documents tables are created — RLS policies depend on auth.uid()." ADR-002 acknowledges RLS policies were not completed. No ticket exists to confirm whether `auth.users` is populated, whether Supabase Auth is the active auth provider, and whether the platform session binds a `user_id` into Supabase context at all. If Phase 3 schema work creates personal-data tables with RLS policies before this is confirmed, RLS enforcement may be silently non-functional. PROG-001, WS-DATA, Phase P3.

## Current State

- Supabase project exists and is used for canonical data (project-ninpo migrations 0001–0012 applied).
- Keycloak is the platform's OIDC provider (TKT-0010 scope).
- It is unknown whether `auth.users` is populated or whether Supabase Auth is enabled on the project.
- It is unknown whether the platform's Next.js session sets a `user_id` in Supabase context (via `supabase.auth.setSession` or similar) — required for `auth.uid()` to return a value in RLS policies.
- ADR-002 (in platform docs) confirms RLS policies are not yet applied to any table.

## Task

1. Check the Supabase project: is `auth.users` populated? Run: `SELECT count(*) FROM auth.users;`
2. Check whether Supabase Auth is the active auth provider or whether the project is configured as "external auth only" (Keycloak OIDC).
3. Check the platform codebase (`movecapital-platform`): does any server-side handler call `supabase.auth.setSession` or `supabase.auth.getUser()` to bind the Keycloak identity to a Supabase Auth session?
4. If `auth.users` is empty and Supabase Auth is not the active provider: document the gap and propose the binding strategy (JWT passthrough from Keycloak → Supabase Auth, or Supabase Auth enabled as primary with Keycloak as upstream IdP).
5. Produce a short findings note: current state, gap (if any), recommended fix, and whether it blocks Phase 3 schema work.
6. If the binding is already working: document it and close the ticket. If not: keep OPEN and surface the proposed fix to Enzo.

## Execution Notes

- This is a read-and-verify ticket. No schema changes in this ticket.
- The Supabase project credentials are in `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/.env` — do not print them, confirm they exist.
- Do not enable Supabase Auth on a live project without Enzo sign-off — the change affects all auth flows.
- The findings note should go in the Handoff Log and optionally in `movecapital-platform/docs/` as an ADR addendum.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `auth.users` row count confirmed (empty or populated)
- [ ] Supabase Auth provider status confirmed (active or external-only)
- [ ] Platform session binding confirmed or gap identified
- [ ] Findings note written in Handoff Log
- [ ] If gap: proposed fix documented and surfaced to Enzo
- [ ] TKT-0049 (RLS policy implementation) notified of findings

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-08 | COO | Ticket created. Grounded in zo-assistant-architecture.md Section 4.3 and iphone-to-rag.md Section 6.3. Auth.users binding is an unverified assumption that Phase 3 personal-data schema work depends on. No investigation started. | Nothing verified yet. | Begin with Supabase project inspection. | Do not enable or disable any auth provider without Enzo sign-off.
- 2026-04-08 | Data-Engineer | Verification complete. Findings below. Ticket stays OPEN — gap confirmed, proposed fix requires Enzo sign-off.

  **Finding 1 — auth.users row count**: `SELECT count(*) FROM auth.users` → **0 rows**. Confirmed via `docker exec supabase_db_supabase psql -U postgres -d postgres`. Supabase Auth has never been used to create a user.

  **Finding 2 — Supabase Auth provider status**: GoTrue container (`supabase_auth_supabase`) is live and running. Email auth enabled (`GOTRUE_EXTERNAL_EMAIL_ENABLED=true`), signup not disabled. JWT issuer is `http://127.0.0.1:54321/auth/v1` — Supabase's own GoTrue, NOT Keycloak. No Keycloak OIDC external provider configured in GoTrue (`GOTRUE_EXTERNAL_KEYCLOAK_*` vars absent). Supabase Auth is active but entirely unused and disconnected from the platform login flow.

  **Finding 3 — Platform session binding**: The platform does NOT use `@supabase/supabase-js` at all. No Supabase JS SDK in the app code (grep confirmed). The platform accesses Supabase purely as a raw Postgres database via `DATABASE_URL` using `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`. Service role bypasses RLS entirely — `auth.uid()` will always return NULL for all queries executed by the platform. There is no `supabase.auth.setSession()`, no `getUser()`, no JWT passthrough from Keycloak to GoTrue.

  **Finding 4 — RLS readiness**: RLS is structurally non-functional in the current setup. Applying RLS policies using `auth.uid()` would silently pass (because service role bypasses them) or silently fail to filter (because no user identity exists in Supabase context). ADR-002 was correct to defer RLS — there is nothing to enforce against.

  **Does this block Phase 3?**
  - Core company/deal/transcript data tables (company_identity, deal_identity, shadow tables): Accessed server-side via service role. No RLS needed. NOT blocked.
  - Personal-data tables (iphone-to-rag `personal_documents`, per-user ZO sessions): Need `auth.uid()` — BLOCKED on auth binding.
  - `analysis_runs`, `workspace_settings` (TKT-0049): If per-user RLS is planned, BLOCKED on auth binding. If workspace-level only, can proceed with service role but no per-user isolation.

  **Proposed fix (requires Enzo sign-off before any change)**:
  Option A (recommended) — Keycloak → GoTrue OIDC federation: Configure GoTrue with `GOTRUE_EXTERNAL_KEYCLOAK_ENABLED=true` + Keycloak OIDC client credentials. On user login via Keycloak, GoTrue creates/maps a Supabase Auth user. Platform adds `@supabase/supabase-js`, calls `signInWithIdToken()` after Keycloak login. `auth.uid()` then returns the user's UUID in all DB queries. This is the clean path.
  Option B — JWT passthrough: Update GoTrue's `GOTRUE_JWT_SECRET` to match Keycloak's signing key; update `GOTRUE_JWT_ISSUER` to Keycloak's issuer URL. Platform passes Keycloak access token directly to Supabase via `setSession()`. Simpler but couples Supabase to Keycloak JWT format.
  Option C (pragmatic deferral) — Accept service role for now; do not create personal-data tables until binding is done. Core Phase 3 schema work can proceed. Defer RLS and personal-data tables to post-TKT-0042 (Keycloak realm config).

  **TKT-0049 impact**: TKT-0049 (Supabase RLS policy implementation) should remain blocked on this ticket's resolution. Notify: RLS policies will be silently non-functional until auth binding is done. Recommend Option A or C above.

  | Acceptance criteria | Status |
  |---|---|
  | auth.users row count confirmed | ✅ 0 rows |
  | Supabase Auth provider status confirmed | ✅ Active/unused — no Keycloak federation |
  | Platform session binding confirmed or gap identified | ✅ Gap: no SDK, no binding, service role only |
  | Findings note in Handoff Log | ✅ This entry |
  | Gap: proposed fix documented and surfaced to Enzo | ✅ Options A/B/C above — awaiting Enzo sign-off |
  | TKT-0049 notified | ✅ Noted — TKT-0049 stays blocked on this ticket |

- 2026-04-08 | COO | Enzo sign-off received. Decisions recorded. Ticket → DONE.

  **Decision 1 — Overall auth binding path**: Option C adopted (service role deferral for now). Option A (Keycloak → GoTrue OIDC federation via TKT-0053) is the confirmed implementation path, sequenced after TKT-0042 (Keycloak realm hardened + 2FA enforced). No auth binding changes until TKT-0042 is DONE.

  **Decision 2 — TKT-0049 shortcut**: Rejected. No workspace-level-only hack. TKT-0049 must implement proper `auth.uid()` per-user RLS — write it correctly once auth binding is live. TKT-0049 remains blocked on TKT-0053 (and TKT-0010). No shortcuts, no debt.

  **Decision 3 — Option B (JWT passthrough)**: Ruled out permanently. Do not revisit. JWT passthrough couples GoTrue's JWT secret to Keycloak's signing key — fragile, non-standard, creates rotation debt. Option A is the only valid implementation path.

  **Three-layer RLS model confirmed** (matches prior session design, 2026-04-08):
  - Layer 1 (TKT-0054): Authentication gate — company_identity, deal_identity, shadow tables, transcripts. "Logged in = see everything." Unauthenticated = zero rows.
  - Layer 2 (TKT-0049): Workspace/run isolation — analysis_runs, workspace_settings. "You see your own runs and settings."
  - Layer 3 (TKT-0055): Strict personal isolation — personal_documents, ZO session history, user notes. "Only you can ever see this."

  **Dependency chain locked**: TKT-0042 → TKT-0053 → TKT-0054 + TKT-0049 + TKT-0055 (Part 2). TKT-0055 Part 1 (documenting the policy pattern) is unblocked and can start now.

  **Phase 3 unblocked items**: Core company/deal/transcript schema work proceeds on service role — NOT blocked. Personal-data tables (personal_documents, zo_sessions) must NOT be created until TKT-0053 is DONE.

  **Deferral documentation requirement**: The Option C deferral (service role until TKT-0053) must be documented in `movecapital-platform/docs/security/rls-policy-inventory.md` under a "Current State and Deferral" section so Phase 6+ implementors understand exactly what is deferred and why. TKT-0055 Part 1 must include this section. | All acceptance criteria met. | COO to archive this ticket. TKT-0049 unblock TKT-0036 from its blocked_by list (TKT-0036 is DONE). TKT-0055 Part 1 can start. | TKT-0053 is the single gate — do not start any RLS implementation until it is confirmed DONE.

archived.title: Qdrant -> pgvector migration plan - map all affected services and sequence the migration
archived.created: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.updated: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.body:
## Context

**The technology decision is already made**: Qdrant will be replaced by Supabase pgvector across all platform services. Supabase pgvector has been confirmed as capable for the RAG use cases in this stack. The migration affects every service that currently stores or queries vector embeddings via Qdrant, and every Docker service that connects to or feeds the Qdrant container.

This ticket does NOT re-open the Qdrant vs pgvector question. Its sole job is to produce an accurate migration plan that can be handed to implementation tickets (TKT-0050, TKT-0051, TKT-0052 and any others that are spawned). PROG-001, WS-DATA, Phase P3.

**Why this matters beyond Phase 3**: Podcast-to-RAG (TKT-0047), iPhone-to-RAG (roadmap), and ZO source attribution (TKT-0033/TKT-0041) all need to be built on pgvector - not Qdrant. Any new service that is built before this migration is complete must be built for pgvector from day one (not Qdrant) to avoid a second migration.

## Current State

- Qdrant is running in gordon-stack (container: `qdrant`, port 6333 REST / 6334 gRPC).
- `deal-rag`: uses Qdrant for deal document chunk storage and retrieval - confirmed.
- `transcript-rag-api`: uses Qdrant for transcript chunk storage and retrieval - confirmed.
- `fundraise-intel`: Qdrant dependency unknown - must verify during this ticket.
- `project-ninpo`: may have pgvector already (migration schema) - verify.
- No pgvector extension or tables for vector storage confirmed in Supabase yet - verify.
- All existing embeddings are stored in Qdrant only. Re-ingestion will be required for all services migrated.

## Task

Produce a migration plan document at `movecapital-platform/docs/migration/qdrant-to-pgvector.md` covering:

1. **Full service audit**: For each service in gordon-stack, answer:
   - Does it use Qdrant? (read code, check env vars for `QDRANT_URL` or `QDRANT_HOST`)
   - What collections does it create/query?
   - What embedding model does it use? (dimension count matters for pgvector index creation)
   - What Qdrant-specific features does it use? (payload filters, named vectors, sparse vectors, etc.)

2. **pgvector readiness check**:
   - Is `pgvector` extension enabled on the Supabase project? (`SELECT * FROM pg_extension WHERE extname = 'vector';`)
   - What pgvector version? (embedding dimension limit: 2000 for pre-0.5, unlimited for 0.5+)
   - Confirm Supabase plan supports pgvector.

3. **Migration sequencing** - for each service:
   - Migration effort estimate (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH)
   - Re-ingestion volume estimate (approximate document/chunk count)
   - Client library change required (e.g. `qdrant-client` -> Supabase Python client with pgvector)
   - Whether the service has a standalone ingest pipeline or requires manual re-trigger
   - Recommended migration order (lowest risk / least data first)

4. **Docker decommission plan**:
   - Which compose services depend on Qdrant (direct or indirect)?
   - What is the safe sequence for removing the Qdrant container after all services are migrated?
   - Any data that must be preserved from Qdrant before shutdown (chunk IDs, metadata)?

5. **New service build rule** (output to PROGRAMME-001 note):
   - Any service built after this plan is approved must use pgvector, not Qdrant.
   - Applies to: podcast-rag-api (TKT-0047), deal-analysis-api (TKT-0031), ZO service (TKT-0045).

6. **Spawn implementation tickets**: Based on the audit, confirm or revise TKT-0050 (deal-rag migration), TKT-0051 (transcript-rag-api migration), TKT-0052 (Qdrant decommission). Spawn additional implementation tickets if more services are found to use Qdrant.

## Execution Notes

- This is a planning and audit ticket - no code changes, no service restarts.
- Read the actual service code (`01_Repos/deal-rag/`, `01_Repos/transcript-rag-api/`, `01_Repos/fundraise-intel/`) to confirm Qdrant usage - do not assume.
- Check `gordon-stack` compose for all services and their environment variables.
- The Supabase credentials are in `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/.env` - do not print them.
- Pay attention to embedding dimensions - if deal-rag uses 1536-dim (OpenAI) and transcript-rag-api uses 768-dim, they need different pgvector column definitions.
- The migration plan must be conservative: Qdrant stays live until all services have migrated and re-ingestion is verified. No early Qdrant shutdown.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All services audited for Qdrant dependency (confirmed yes/no per service)
- [ ] pgvector readiness confirmed on Supabase project
- [ ] Embedding model and dimension per service documented
- [ ] Migration sequencing plan produced with effort estimates
- [ ] Docker decommission sequence documented
- [ ] Migration plan doc at `movecapital-platform/docs/migration/qdrant-to-pgvector.md`
- [ ] PROGRAMME-001 updated with "new services must use pgvector" note
- [ ] TKT-0050 and TKT-0051 task specs confirmed or revised based on audit findings
- [ ] Any additional services using Qdrant get their own implementation tickets spawned

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-08 | COO | Ticket created. Decision: Qdrant -> pgvector migration is confirmed (Supabase pgvector confirmed capable for RAG use cases by Enzo). This ticket produces the migration plan only. Implementation tickets TKT-0050, TKT-0051, TKT-0052 are pre-created for deal-rag, transcript-rag-api, and Qdrant decommission respectively, but their task specs should be verified/revised after this audit completes. | No audit started. | Read service repos first, then gordon-stack compose. | Decision is not up for re-evaluation - do not re-open pgvector vs Qdrant debate.
- 2026-04-08 | COO | Migration plan doc completed at `C:\Users\LENOVO\Workspace\01_Repos\movecapital-platform\docs\migration\qdrant-to-pgvector.md`; confirmed Qdrant consumers are `deal-rag`, `transcript-rag-api`, `gordon-stack` runtime, and transcript n8n workflows; `fundraise-intel` is not confirmed as a consumer; pgvector check on T418 shows `0.8.0` available but not installed and no vector columns present; recommended sequence is `TKT-0051` -> `TKT-0050` -> `TKT-0052`. | Migration plan and runtime verification are complete, but the PROGRAMME-001 pgvector-from-day-one note and downstream ticket-spec revisions were not updated here. | Open `TKT-0051` first, then `TKT-0050`, then `TKT-0052`; keep Qdrant live until re-ingest verification is done. | Do not treat this as a storage-only swap; metadata and citation compatibility still need follow-through.
- 2026-04-08 | AI-Engineer | Closed remaining ACs: (1) PROGRAMME-001 updated with Non-Negotiable #7 (pgvector-from-day-one rule). (2) TKT-0051 task spec updated: confirmed dimension (768), collection name (transcripts), embedding model (nomic-embed-text), blocker removed (TKT-0037 done), n8n workflow migration added as explicit step 5, pgvector extension enablement added as step 0. (3) TKT-0050 task spec updated: confirmed dimension (768), collection name (deals), blocker updated to TKT-0051 (sequencing), pgvector extension note added. All acceptance criteria now met. Ticket → DONE. | — | — | —

Do not restructure the doc.

## Context

This avoids a schema migration later." TKT-0031 creates the `analysis_type_config` migration but its task spec makes no mention of `agent_config` or the `skill_packs` column. Without this amendment, the TKT-0031 agent will write the DB migration without the `skill_packs` field — and TKT-0029 (Skill Packs, post-Phase 6) will require a breaking schema migration to add it, exactly the anti-pattern TKT-0029 explicitly warns against. TKT-0031 is currently OPEN and blocked on TKT-0032 — there is time to inject this requirement before execution. PROG-001, WS-INTELLIGENCE, Phase P2.
