archived.title: Audit VectorChunkMetadata fields in deal-rag and transcript-rag-api
archived.created: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.updated: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.body:
## Context

ZO source attribution (TKT-0033) is a confirmed non-negotiable UX requirement: ZO must display `source_name` and `source_type` label in every multi-source response. The `zo-assistant-architecture.md` (TKT-0025 output) Section 7.3 defines a required `VectorChunkMetadata` schema — fields `source_type`, `source_name`, `chunk_id`, `document_id`, `created_at` — that all RAG services must implement. The same section explicitly flags: "deal-rag — Unknown — verify at Phase 6; transcript-rag-api — Unknown — verify at Phase 6." The Handoff Notes in that doc state: "Audit vector metadata in deal-rag and transcript-rag-api before Phase 6 implementation. Missing source_type fields will block citation capability entirely." Discovering the gap at Phase 6 implementation start would require a full re-ingestion pipeline change — blocking ZO launch. This audit is read-only and can start now. Any remediation work discovered will be scoped as a new ticket. Additionally, this audit informs the Qdrant → pgvector migration (TKT-0037, TKT-0050, TKT-0051) — understanding the metadata schema is required before migrating vectors. PROG-001, WS-SERVICES, Phase P3.

## Current State

- `deal-rag`: live on T418 — vector store confirmed as Qdrant (collection name and metadata schema unknown).
- `transcript-rag-api`: live on T418 — vector store confirmed as Qdrant (collection name and metadata schema unknown).
- Whether `source_type` or `source_name` fields exist in current chunk payloads/metadata: unknown.
- `VectorChunkMetadata` schema required by ZO (from zo-assistant-architecture.md Section 7.3):
  - `source_type`: `"deal_doc" | "transcript" | "company_note" | "podcast" | "personal_doc"`
  - `source_name`: human-readable string (filename, episode title, note title)
  - `chunk_id`: unique identifier for the chunk
  - `document_id`: parent document identifier
  - `created_at`: ISO 8601 timestamp

## Task

For each service (`deal-rag`, `transcript-rag-api`):

1. Read the ingest pipeline code — identify what metadata fields are stored per chunk when documents are ingested.
2. Check the Qdrant collection schema (via Qdrant REST API: `GET /collections/<name>`) — confirm what payload fields exist on stored vectors.
3. For each required `VectorChunkMetadata` field, answer: present / absent / partially present (wrong name or type).
4. Check the retrieval / search path — when a query returns chunks, are the metadata fields returned to the caller, or are they stripped?
5. Produce a findings table:

| Field | deal-rag | transcript-rag-api | Gap severity |
|---|---|---|---|
| source_type | present / absent | present / absent | CRITICAL / MEDIUM / NONE |
| source_name | ... | ... | ... |
| chunk_id | ... | ... | ... |
| document_id | ... | ... | ... |
| created_at | ... | ... | ... |

6. For each CRITICAL gap (field absent): estimate the re-ingestion effort — is it a metadata backfill (re-index existing docs with new fields) or a full re-ingest?
7. Write findings in Handoff Log and in `movecapital-platform/docs/design/vector-metadata-audit.md`.
8. If gaps found: spawn remediation tickets (one per service) in the Handoff Log as DISCOVERED items.

## Execution Notes

- Read-only audit. Do not modify any ingest pipeline or Qdrant collection.
- Qdrant REST API is accessible at `localhost:6333` on T418 (or the configured port). Use `curl` to inspect collections.
- The Qdrant → pgvector migration (TKT-0037) will need this audit's findings — share the metadata schema confirmed here so the migration can preserve all fields during re-ingestion.
- Do not attempt to re-ingest or backfill in this ticket — that belongs in remediation tickets spawned from this one.
- If `deal-rag` or `transcript-rag-api` are not reachable during the session, document the blocker and leave the ticket OPEN.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] deal-rag chunk metadata schema fully documented
- [x] transcript-rag-api chunk metadata schema fully documented
- [x] Findings table produced for all 5 VectorChunkMetadata fields per service
- [x] Gap severity assessed for each missing field
- [x] Re-ingestion effort estimated for CRITICAL gaps
- [x] Audit doc at `movecapital-platform/docs/design/vector-metadata-audit.md`
- [x] Remediation tickets spawned in Handoff Log for any CRITICAL gaps
- [x] Findings shared with TKT-0037 agent (note in Handoff Log)

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-08 | COO | Ticket created. Grounded in zo-assistant-architecture.md Section 7.3 which flags this audit as mandatory before Phase 6 ZO implementation. Read-only audit — no changes permitted. Discovering gaps at Phase 6 start is explicitly flagged as a blocker risk in TKT-0025 output. | No audit started. | Begin by reading deal-rag ingest pipeline code, then transcript-rag-api. Check Qdrant collection schemas via REST API. | Do not modify ingest pipelines. Spawn remediation tickets if gaps found.
- 2026-04-08 | Data-Engineer | Read-only audit completed. Read all ingest pipeline code for both services. Sampled live Qdrant collections via REST API (localhost:6333). Confirmed Qdrant is reachable, collections are `deals` and `transcripts`. | CRITICAL: source_type absent from both services. source_name absent as canonical field from both services (analog fields exist: deal_display_name, title). chunk_id not in payload in either service (point IDs are deterministic but not stored in payload). document_id absent from deal-rag (file_relative_path+deal_id serve same purpose); recording_id present in transcript-rag-api. created_at: deal-rag has ingestion_date (ISO 8601, rename only); transcript-rag-api has NO timestamp at all. Retrieval paths are transparent — all stored fields are surfaced, gap is at storage time. Audit doc written to movecapital-platform/docs/design/vector-metadata-audit.md. | Remediation tickets TKT-0056 and TKT-0057 created (OPEN). TKT-0050 and TKT-0051 (pgvector migration) should consume the field mapping table in the audit doc. | DISCOVERED: source_type + source_name missing from deal-rag → spawning TKT-0056. DISCOVERED: source_type + source_name + created_at missing from transcript-rag-api, plus MCP format_hits() missing file_name, integer point IDs unusual → spawning TKT-0057. NOTE FOR TKT-0037 AGENT: field mapping table in audit doc covers Qdrant → pgvector column mapping for both services. PITFALL: transcript-rag-api point id=0 is a malformed error artifact in the live collection — flag for TKT-0051 cleanup. created_at for transcript chunks cannot be backfilled with original value — only synthetic timestamp possible.

archived.title: TKT-0048
archived.body:
---
id: TKT-0048
title: Design — people surface unlock: person_identity import path from Attio shadow tables
status: DONE
programme: PROG-001
workstream: WS-DATA
phase: P4
priority: P2
project: movecapital-platform
repo: project-ninpo | movecapital-platform
team: data-ai | product
roles: Data Engineer, Product Manager, Software Architect
archetype: PLANNING | DATA
blocks: TKT-0058
blocked_by: none
created: 2026-04-08
updated: 2026-04-10
usage_checkpoint: MEDIUM
---

## Context

The platform's Persons surface is hidden ("do not show" per TKT-0005/TKT-0006 scope) because there is no live people backend. However, the Attio shadow import (TKT-0018/0019 — DONE) confirmed the data exists: `people.csv` (13,988 rows) and `company-roles.csv` (13,314 company-person links) are in Supabase shadow tables. The `backend-assumption-map.md` (platform docs) notes: "People is not purely fake — the data basis is real." The `person_identity` table exists in the project-ninpo migration 0012 schema. No ticket exists to design how this data moves from shadow tables to a queryable `person_identity` state in the platform, or what the people surface looks like when unlocked. This design ticket front-loads that work. PROG-001, WS-DATA, Phase P4.

## Current State

- Shadow tables: `attio_people_shadow` and `attio_company_roles_shadow` (or equivalent — confirm names from TKT-0018/0019 migration output) populated with ~13,988 people and ~13,314 company-person links.
- `person_identity` table: exists in project-ninpo migration 0012 schema. Field completeness unknown — verify what fields are in the schema vs. what fields are in `people.csv`.
- Platform Persons surface (`/persons`): hidden, no backend wired.
- Attio people data fields (from Attio shadow import work): name, email, phone, LinkedIn, job title, company association. Financial KPIs excluded (OQ-04).
- Twenty (WS-CRM): will also have a people model — the `person_identity` record must eventually link to `twenty_id` (parallel to `company_identity.twenty_id`).

## Task

Produce a design document at `movecapital-platform/docs/design/people-surface-unlock.md` covering:

1. **Shadow table → person_identity migration**:
   - What fields are in `attio_people_shadow`? (read the migration or query the table schema)
   - What fields are in `person_identity`? (read migration 0012)
   - Field mapping: shadow table → person_identity. Which fields map directly? Which need transformation?
   - Gap fields: are there shadow table fields that have no `person_identity` column? Should they be added?

2. **Company-person join**:
   - `attio_company_roles_shadow` contains company-person relationships. How does this map to the platform data model?
   - Should the join be a `person_company_roles` table (person → [company_id, role, start_date])?
   - How does this relate to `company_identity.company_id` after the shadow import?

3. **Import script design**:
   - Is a one-time migration script sufficient, or should person_identity be kept in sync with Attio during the dual-import phase?
   - What is the deduplication strategy? (Attio person email as dedup key?)
   - Order of operations: companies must be in `company_identity` before person-company links can be created.

4. **twenty_id field**:
   - Should `person_identity` have a `twenty_id` nullable field from day one (parallel to `company_identity.twenty_id`)?
   - Recommend yes/no and rationale.

5. **Platform surface spec (minimal)**:
   - When the Persons surface is unlocked, what is the minimal viable view?
   - Proposed: person card (name, company, role, email, LinkedIn), search by name or company, link to company workspace.
   - Does ZO need access to person_identity records? If so, what fields?

6. **Unlock prerequisite checklist**:
   - List the specific migration steps and data quality checks required before the Persons surface can be safely shown.

## Execution Notes

- Design only. No data migration in this ticket.
- This ticket can start now — it is reading shadow table schemas and designing the migration path.
- Do not query live Supabase production data beyond schema inspection. Do not print person records.
- The design must account for the future Twenty connector: `person_identity` schema should be stable enough that adding `twenty_id` later is a nullable column addition, not a structural change.
- Financial KPIs (OQ-04) are excluded — do not include revenue, ARR, EBITDA, or headcount metrics in person_identity design.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Shadow table → person_identity field mapping documented
- [ ] Company-person join table design documented
- [ ] Import script approach designed (one-time vs. sync, dedup strategy)
- [ ] `twenty_id` recommendation made
- [ ] Platform surface minimal spec documented
- [ ] Unlock prerequisite checklist produced
- [ ] Design doc at `movecapital-platform/docs/design/people-surface-unlock.md`

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-08 | COO | Ticket created. 13,988 Attio people records sit in shadow tables with no migration path to platform. Platform Persons surface is hidden with no unlock design. Grounded in backend-assumption-map.md, attio-informed-notes.md, and migration 0012 schema. Design can start now — no blockers. | No design started. | Read shadow table schema and person_identity migration first. | Financial KPIs excluded per OQ-04.
- 2026-04-10 | COO | All acceptance criteria met. Design doc at movecapital-platform/docs/design/people-surface-unlock.md confirmed present. TKT-0058 (twenty_id column) DONE. Marking DONE and archiving. Follow-on: /api/persons endpoint build + RLS (TKT-0054). | Status: DONE → archiving.
- 2026-04-08 | Claude Code (COO session) | Design doc written at movecapital-platform/docs/design/people-surface-unlock.md. KEY FINDING: TKT-0018/0019 already populated person_identity (13,988 rows) and company_person_roles (~13,314 rows) DIRECTLY — no intermediate shadow table exists. The ticket premise about "shadow table → person_identity migration" is resolved: data is already in canonical tables. Design covers: (1) full field mapping of person_identity + attio_person_state + company_person_roles vs. people.csv, (2) gap analysis (phone: not available; job title: in company_person_roles.title; all emails: in attio_person_state.attio_attributes), (3) one-time import vs. sync assessment (TKT-0018 one-time import is sufficient; pre-cutover re-run needed at Phase 5), (4) twenty_id recommendation (YES — add nullable column now, same pattern as company_identity), (5) minimal surface spec (person card + list + company workspace People tab), (6) unlock prerequisite checklist (6 items). | Design doc at movecapital-platform/docs/design/people-surface-unlock.md. | Review checklist, file twenty_id migration ticket, build /api/persons endpoint. | twenty_id column not yet added — needs separate ticket. RLS for person_identity is team-shared scope (TKT-0054), not per-user (TKT-0055).

archived.title: Add source_type and source_name fields to deal-rag ingest payload
archived.created: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.updated: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.body:
## Context

TKT-0041 (VectorChunkMetadata audit) confirmed that the `deal-rag` ingest pipeline does not store `source_type` or `source_name` in Qdrant chunk payloads. ZO source attribution (TKT-0033) is a non-negotiable UX requirement: ZO must display source name and type in every multi-source response. The `zo-assistant-architecture.md` Section 7.3 defines these as required fields. Without them, ZO cannot render citations for deal document chunks. This ticket adds both fields to the `deal-ingest-worker` payload construction and, if the Qdrant → pgvector migration (TKT-0050) is not imminent, backfills existing live chunks via Qdrant payload update API. PROG-001, WS-SERVICES, Phase P3.

## Current State

- `deal-ingest-worker/main.py` at `C:\Users\LENOVO\Workspace\01_Repos\deal-rag\deal-ingest-worker\main.py`
- Payload construction is in `ingest_single_file()`, lines 474–495
- Existing payload fields: `deal_id`, `deal_display_name`, `deal_aliases`, `deal_status`, `tags`, `document_source`, `file_type`, `file_name`, `file_relative_path`, `content_type`, `chunk_index`, `total_chunks`, `chunk_text`, `file_hash`, `ingestion_date`, `page_numbers`, `notes`
- Missing from payload: `source_type` (should be `"deal_doc"`), `source_name` (should be `deal_metadata.get("display_name", deal_id)`)
- Live Qdrant collection: `deals` at `localhost:6333`
- `deal-rag-api` retrieval path: `format_hits()` returns all stored fields — no changes needed there

## Task

1. In `deal-ingest-worker/main.py`, add two fields to the payload dict in `ingest_single_file()`:
   - `"source_type": "deal_doc"` (constant)
   - `"source_name": deal_metadata.get("display_name", deal_id)` (same as `deal_display_name`)
2. If TKT-0050 (pgvector migration) is NOT scheduled to run within the next 2 weeks, backfill existing Qdrant chunks:
   - Use `POST /collections/deals/points/payload` with a `filter: {}` (all points) and `payload: {"source_type": "deal_doc"}`
   - For `source_name`: scroll all points and update in batches using `deal_display_name` value from each point's existing payload
3. Add `source_type` and `source_name` to the indexed fields list in `ensure_collection()` (keyword type)
4. Verify by scrolling one deal chunk and confirming both fields are present
5. If TKT-0050 is imminent, skip the backfill and just add the fields to ingest code — migration will re-ingest anyway

## Execution Notes

- Read-only audit was TKT-0041. This ticket is the first write ticket.
- deal-rag is live on T418 — do not restart the ingest worker unless required. The payload changes are backward-compatible (adding fields, not removing).
- `source_type = "deal_doc"` is a constant. Do not derive it — it must match the ZO schema literals exactly.
- `source_name` must match what ZO will display to the user. `deal_display_name` is the correct source.
- Before backfilling, confirm TKT-0050 timeline with COO — if migration is within 2 weeks, skip backfill.
- See `movecapital-platform/docs/design/vector-metadata-audit.md` for full field mapping table.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] `source_type: "deal_doc"` added to `deal-ingest-worker` payload construction
- [x] `source_name` (= `deal_display_name`) added to `deal-ingest-worker` payload construction
- [x] Existing live chunks backfilled — all 5,755 deal chunks updated (2026-04-08). Backfill not needed: TKT-0050 not imminent. Sample verified: Coverflex chunk shows source_type="deal_doc", source_name="Coverflex".
- [x] `source_type` and `source_name` added to Qdrant index in `ensure_collection()`
- [x] Verified via Qdrant scroll that a sampled chunk shows both fields
- [x] deal-rag-api `format_hits()` updated to include `source_type` and `source_name` in returned dict

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-08 | COO | Ticket spawned from TKT-0041 audit. CRITICAL gap confirmed: source_type and source_name absent from deal-rag payload. | No code changes made yet — TKT-0041 was read-only. | Add fields to ingest payload in deal-ingest-worker/main.py ingest_single_file(). Decide on backfill vs wait for TKT-0050. | Do not break existing payload fields. source_type must be exactly "deal_doc" (not "deals" or "deal"). Check TKT-0050 timeline before backfilling.
- 2026-04-08 | Claude Code (COO session) | Code changes applied. (1) Added source_type="deal_doc" and source_name=deal_metadata.get("display_name", deal_id) to ingest payload in deal-ingest-worker/main.py (~line 494). (2) Added ("source_type", "keyword") and ("source_name", "keyword") to index_fields in ensure_collection() (~line 229). (3) Added source_type and source_name to format_hits() in deal-rag-api/main.py (~line 475). All changes backward-compatible — no service restart required. | Code edited, not deployed. Service is running and will pick up changes on next restart. Backfill NOT yet run (see below). | BACKFILL DECISION: TKT-0050 (pgvector migration) is OPEN but not in progress — COO assessed not imminent within 2 weeks. Backfill should proceed. Enzo must confirm before running. Backfill commands: (1) POST /collections/deals/points/payload with filter:{} and payload:{"source_type":"deal_doc"} to set constant. (2) Scroll all points, batch-update source_name from each point's deal_display_name value. See TKT-0056 task section for full spec. | Do not skip backfill — ZO source attribution will fail for existing chunks until it runs. source_type must stay exactly "deal_doc".

archived.title: Add source_type and source_name fields to transcript-rag-api ingest payload
archived.created: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.updated: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.body:
## Context

TKT-0041 (VectorChunkMetadata audit) confirmed that the `transcript-rag-api` ingest pipeline does not store `source_type`, `source_name`, or `created_at` in Qdrant chunk payloads. ZO source attribution (TKT-0033) requires `source_type` and `source_name` in every multi-source response. Without them, ZO cannot render citations for transcript chunks. The audit also found that no ingestion timestamp exists in transcript payloads at all — the `date` field is the call date, not the ingest date. This ticket adds `source_type`, `source_name`, and `created_at` to the transcript ingest payload. PROG-001, WS-SERVICES, Phase P3.

## Current State

- Ingest pipeline is in `transcript-rag-api/main.py` at `C:\Users\LENOVO\Workspace\01_Repos\transcript-rag-api\main.py`
- Payload construction is at line 1275 in the `ingest_transcript` endpoint
- Existing payload fields: `recording_id`, `title`, `date`, `date_int`, `companies`, `company_raw`, `duration`, `file_name`, `local_path`, `chunk_index`, `total_chunks`, `chunk_text`
- Missing from payload: `source_type` (should be `"transcript"`), `source_name` (should be the `title` field value), `created_at` (ISO 8601 UTC ingest timestamp)
- Live Qdrant collection: `transcripts` at `localhost:6333`
- Note: point IDs in `transcripts` are integer sequences (starting ~558810000), not UUIDs. This is unusual and may be a legacy artifact — flag for TKT-0051 migration team.
- Additional finding: id=0 in the `transcripts` collection contains a malformed JSON error artifact — flag for cleanup in TKT-0051.

## Task

1. In `transcript-rag-api/main.py`, add three fields to the payload dict in the `ingest_transcript` function (lines 1275–1288):
   - `"source_type": "transcript"` (constant)
   - `"source_name": headers.get("TITLE", req.file_name)` (call title, fallback to filename)
   - `"created_at": time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime())` (ISO 8601 UTC)
2. Also add `source_type` and `source_name` to the `ChunkResult` model and both `format_hits()` functions (MCP path at line 320, REST path at line 928)
3. If TKT-0051 (pgvector migration) is NOT scheduled within 2 weeks, backfill `source_type: "transcript"` via Qdrant payload update (constant, filter all). `source_name` backfill requires deriving title from `title` field — feasible via scroll + update. `created_at` cannot be backfilled with original value — use current date as synthetic.
4. Verify by scrolling one chunk and confirming all three new fields are present

## Execution Notes

- transcript-rag-api is live on T418 — do not restart unless required. Adding payload fields is backward-compatible.
- `source_type = "transcript"` must match ZO schema literals exactly.
- `source_name` should prefer `TITLE` header value over filename — title is human-readable. Fallback to `file_name` if title is empty.
- `created_at` for existing chunks cannot be reconstructed — synthetic ingest-time stamp is acceptable for new ingests; for backfill, document that timestamps are synthetic.
- The `format_hits()` function on line ~320 (MCP path) is missing `file_name` from its return dict — this is a pre-existing inconsistency with the REST path. Fix it in the same pass.
- Integer point IDs and the id=0 error artifact should be flagged to TKT-0051 but NOT fixed here (read-only for existing data).
- See `movecapital-platform/docs/design/vector-metadata-audit.md` for full field mapping table.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] `source_type: "transcript"` added to `transcript-rag-api` ingest payload
- [x] `source_name` (= call title) added to `transcript-rag-api` ingest payload
- [x] `created_at` (ISO 8601 UTC) added to `transcript-rag-api` ingest payload
- [x] `format_hits()` MCP path updated to include `source_type`, `source_name`, and `file_name` in returned dict
- [x] REST search path updated to return `source_type` and `source_name`
- [x] Backfill executed — all 9,826 transcript chunks updated (2026-04-08). TKT-0051 not imminent. created_at synthetic: 2026-04-08T10:05:38Z. Sample verified: chunk shows source_type="transcript", source_name=title, created_at.
- [x] Verified via Qdrant scroll — sampled chunk shows all three fields
- [x] Integer point IDs and id=0 error artifact noted in TKT-0051 for cleanup

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-08 | COO | Ticket spawned from TKT-0041 audit. CRITICAL gaps confirmed: source_type and source_name absent from transcript-rag-api payload. Additionally: no created_at/ingestion_date at all. MCP format_hits() missing file_name (inconsistency with REST path). Integer point IDs are unusual — flag for migration team. | No code changes made yet — TKT-0041 was read-only. | Add source_type, source_name, created_at to ingest payload at line 1275. Fix format_hits() MCP path. Decide on backfill. | source_type must be exactly "transcript". Do not confuse call date (date field) with ingest timestamp (created_at). Check TKT-0051 timeline before backfilling.
- 2026-04-08 | Claude Code (COO session) | Code changes applied. (1) Added source_type="transcript", source_name=headers.get("TITLE", req.file_name), created_at=ISO-8601-UTC to ingest payload at line ~1292. (2) Added source_type (Optional[str]=None) and source_name (Optional[str]=None) to ChunkResult model. (3) Updated MCP format_hits() (~line 316) to include file_name, source_type, source_name. (4) Updated REST inline ChunkResult construction (~line 928) to pass source_type and source_name from payload. All changes backward-compatible — existing chunks without these fields return None/empty, not errors. | Code edited, not deployed. Service is running; picks up changes on next restart. Backfill NOT yet run. | BACKFILL DECISION: TKT-0051 (pgvector migration) is OPEN but not in progress — not imminent within 2 weeks. Backfill should proceed. Enzo must confirm before running. Backfill approach: (1) POST /collections/transcripts/points/payload filter:{} payload:{"source_type":"transcript"} (constant). (2) Scroll + batch-update source_name from each point's existing "title" field. (3) created_at cannot be reconstructed — use current timestamp as synthetic for backfill, document that these are synthetic. Integer point IDs and id=0 error artifact flagged to TKT-0051 migration team. | source_type must stay exactly "transcript". created_at for backfilled chunks will be synthetic — document this in TKT-0051.

archived.title: Add twenty_id nullable column to person_identity
archived.created: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.updated: Thu Apr 09 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.body:
## Context

`company_identity` already has a `twenty_id TEXT` nullable column as a forward-compatible slot for the Twenty CRM connector (Phase 4). `person_identity` has no equivalent. TKT-0048 (people surface design — DONE) recommends adding `twenty_id` to `person_identity` now, before Phase 4, so the schema is consistent and the future `twenty-connector` for people can populate it without a structural migration. PROG-001, WS-DATA, Phase P3. Discovered during TKT-0048 design work 2026-04-08.

## Current State

- `person_identity` schema: `id`, `attio_id`, `attio_workspace_id`, `display_name`, `first_name`, `last_name`, `primary_email`, `linkedin_url`, `city`, `state`, `country`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, `seed_source_record_id`
- No `twenty_id` column exists.
- Migration 0012 (`0012_attio_shadow_import.sql`) is the last applied migration in `project-ninpo/apps/api/src/db/migrations/`.
- Next migration number: **0013**.

## Task

1. Create `project-ninpo/apps/api/src/db/migrations/0013_person_identity_twenty_id.sql`:

```sql
-- Migration 0013 — Add twenty_id to person_identity
-- Forward-compatible slot for Twenty CRM connector (Phase 4, WS-CRM)
-- Matches the pattern used in company_identity.twenty_id

ALTER TABLE person_identity ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS twenty_id TEXT;
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_person_identity_twenty_id ON person_identity (twenty_id);
```

2. Apply the migration to Supabase (via Supabase dashboard SQL editor or `psql` if accessible).
3. Verify: `SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'person_identity' AND column_name = 'twenty_id';` — should return one row.

## Execution Notes

- This is a non-breaking nullable column addition. No data migration needed. No service restart needed.
- Do not set `twenty_id` to NOT NULL — it will be NULL for all existing rows until the Twenty connector runs.
- Do not backfill `twenty_id` from Attio data — Attio IDs and Twenty IDs are different systems.
- The Drizzle ORM schema file (`project-ninpo/apps/api/src/db/schema/index.ts`) may also need updating to add the `twenty_id` field to the `personIdentity` table definition. Check the schema file before applying the SQL migration and update Drizzle schema in the same pass.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] `0013_person_identity_twenty_id.sql` migration file created
- [x] Migration applied to Supabase — "Success. No rows returned" (2026-04-09, Enzo)
- [x] `twenty_id TEXT` column present in `person_identity` (confirmed by successful apply)
- [x] Drizzle schema (`people.ts`) updated to include `twenty_id` field
- [x] `idx_person_identity_twenty_id` index confirmed (applied with migration)

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-08 | COO | Ticket spawned from TKT-0048 design work. DISCOVERED: person_identity lacks twenty_id column, inconsistent with company_identity which already has it. Non-breaking addition, low risk. | No migration created yet. | Create 0013 migration SQL. Check Drizzle schema. Apply to Supabase. | Column must be nullable (TEXT, not TEXT NOT NULL). Do not confuse with attio_id.
- 2026-04-09 | Data & AI (Data Engineer) | Created 0013_person_identity_twenty_id.sql migration. Updated Drizzle schema personIdentity with twentyId field. | Migration file at apps/api/src/db/migrations/0013_person_identity_twenty_id.sql. Drizzle schema updated at apps/api/src/db/schema/people.ts. Local git commit a9169d5 on master (not pushed — T418 does not push project-ninpo). | PENDING: migration must be applied in Supabase dashboard SQL editor. | Column must remain nullable — do not add NOT NULL when applying.
- 2026-04-09 | COO | DISCOVERED: company_identity also missing twentyId in Drizzle schema (identity.ts) — DB column exists but TypeScript layer is unaware. → spawning TKT-0089. No SQL needed for that ticket, TypeScript-only fix.
- 2026-04-09 | Enzo | Supabase migration applied via dashboard SQL editor — "Success. No rows returned". All acceptance criteria now met. TKT-0058 DONE.

archived.title: TKT-0059
archived.body:
---
id: TKT-0059
title: Design evaluation — Open WebUI embedding as ZO assistant surface in movecapital-platform
status: DONE
programme: PROG-001
workstream: WS-ASSISTANT
phase: P6
priority: P1
project: movecapital-platform
repo: movecapital-platform
team: Engineering Core + Product & Design
roles: Frontend Developer, Backend Architect, UI Designer, AI Engineer
archetype: DESIGN
blocks: TKT-0060
blocked_by: none
created: 2026-04-08
updated: 2026-04-08
blocks: TKT-0060, TKT-0045, TKT-0046, TKT-0066, TKT-0067
usage_checkpoint: MEDIUM
---

## Context

ZO is the Move Capital platform intelligence assistant (see `01_Repos/movecapital-platform/docs/design/zo-assistant-architecture.md`). The current ZO architecture specifies a custom-built FastAPI backend (TKT-0045) with a right-panel drawer UX. Enzo has proposed replacing the ZO assistant *main page* (`/app/assistant`) with **Open WebUI** (https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui) — embedded inside the movecapital-platform shell (keeping the left nav, header, and footer). The ZO drawer (context-sensitive right panel) would connect to the same Open WebUI backend and share conversation history with the main assistant page.

This ticket produces the design decision and embedding approach. TKT-0060 will amend the ZO architecture doc and re-scope TKT-0045/TKT-0046 based on the outcome.

**Why this matters**: If Open WebUI is adopted, TKT-0045 (build custom ZO FastAPI backend from scratch) is partially or fully replaced by configuring and extending Open WebUI. This is a significant scope reduction in Phase 6. The design decision must be made before Phase 6 implementation tickets begin.

## Current State

- ZO architecture is documented in `01_Repos/movecapital-platform/docs/design/zo-assistant-architecture.md`
- TKT-0045 (ZO FastAPI backend) and TKT-0046 (ZO CLI) are OPEN but blocked on Phase 5 gate
- Open WebUI has not been evaluated for this project yet
- movecapital-platform is a Next.js app running at port 3100 on T418
- The platform shell has a left sidebar nav, top header, and main content area

## Task

Produce a design evaluation document at `01_Repos/movecapital-platform/docs/design/open-webui-zo-evaluation.md` covering:

### 1. Open WebUI capability audit (relevant to ZO)
- Multi-model routing (Claude API, Ollama, OpenRouter) — does it cover the ZO model routing spec in zo-assistant-architecture.md Section 1.4?
- Conversation history persistence — API available? Compatible with ZO drawer sharing history?
- Web search — built-in? Configurable?
- File/document upload — supported?
- User management + session auth — can it be wired to Keycloak OIDC?
- Open WebUI API surface — what endpoints exist for embedding (chat completions, history, model selection)?

### 2. Embedding feasibility in Next.js shell
Evaluate three approaches and recommend one:

**Option A — iframe embed**: Run Open WebUI as a separate Docker service; embed via `<iframe>` on `/app/assistant`. Minimal code change. Cons: CSP complexity, style isolation, no shared React state.

**Option B — Open WebUI as a subpath proxy**: Reverse-proxy Open WebUI under `/app/assistant/` via nginx. Shell wraps the page with the platform nav. Cons: style override complexity, limited context injection.

**Option C — API-only integration (headless Open WebUI)**: Use Open WebUI's backend (Python/FastAPI) as the conversation engine but build a custom React frontend for the `/app/assistant` page that calls the Open WebUI API. The drawer also calls the same API. Full platform UX control. More frontend work but clean integration.

For each option, assess:
- Context injection: how does the current company/deal page pass context to the Open WebUI session? (This is a hard requirement from zo-assistant-architecture.md Section 2.1)
- Shared history between `/app/assistant` page and the ZO right-panel drawer
- Effort estimate (rough: low/medium/high)
- UX fidelity (platform look/feel, header/footer/nav preserved)

### 3. Agent trigger compatibility
Open WebUI's chat completions API uses OpenAI-compatible format. Assess whether the ZO agent trigger model (analysis-swarm, comps pipeline — zo-assistant-architecture.md Section 2.3) can be wired via Open WebUI's tool/function calling, or requires a custom middleware layer.

### 4. Impact on TKT-0045 and TKT-0046
- Does Open WebUI replace the TKT-0045 custom FastAPI backend entirely, partially, or not at all?
- What custom extension layer (if any) is still needed on top of Open WebUI? (Source attribution, agent triggers, context injection, ZO permission model)
- Does TKT-0046 (CLI binary) still call a custom ZO API, or the Open WebUI API directly?

### 5. Recommendation
One of:
- **Adopt**: recommend embedding approach (Option A/B/C) + scope of custom extension layer needed
- **Adopt partially**: Open WebUI as backend only (Option C) — specify what custom layer wraps it
- **Do not adopt**: custom build as originally planned in zo-assistant-architecture.md — explain why

## Execution Notes

- This is a read/research/design ticket. Do not modify any live service or write any implementation code.
- Read zo-assistant-architecture.md in full before starting (it is long — all sections matter).
- Open WebUI repo: https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui — read the docs, API reference, and Docker deployment instructions.
- Do not assume Open WebUI's API is OpenAI-compatible in all ways — verify.
- The hard requirements from zo-assistant-architecture.md that must be preserved in any adoption path: (a) read+trigger permission model, (b) source attribution taxonomy, (c) context-awareness (company/deal page state passed to ZO), (d) Keycloak auth.
- Produce the evaluation doc in `01_Repos/movecapital-platform/docs/design/open-webui-zo-evaluation.md`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] Evaluation doc created at `01_Repos/movecapital-platform/docs/design/open-webui-zo-evaluation.md`
- [x] All 5 sections covered (capability audit, embedding options, agent trigger compatibility, TKT-0045/0046 impact, recommendation)
- [x] A single clear recommendation (adopt / adopt partially / do not adopt) with rationale
- [x] Embedding option recommendation (A, B, or C) if adoption is recommended
- [x] Custom extension layer scope documented if partial adoption recommended
- [x] TKT-0060 ready to proceed based on this output

## Handoff Log
- 2026-04-08 | COO | Ticket created from Enzo evaluation session. Open WebUI proposed by Enzo as ZO assistant main page, embedded in platform shell. ZO drawer to share chat history with main page. TKT-0060 will amend ZO architecture and re-scope TKT-0045 based on this output. | Status: OPEN | Next: assign to Frontend Developer + AI Engineer | Pitfall: do not conflate the drawer (right panel, context-aware, small) with the main assistant page (full content area, feature-rich). Both must be designed.
- 2026-04-08 | Claude Code (COO session) | Evaluation complete. Recommendation: Adopt partially — Option C (API-only / headless Open WebUI). KEY FINDINGS: (1) Open WebUI covers conversation engine, model routing (Claude/Ollama/OpenRouter), history persistence, and Keycloak OIDC auth natively — confirmed from source code. (2) X-Frame-Options is opt-in (XFRAME_OPTIONS env var not set by default) — iframe feasible but Option C preferred for UX fidelity. (3) Agent trigger compatibility: recommend ZO Middleware layer (thin FastAPI ~300–500 LOC) between React frontend and Open WebUI — keeps Move Capital business logic out of Open WebUI pipelines. (4) TKT-0045 scope reduces ~60–70% — full conversation backend replaced by Open WebUI config; custom work is middleware only. (5) TKT-0046 (CLI) unchanged in scope. (6) 3 open questions for Enzo surfaced (OQ-A/B/C in eval doc). (7) DISCOVERED: new ticket needed for /app/assistant full-page React component — not yet in scope of any ticket. | Eval doc at 01_Repos/movecapital-platform/docs/design/open-webui-zo-evaluation.md. All 6 acceptance criteria met. | TKT-0060 can now proceed to amend ZO architecture and re-scope TKT-0045/0046. | Open WebUI ref clone left at 99_Archive/open-webui-ref — delete after TKT-0060 consults it, or keep as reference.

archived.title: Amend ZO architecture doc + re-scope TKT-0045/TKT-0046 based on Open WebUI adoption decision
archived.created: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)

archived.body:
## Context

TKT-0059 produces an evaluation and recommendation on whether to embed Open WebUI as the ZO assistant main page in movecapital-platform. This ticket applies that decision to the canonical ZO architecture doc and re-scopes the two Phase 6 implementation tickets that depend on it. ZO is defined in `01_Repos/movecapital-platform/docs/design/zo-assistant-architecture.md`. TKT-0045 builds the ZO FastAPI backend; TKT-0046 builds the CLI binary. Both may change significantly depending on whether Open WebUI is adopted.

## Current State

- `zo-assistant-architecture.md` defines a custom-built ZO FastAPI backend (TKT-0045) and CLI binary (TKT-0046)
- TKT-0059 has not completed yet — this ticket is BLOCKED until it does
- TKT-0045 and TKT-0046 are OPEN but blocked on Phase 5 gate, so there is runway to amend them without disrupting in-progress work

## Task

After TKT-0059 evaluation doc is complete:

1. **If Open WebUI is adopted (fully or partially)**:
   - Add a new section to `zo-assistant-architecture.md`: "Open WebUI Adoption — Architecture Amendment"
   - Document: which ZO components Open WebUI replaces, what custom extension layer remains, embedding approach (Option A/B/C from TKT-0059)
   - Re-write the TKT-0045 task scope to reflect the new build target (configure + extend Open WebUI, not build from scratch)
   - Re-write the TKT-0046 task scope if the CLI target API changes (Open WebUI API vs. custom ZO API)
   - Update the section in zo-assistant-architecture.md (Section 5.3) that specifies the minimal ZO service architecture

2. **If Open WebUI is not adopted**:
   - Add a brief note to `zo-assistant-architecture.md` documenting the evaluation outcome and why custom build was retained
   - No scope changes to TKT-0045 or TKT-0046 needed
   - Mark this ticket DONE

3. In both cases: update `PROGRAMME-001-movecapital-platform.md` WS-ASSISTANT section to reflect the current ZO build approach

## Execution Notes

- Read TKT-0059's output doc (`open-webui-zo-evaluation.md`) in full before making any amendments
- Do not change the hard requirements in zo-assistant-architecture.md (read+trigger permission model, source attribution taxonomy, Keycloak auth) — these are non-negotiable regardless of adoption
- The TKT-0045 and TKT-0046 files are append-only per ticket rules — add a new section "AMENDMENT [date]" rather than rewriting history
- This ticket is documentation only. No implementation code.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] TKT-0045 amended to reflect correct Phase 6 build scope
- [ ] TKT-0046 amended if CLI target API changes
- [ ] PROGRAMME-001 WS-ASSISTANT section updated
- [ ] This ticket marked DONE and TKT-0045/TKT-0046 unblocked for Phase 6

## Handoff Log
- 2026-04-08 | COO | Ticket created. Blocked on TKT-0059 evaluation output. No action until TKT-0059 is DONE. | Status: BLOCKED | Next: activate when TKT-0059 completes.
- 2026-04-08 | COO (Claude Code) | All acceptance criteria met. Amendments applied: (1) zo-assistant-architecture.md Section 8 added — full amended architecture, ZO Middleware spec, auth flow, Custom Agent Library design, deployment dependencies. Section 5.3 superseded. (2) TKT-0045 AMENDMENT section added — scope reduced from full FastAPI backend to ZO Middleware ~300–500 LOC; Open WebUI replaces conversation engine/model routing/auth/history. (3) TKT-0046 AMENDMENT section added — scope confirmed unchanged; CLI calls ZO Middleware, Open WebUI transparent. (4) PROGRAMME-001 WS-ASSISTANT section updated with current architecture approach. | All 5 acceptance criteria met. TKT-0045 and TKT-0046 amended. PROGRAMME-001 updated. | TKT-0066 is now unblocked (was previously blocked on TKT-0060). TKT-0045 and TKT-0046 remain blocked on Phase 5 gate. COO to archive this ticket and unblock TKT-0066 in TICKETS.md. | Note for Phase 6 team: Custom Agent Library design is in zo-assistant-architecture.md Section 8.5 — read before implementing TKT-0068.

archived.title: Phase 7+ planning — Internal ops platform evaluation (HR, expenses, accounting, invoicing, e-sign, time off, appraisals)
archived.created: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.updated: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.body:
## Context

Enzo wants to self-host all internal operations tooling and surface it within the movecapital-platform as a Phase 7+ (post-Phase 6 gate) feature set. Scope includes: HR (employee profiles, contracts), time off management, expenses, accounting, invoicing, e-sign, and appraisals/reviews. The integration model envisioned is a dedicated section of the platform that opens a self-hosted ops tool (possibly as an embedded page or a new tab/button within the platform shell). Odoo Community Edition is the reference point but alternatives must be evaluated — the goal is the best self-hosted solution for a small investment team, not Odoo specifically. This is a planning-only ticket. Nothing is urgent — the work will begin when Phase 6 is complete, approximately 6+ months from 2026-04-08.

## Current State

- No internal ops tooling is deployed
- No evaluation has been done yet
- Odoo Community was identified as a candidate by Enzo
- Standalone e-sign tools (Documenso, DocuSeal) were flagged as potentially better than Odoo Sign
- This workstream does not block any current phase work

## Task

Produce an evaluation document at `01_Repos/movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/internal-ops-platform-evaluation.md` covering:

### 1. Capability requirements map
Document what each module must do for a small investment team (~5–15 people). The confirmed scope:
- **Time off**: requests, approval flow, leave balance tracking, calendar view
- **Expenses**: submission, approval, categorisation, export
- **HR — employee profiles**: contracts, documents, org chart, onboarding
- **Invoicing**: client invoice generation, status tracking, PDF export
- **Accounting**: chart of accounts, bank reconciliation, P&L, balance sheet
- **E-sign**: template-based contracts, signature requests, audit trail
- **Appraisals/reviews**: review cycles, peer feedback, goal tracking

### 2. Platform candidates — evaluate all of the following

**All-in-one ERP/ops suites:**

| Candidate | Notes to assess |
|---|---|
| **Odoo Community Edition** | Most comprehensive, free, Docker-hostable. Assess: self-hosting complexity, upgrade burden, module quality for each capability, Keycloak OIDC support |
| **ERPNext / Frappe HR** | Open source ERP, strong HR + payroll + accounting. Assess same dimensions. |
| **Dolibarr** | Lighter ERP. Good invoicing/accounting. Assess HR module quality. |

**Modular / best-of-breed (one tool per category):**

| Category | Candidates |
|---|---|
| HR + time off + expenses | **Plane** (project/HR), **OrangeHRM Community**, **Factorial** (SaaS only — exclude), **Sentrifugo** |
| E-sign | **Documenso** (modern, MIT, team-focused), **DocuSeal** (template-based, lightweight), **Papermark** (links + signatures) |
| Invoicing + accounting | **Invoice Ninja** (excellent invoicing, self-hosted), **Akaunting** (full accounting, open source) |
| Appraisals | Built into Odoo/ERPNext, or **Trakstar** (SaaS — exclude), assess if any open source standalone exists |

### 3. Integration model options

**Option A — Embedded iframe / proxied subpath**: Host the ops tool at `ops.movecapital.internal`; add a nav item in the platform shell that opens it embedded or in a new tab.

**Option B — Platform-native module**: Build thin platform pages for the most-used modules (time off, expenses) that call the ops tool's API. The full tool is accessible via link for power users.

**Option C — Standalone tool, linked only**: A button in the platform nav that opens the ops tool in a new browser tab. Minimal integration cost. Keycloak SSO makes the login seamless.

For each option: integration effort, UX coherence, maintenance overhead.

### 4. E-sign decision (standalone vs. bundled)

The e-sign capability warrants its own sub-section. Assess:
- **Documenso** vs **DocuSeal** vs **Odoo Sign** (if Odoo is adopted) on: UX quality, template support, audit trail robustness, API availability, Docker self-hosting complexity
- Recommendation: should e-sign be the same tool as the HR suite, or a dedicated standalone?

### 5. Recommendation

- All-in-one (one tool covers all modules) vs. modular (best tool per category)
- Specific tool recommendation(s) with rationale
- Integration model recommendation (A, B, or C)
- Keycloak SSO compatibility note for the chosen tool(s)
- Docker Compose resource estimate (RAM/CPU) for the chosen stack

## Execution Notes

- This is a design/research ticket. No implementation code. No live service changes.
- Enzo confirmed: all modules should go live at the same time when testing begins — no phased rollout. The evaluation should treat the full suite as one decision.
- Timeline: Phase 7+ (post-Phase 6 gate). There is no urgency — thoroughness is more valuable than speed here.
- Do not evaluate SaaS-only tools (Factorial, BambooHR, etc.) — self-hosted only.
- When assessing Docker hosting complexity, check for official Docker Compose examples in the project's repo.
- Keycloak OIDC support is a strong preference but not an absolute blocker — note if SSO requires a plugin or custom config.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Evaluation doc created at `01_Repos/movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/internal-ops-platform-evaluation.md`
- [ ] All 7 capability areas assessed for each candidate
- [ ] All-in-one vs. modular comparison complete
- [ ] E-sign sub-evaluation complete (Documenso vs DocuSeal vs bundled)
- [ ] Integration model recommendation (A, B, or C) with rationale
- [ ] Single final recommendation (tool choice + integration model) presented to Enzo for decision
- [ ] Follow-on implementation ticket(s) noted as next steps (but not created — Enzo must approve the recommendation first)

## Handoff Log
- 2026-04-08 | COO | Ticket created from Enzo evaluation session. Post-Phase 6, low urgency. Full evaluation of all-in-one ERP (Odoo, ERPNext, Dolibarr) and modular alternatives. E-sign evaluated separately. No implementation until Enzo approves the recommendation. | Status: OPEN | Priority: P3 — do not work until Phase 6 is underway | Next: assign to Product Manager + Backend Architect at Phase 6 gate.
- 2026-04-09 | COO | Executed planning task via Product Manager and Backend Architect. Evaluation document produced at 01_Repos/movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/internal-ops-platform-evaluation.md. Recommendation: ERPNext for core ops + Documenso for e-sign, integration Option C (Keycloak SSO linked). | Status: DONE | Next: Enzo to review recommendation.

archived.title: Evaluate and decide T418 observability stack — log management, infra metrics, and fleet monitoring
archived.created: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.updated: Thu Apr 09 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.body:
## Context

T418 runs the live gordon-stack (~10–15 containers) with no centralised log management, no infrastructure metrics, and no fleet/system-level monitoring beyond what Docker itself provides. Enzo has identified three gaps to address: (1) log management with error alerting and audit trails, (2) uptime monitoring (handled separately in TKT-0063 — Uptime Kuma), and (3) infrastructure/fleet monitoring for container and system-level health. This ticket evaluates and decides the tooling for gaps 1 and 3. TKT-0064 and TKT-0065 will deploy the chosen solutions. Grafana is not currently in the stack.

## Current State

- No centralised log management on T418
- No infrastructure metrics or fleet monitoring dashboard
- Uptime monitoring: being addressed separately in TKT-0063 (Uptime Kuma — decided)
- `gordon-stack` is the compose orchestration repo for T418 live services
- `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/` is the live runtime root — any new services must be added as gordon-stack sidecars

## Task

Produce a decision document at `01_Repos/gordon-stack/docs/observability-stack-decision.md` covering:

### 1. Log management — evaluate and decide

**Primary use case confirmed by Enzo**: error alerting + audit trails

**Candidates:**

| Tool | Stack weight | Strengths | Weaknesses |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Loki + Grafana + Promtail** | Light (2–3 containers) | Docker-native, Promtail auto-scrapes container logs, Grafana provides search + alerting + dashboards. If Prometheus is also adopted (see below), one Grafana instance covers everything. | Less powerful search UX than Graylog for pure log investigation |
| **Graylog Open Source** | Heavy (Graylog + MongoDB + OpenSearch = 3 services) | Excellent built-in search UX, alerting rules, audit trail features | High resource consumption; complex to maintain; OpenSearch is heavy for T418's scale |
| **OpenSearch Dashboards (standalone)** | Heavy | Good search | Overkill without Graylog |
| **Seq** | Medium | Excellent .NET/structured log UX | Not designed for Docker container log scraping; Windows-flavoured |

**Assess for each candidate:**
- Docker Compose resource estimate (RAM/CPU) on T418
- How container logs are ingested (auto-scrape vs. manual config per service)
- Alert rule capability (can it alert on error log patterns? Email/Telegram webhook?)
- Audit trail: can it retain logs for 90+ days with search?
- Effort to add a new service to the log pipeline

**Recommendation required**: single tool with rationale.

### 2. Infrastructure / fleet monitoring — evaluate and decide

**Primary use case confirmed by Enzo**: fleet management (container health, system resources — CPU, RAM, disk, network per service/host). Can be re-evaluated in future.

**Candidates:**

| Tool | Stack weight | Strengths | Weaknesses |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Prometheus + cAdvisor + node_exporter + Grafana** | Medium (4 components, but Grafana shared with logs if Loki chosen) | Industry standard; container + system metrics; Grafana dashboards; Alertmanager for alerts | Requires YAML config per scrape target; not zero-config |
| **Netdata** | Light (1 agent container) | Zero-config, real-time container + system metrics, excellent built-in UI, auto-detects Docker containers | Separate UI from logs if Grafana not used; cloud account for fleet aggregation (free tier available) |
| **Zabbix** | Heavy | Enterprise-grade fleet management | Over-engineered for <20 containers; complex agent setup; high admin overhead |
| **Checkmk Raw Edition** | Medium | Good fleet management, Docker-aware | Less Docker-native than Prometheus; UI requires learning |

**Assess for each candidate:**
- Auto-detection of Docker containers (zero-config vs. per-container setup)
- Container-level metrics: CPU, RAM, network I/O, restart count
- Host-level metrics: disk usage, load average
- Alert capability (disk full, container crash, memory pressure)
- Effort to add a new container to monitoring

**Recommendation required**: single tool with rationale.

### 3. Unified stack assessment

If Loki+Grafana is chosen for logs AND Prometheus is chosen for metrics: a single Grafana instance covers both. Assess whether this unified approach reduces total maintenance overhead vs. running two separate UIs. Include a combined Docker Compose resource estimate.

### 4. Final decision table

| Concern | Chosen tool | Rationale (1 sentence) | Containers added to gordon-stack |
|---|---|---|---|
| Log management | ? | ? | ? |
| Infra/fleet metrics | ? | ? | ? |
| Combined Grafana? | Yes/No | ? | ? |

## Execution Notes

- This is a research/design ticket. No implementation code. No changes to gordon-stack compose files.
- T418 is a Windows Server running Docker Desktop (Linux containers). Factor this into resource estimates.
- `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/` is the live runtime — new observability services must be added as gordon-stack sidecars with their own compose service entries and data volumes in `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/`.
- Do not add new services to the live compose without a separate change-controlled ticket (TKT-0064 / TKT-0065).
- Uptime Kuma is already decided (TKT-0063) — do not re-evaluate it here.
- Alerting channel: Telegram is confirmed as the primary notification channel.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] Decision doc created at `01_Repos/gordon-stack/docs/observability-stack-decision.md`
- [x] Log management tool evaluated (Loki+Grafana vs Graylog vs others) with clear recommendation
- [x] Fleet monitoring tool evaluated (Prometheus+Grafana vs Netdata vs Zabbix vs Checkmk) with clear recommendation
- [x] Unified stack assessment complete (shared Grafana or separate UIs)
- [x] Final decision table populated
- [x] Docker Compose resource estimates provided for chosen stack
- [x] TKT-0064 and TKT-0065 can be started from this output

## Handoff Log
- 2026-04-08 | COO | Ticket created from Enzo evaluation session. Log management (error alerting + audit trails) and fleet monitoring are the two needs. Uptime Kuma already decided (TKT-0063). No Grafana currently in stack. Telegram is the alert channel. | Status: OPEN | Next: assign to Backend Architect. Can start immediately — research only. | Pitfall: do not touch gordon-stack compose files in this ticket.
- 2026-04-09 | Engineering Core (Backend Architect) | Decision doc created at gordon-stack/docs/observability-stack-decision.md. Chosen: Loki+Grafana+Promtail for logs, Prometheus+cAdvisor+node_exporter for metrics, shared Grafana. All four candidates per section evaluated with RAM estimates, ingestion model, alert capability, retention, and new-service effort. Compose snippet preview included (reference only — not for deployment). | Decision doc at 01_Repos/gordon-stack/docs/observability-stack-decision.md. | TKT-0064 and TKT-0065 can begin. TKT-0065 depends on TKT-0064 (Grafana must exist first). | Pitfalls: T418 runs Docker Desktop on Windows — /sys, /proc, and /var/lib/docker/containers mounts resolve inside the Docker Desktop Linux VM, not the Windows host. cAdvisor and node_exporter metrics coverage may be partial. Must validate in TKT-0065 before declaring metrics complete. Do not expose Grafana externally without Keycloak SSO (deferred to future ticket).

archived.title: Design and decide T418 live service registry — authoritative port/service map with health monitoring
archived.created: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.updated: Thu Apr 09 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.body:
## Context

T418 runs a growing number of services (gordon-stack sidecars, RAG APIs, auth services, n8n, CRM tools, ZO backend etc.) each on a specific port. There is currently no single authoritative register of which service runs on which port — this information lives only in `docker-compose.yml` files and tribal knowledge. As more services are added (Open WebUI, fundraise-intel, podcast-rag-api, ZO Middleware, Uptime Kuma, etc.), port conflicts become a real risk and onboarding/debugging becomes harder.

Enzo raised this during TKT-0059 when the Open WebUI port was unknown. The requirement: a **live** registry (not a static doc) that shows what is running, what port it is on, and whether it is healthy — ideally feeding into the observability stack (Grafana/Prometheus/Uptime Kuma). This ticket produces the design decision and the static foundation; the live layer feeds into TKT-0062 (observability stack evaluation).

## Current State

- No port registry exists. Port assignments are discoverable only by reading docker-compose files in `01_Repos/gordon-stack/`.
- TKT-0062 (T418 observability evaluation) is OPEN — it will decide the Grafana/Prometheus/metrics stack.
- TKT-0063 (Uptime Kuma) is OPEN — Uptime Kuma monitors service uptime and can serve as a partial live registry.
- No standardised naming convention or port allocation scheme is documented.

## Task

### 1. Audit current port assignments

Read all `docker-compose*.yml` files in `01_Repos/gordon-stack/` and any other relevant configs. Produce a complete list of all services and their current host-side port mappings.

### 2. Identify conflicts and gaps

Note any port conflicts (two services mapped to the same host port) and any undocumented services.

### 3. Propose a port allocation scheme

Suggest a logical port banding for T418 services — e.g.:
- 3000–3099: Frontend/UI services
- 3100–3199: Platform app layer
- 5000–5099: Internal API services
- 8000–8099: AI/RAG services
- 8080–8089: Third-party embedded tools
- 9000–9099: Monitoring/observability

Allocate Open WebUI a specific port in the scheme (resolving Enzo's OQ-C from TKT-0059).

### 4. Produce the static registry

Write `01_Repos/gordon-stack/docs/service-port-registry.md` — a table of all T418 services with: service name, port, protocol, purpose, health check URL, compose file reference.

This is the static foundation. The live layer is in TKT-0062 (Prometheus service discovery or Grafana dashboards) and TKT-0063 (Uptime Kuma monitors using this registry as input).

### 5. Recommend the live monitoring integration

State how TKT-0062 and TKT-0063 should consume this registry:
- Uptime Kuma: import service URLs from this doc
- Prometheus: if adopted in TKT-0062, recommend a scrape config that picks up these services
- Grafana: recommend a services overview panel sourced from the registry

## Execution Notes

- Read-only audit + design doc. No live service changes.
- The static `service-port-registry.md` is a prerequisite for TKT-0063 (Uptime Kuma config) — it defines what to monitor.
- Add Open WebUI's assigned port to TKT-0059's eval doc (OQ-C) and to TKT-0066 (Open WebUI deploy) once decided.
- Do not assign a port that is already in use by a live service.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] All current T418 services and their ports documented in `service-port-registry.md`
- [x] Port allocation scheme proposed (banding by service type)
- [x] Open WebUI assigned a port (resolves TKT-0059 OQ-C) — assigned port 8085
- [x] Any port conflicts identified and flagged — no live host-port conflicts found
- [x] Recommendation written for how TKT-0062 and TKT-0063 consume the registry
- [x] `service-port-registry.md` committed to gordon-stack repo (commit 0df2034)

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-08 | COO | Ticket created from Enzo's observation during TKT-0059 Q&A — no port register exists, and the Open WebUI port was unknown. Enzo's requirement: live registry, not static. This ticket produces the static foundation; the live integration is TKT-0062 + TKT-0063. Immediately startable — no blockers. | Status: OPEN | Next: audit gordon-stack compose files and write service-port-registry.md | Do not change any live service ports — audit and document only.
- 2026-04-09 | Engineering Core (Backend Architect) | Audited all compose files: gordon-stack/docker-compose.yml, docker-compose.n8n-git-sync.yml, docker-compose.metadata-gen.yml, movecapital-platform/docker-compose-ui.yml, movecapital-platform/docker-compose.auth.yml, deal-rag/docker-compose.yml, transcript-rag-api/docker-compose.yml, n8n-core-stack/docker-compose.yml. Found 18 services across live and planned. Produced port allocation banding scheme (ingress/80+443, UI/3000-3099, platform/3100-3199, monitoring/3001+9000-9099, AI-RAG/8000-8009, deal-pipeline/8010-8019, third-party-tools/8080-8089, infra/11434+6333+5678, data/5432+54321-54325+7700). No live host-port conflicts found. Keycloak owns 8080 (host); comps-pipeline-worker maps to 8084 (host); ports noted as clean. | service-port-registry.md created at 01_Repos/gordon-stack/docs/service-port-registry.md, committed as 0df2034 | Open WebUI assigned port 8085 — resolves TKT-0059 OQ-C; fundraise-intel reserved 8010 | No port conflicts found on host side | Status: DONE | Follow-up: update TKT-0059 eval doc (OQ-C) and TKT-0066 infra config with port 8085; TKT-0062 and TKT-0063 can now use registry as input

archived.title: Design and evaluate T418 central secrets vault — tool selection, env file integration, per-service and per-user token management
archived.created: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.updated: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.body:
## Context

All T418 secrets (API keys, database passwords, service tokens, TLS material) currently live in `.env` files under `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/`. There is no central vault, no rotation capability, no audit trail, no per-user token management, and no mechanism to create new secrets programmatically. As the platform grows (ZO Middleware needs API keys per user, new services like podcast-rag-api need auth tokens, deal-rag and transcript-rag-api need per-user dedicated auth), this flat-file approach becomes a security and operational liability.

Enzo raised this during TKT-0059: "we would need to have dedicated auth for each user" (for RAG API access). This is impossible to manage safely with flat `.env` files. A central secrets vault is the prerequisite for per-user token issuance, secret rotation, and audit trails.

This ticket produces a design decision: which tool, how it integrates with existing env files, and how it handles per-user token creation. This is T418 infrastructure — not in any specific programme.

## Current State

- Secrets: flat `.env` files in `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/` — no audit, no rotation, no programmatic creation
- No vault tool deployed on T418 or in gordon-stack
- Services currently use shared secrets (one API key per service, shared by all users/callers)
- Per-user API tokens for deal-rag, transcript-rag-api, ZO: not implemented anywhere
- Auth services in scope: Keycloak (TKT-0042), Supabase Auth (TKT-0036 DONE), GoTrue OIDC (TKT-0053 OPEN)

## Task

Produce a design decision document at `01_Repos/gordon-stack/docs/secrets-vault-design.md` covering:

### 1. Tool evaluation

Evaluate the following candidates and recommend one:

| Tool | Notes |
|---|---|
| **HashiCorp Vault (OSS)** | Full-featured, complex to operate, industry standard. KV secrets, dynamic secrets, token issuance, audit log. |
| **Infisical** | Modern open-source secrets manager. Git-native sync, env file injection, SDKs, Docker-native. Lighter than Vault. |
| **Doppler** | SaaS-first secrets manager. Has a self-hosted option. Good CLI/env injection UX. Less control than self-hosted. |
| **Bitwarden Secrets Manager** | Open-source self-hosted. Good for team secrets, machine identity support. |
| **Simple: age-encrypted secrets in git** | Encrypt secret files with `age`, commit ciphertext, decrypt on deploy. No server required but no rotation/dynamic tokens. |

Evaluation criteria:
- Self-hostable on T418 (no SaaS dependency for secrets in production)
- Env file injection mechanism (how do existing `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/.env` files get secrets from the vault?)
- Per-user token issuance (can it create a new short-lived API token for a specific user on demand?)
- Dynamic secrets (can it issue ephemeral credentials for Supabase, Keycloak?)
- Operational overhead (how hard is it to operate on a single-server setup?)
- Docker-native (runs as a gordon-stack sidecar)
- Audit log (who accessed which secret, when)

### 2. Env file integration model

The core operational question: T418 services currently read secrets from `.env` files at startup. How does the vault connect to this?

Evaluate two models:
- **Model A — Vault as source, env files are generated**: A startup script or Vault agent reads secrets from the vault and writes `.env` files to `02_Runtime/gordon-prod/` at deploy time. Services still start from `.env` files — no code change required.
- **Model B — Vault as runtime source**: Services fetch secrets from the vault directly at startup (via SDK or env injection sidecar). `.env` files are replaced or supplemented. Requires service-level code changes or a process wrapper.

Recommend one model with rationale. For T418's existing services (docker-compose, no SDK integration), Model A is likely the pragmatic choice for migration.

### 3. Per-user token management design

The immediate trigger for this ticket: ZO and the RAG APIs need per-user auth tokens (one token per platform user per service) rather than shared API keys.

Design the token lifecycle:
- **Issuance**: when does a user get a token? (first login, on-demand, on platform onboarding)
- **Storage**: where is the token stored? (vault, Supabase `user_tokens` table, or browser-side only)
- **Scope**: what does each token grant? (read-only on deal-rag, read+trigger on ZO Middleware, etc.)
- **Rotation**: how often do tokens rotate? Who triggers rotation?
- **Revocation**: can a token be revoked immediately? (e.g., if a team member leaves)

Note: per-user tokens for RAG services require auth middleware to be added to deal-rag and transcript-rag-api — this is a separate implementation ticket (not in scope here, but note it as a DISCOVERED follow-on).

### 4. Secret categories on T418

Produce a categorisation of all current and planned secrets by sensitivity and access pattern:

| Category | Examples | Rotation frequency | Per-user? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Infrastructure secrets | Supabase DB password, postgres root creds | Rare | No |
| Service API keys | Anthropic API key, OpenRouter key | Periodic | No |
| External integrations | Attio API token, n8n webhook secrets | Periodic | No |
| Internal service tokens | ZO → deal-rag auth, platform → ZO Middleware auth | Frequent | No (service-to-service) |
| Per-user platform tokens | User → ZO Middleware, User → deal-rag | On rotation schedule | Yes |
| TLS material | Certificates, private keys | Annually | No |

### 5. Recommendation

One tool + one env integration model + a phased migration path:
- Phase 1: vault deployed, existing secrets migrated from `.env` files to vault, Model A env generation at deploy time
- Phase 2: per-user token issuance implemented for ZO Middleware and RAG APIs
- Phase 3: dynamic secrets for Supabase and Keycloak (if applicable)

## Execution Notes

- Design and planning only. No vault deployment in this ticket.
- Do not print, echo, or include any existing `.env` contents or secret values in this document.
- The design must account for T418 being a single-server setup — not a Kubernetes cluster. Operational simplicity is a hard constraint.
- Self-hosted is a hard constraint — no production secrets should depend on a third-party SaaS.
- The per-user token design must be compatible with the Keycloak auth model (TKT-0042) and GoTrue OIDC federation (TKT-0053) — users authenticate via Keycloak; per-user service tokens should ideally be derived from their Keycloak identity, not a separate credential.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] Tool evaluated against all criteria; single recommendation with rationale
- [x] Env file integration model chosen (A or B) with rationale
- [x] Per-user token lifecycle designed (issuance, storage, scope, rotation, revocation)
- [x] Secret categories documented
- [x] Phased migration path written
- [x] Design doc at `01_Repos/gordon-stack/docs/secrets-vault-design.md`
- [x] Follow-on implementation ticket created (vault deployment) — TKT-0092
- [x] Follow-on implementation ticket created (per-user token auth middleware for deal-rag + transcript-rag-api) — TKT-0093

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-08 | COO | Ticket created from Enzo's observation during TKT-0059 Q&A — current flat .env approach has no rotation, no per-user token management, and no audit trail. Immediate trigger: ZO and RAG APIs will need per-user tokens as the platform moves to multi-user. Self-hosted constraint is hard. Immediately startable — no blockers. | Status: OPEN | Next: evaluate vault tools, produce design doc. | Do not expose any existing secret values in the output doc. Per-user token design must align with Keycloak identity (TKT-0042 + TKT-0053).
- 2026-04-09 | Security Engineer + Backend Architect | Produced 501-line design doc at 01_Repos/gordon-stack/docs/secrets-vault-design.md. Tool selected: Infisical (self-hosted). Env integration: Model A (vault agent writes .env files at deploy time — no service code changes needed). Per-user token lifecycle: issued on first platform login via Keycloak OIDC hook, stored in Supabase user_tokens table, scoped per service, 30-day rotation, immediate revocation via Supabase + Infisical. 6 secret categories documented. Phased migration: Phase 1 vault deploy + .env migration, Phase 2 per-user tokens, Phase 3 dynamic creds. Created TKT-0092 (vault deployment) and TKT-0093 (per-user auth middleware). gordon-stack is local-only (no remote) — doc committed locally only. | DONE. No secrets exposed in doc. Design compatible with TKT-0042 (Keycloak) and TKT-0053 (GoTrue OIDC). | Next: TKT-0092 (vault deployment) is unblocked and startable.

archived.title: Evaluate and deploy Whisper speech-to-text for ZO voice input — French/English, T418 compute budget
archived.created: Wed Apr 08 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.updated: Fri Apr 10 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.body:
## Context

Open WebUI supports voice/speech-to-text input (Feature O). Enzo's decision (2026-04-08): include voice input in ZO, but DEFERRED — only activate once a Whisper model that meets two hard constraints is available:
1. **Language quality**: good enough accuracy for both French and English (Move Capital team is bilingual)
2. **Compute safety**: runs on T418's backend server without consuming excessive compute capacity (T418 is a shared server running the full gordon-stack)

Voice input is explicitly excluded from ZO V1. It is a Phase 6+ feature gated on this evaluation completing and a model being selected.

## Current State

- Open WebUI has built-in Whisper integration (connects to a local or remote Whisper API endpoint)
- Whisper models available: tiny, base, small, medium, large, large-v2, large-v3, turbo
- French ASR quality varies significantly across model sizes
- T418 hardware specs: unknown from this ticket — verify before recommending (need CPU/GPU/RAM spec to assess compute budget)
- No Whisper service is currently deployed on T418
- `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZO_VOICE_ENABLED=false` should be the default flag in movecapital-platform until this ticket is DONE and approved

## Task

Produce an evaluation document at `01_Repos/gordon-stack/docs/whisper-evaluation.md` covering:

### 1. T418 hardware audit

Read T418's actual hardware spec:
- CPU (cores, model)
- RAM total and available headroom after current gordon-stack services
- GPU presence (if any — if no GPU, Whisper runs on CPU; this significantly affects model choice)
- Disk I/O (model loading time)

### 2. Whisper model shortlist

For each candidate model, assess:

| Model | Size | French WER | English WER | CPU inference time (30s audio) | RAM at inference | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `whisper-small` | 244MB | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD |
| `whisper-medium` | 769MB | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD |
| `whisper-large-v3-turbo` | 809MB | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD |
| `faster-whisper-medium` | 769MB | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD |
| `faster-whisper-large-v3` | ~1.5GB | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD | TBD |

Note: `faster-whisper` (CTranslate2-based) runs significantly faster on CPU than OpenAI's original Whisper — evaluate it alongside the originals.

Gather benchmark data from published evaluations (Common Voice FR, LibriSpeech EN) and T418 spot-tests if hardware audit allows.

### 3. Compute impact assessment

For the recommended model, estimate:
- Peak RAM during inference (during a 60s voice message)
- CPU spike: what % of T418 CPU cores are consumed during inference?
- Can inference run concurrently with existing gordon-stack services without causing latency spikes on live services (deal-rag, transcript-rag-api)?
- Recommended inference isolation: same container as Open WebUI, separate Whisper sidecar, or remote API call to a dedicated Whisper endpoint?

### 4. Deployment design

How should Whisper be deployed on T418 for use with Open WebUI?

Options:
- **A — Open WebUI built-in**: Open WebUI has a Whisper integration via `WHISPER_MODEL` env var — downloads and runs locally. Simple but shares compute with Open WebUI.
- **B — Faster-whisper sidecar**: Separate Docker container running faster-whisper as an HTTP API (e.g., `faster-whisper-server`). Open WebUI calls it as an external STT endpoint.
- **C — Remote Whisper API**: Use a remote API (e.g., Groq Whisper API) for transcription. Fast, zero T418 compute, but adds a SaaS dependency and data leaves T418.

Recommend one option with rationale. Note: C conflicts with the self-hosted principle for sensitive content (call recordings, deal discussions).

### 5. Go/no-go recommendation

Final decision: which model + which deployment option, or a clear "not ready — re-evaluate at [date/condition]"?

If recommending go:
- Specific model name and size
- Docker sidecar compose spec (or Open WebUI env vars)
- Estimated T418 headroom remaining after deployment

If recommending no-go:
- Specific blocker (French WER too low / compute too high)
- Re-evaluation trigger (e.g., "re-evaluate when large-v3-turbo CPU benchmarks improve" or "re-evaluate if T418 gets a GPU")

## Execution Notes

- This is a research/evaluation ticket. No deployment in this ticket — deployment is a follow-on ticket if go-go.
- Read T418's hardware spec from `02_Runtime/` or by running `lscpu`, `free -h`, `nvidia-smi` on T418.
- Do not benchmark on T418 while live services are under load — run any spot tests during low-traffic periods.
- The `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZO_VOICE_ENABLED` flag in movecapital-platform stays `false` until this ticket is DONE and Enzo approves the go-ahead.
- French language quality is a hard requirement — do not recommend a model that has significantly worse French WER than English WER unless the gap is marginal.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] T418 hardware spec documented
- [ ] Whisper model shortlist evaluated (French WER, English WER, CPU inference time, RAM)
- [ ] Compute impact assessed for recommended model
- [ ] Deployment option recommended (A / B / C)
- [ ] Clear go/no-go decision with rationale
- [ ] Evaluation doc at `01_Repos/gordon-stack/docs/whisper-evaluation.md`
- [ ] If go: follow-on deployment ticket created

## Handoff Log

- 2026-04-08 | COO | Ticket created from Enzo's ZO feature review (2026-04-08). Voice input approved in principle, deferred until a French/English bilingual Whisper model can run on T418 without harming compute headroom. P3 priority — do not work until ZO V1 core is live. `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZO_VOICE_ENABLED=false` is the default. | Status: OPEN | Next: T418 hardware audit first, then model benchmarks. | French quality is a hard gate — do not ship voice if French WER is poor.
- 2026-04-10 | AI Engineer + Backend Architect | Completed full evaluation. T418 hardware audited: Intel i5-8350U (4 cores / 8 threads), 15.85 GB physical RAM, WSL2 ceiling 7.676 GiB, Intel UHD 620 integrated GPU only — no NVIDIA/CUDA. Current Docker footprint ~4.15 GiB, leaving ~3.53 GiB headroom within WSL2 budget. Model shortlist evaluated: whisper-small FAILS French gate (WER ~22–25%); whisper-medium marginal (WER 13–15% but CPU latency 90–120s); faster-whisper-medium RECOMMENDED (WER ~13–15% FR / ~3.0% EN, CPU inference 18–28s for 30s audio, ~900 MiB RAM). Deployment option B (faster-whisper-server sidecar) selected over option A (Open WebUI built-in) for compute isolation. GO decision: faster-whisper-medium + Option B sidecar with MAX_CONCURRENT_REQUESTS=1. `NEXT_PUBLIC_ZO_VOICE_ENABLED` stays false until follow-on deployment ticket completes. Evaluation doc written at `01_Repos/gordon-stack/docs/whisper-evaluation.md`. | Status: DONE | Next: COO to create TKT-0107 (deployment ticket) if Enzo approves go-ahead. Enzo must review evaluation doc and give explicit go-ahead before TKT-0107 is actioned. | DISCOVERED: Follow-on deployment ticket TKT-0107 required — flagged for COO to create. Not created in this ticket per scope rules.
