archived.title: Design and scaffold ZO CLI — paperclip-like local agent runner
archived.created: Wed May 27 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.updated: Wed May 27 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.body:
## Context

Phase 3 of PROG-ZAF-001. The end goal of the framework is a local "terminal overlay" system that manages and orchestrates AI agents inside their sovereign, native CLI harnesses (like `@anthropic-ai/claude-code`, Gemini CLI, or Codex), letting them run at maximum efficiency with their native commands (e.g., `/goal`, `/dreams`) while ZAF wraps their context, state, and ticket hygiene.

This ticket covers analyzing Paperclip's heartbeat and adapters, designing the ZAF terminal subshell harness wrapper, structuring the ZAF system prompts/plugins library, and scaffolding the local CLI and app integrations.

## Task

1.  **Analyze Paperclip local adapters & heartbeat**:
    *   Study `C:\Users\LENOVO\Workspace\01_Repos\paperclip\server\src\services\heartbeat.ts` to extract state recovery and cron loops.
2.  **Define ZAF Subprocess Harness Contract**:
    *   Design how `zo run <agent-role> --ticket <TKT-ID>` spawns native CLIs (e.g., Claude Code, Codex, Gemini) in a controlled subshell with stdin/stdout piping.
3.  **Design ZAF Prompt & Plugin Library**:
    *   Structure standard prompts (e.g., `.zaf-skill.md`) injected into spawned terminal harnesses to enforce strict ticket schema updates, handoff logs, and token budgeting rules.
4.  **Sketch Desktop App & Extension Wrapper**:
    *   Design Tauri desktop layout (HTML/CSS visualizer compiled into a native desktop utility).
    *   Draft a VSCode/Antigravity extension architecture to embed ZAF active panels and terminals directly into the IDE's sidebar.
5.  **Scaffold local CLI**:
    *   Create directory `cli/` and entry point `cli/zo.js`.
    *   Implement initial commands: `zo ticket create <title>`, `zo ticket status <TKT-ID>`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] Completed Paperclip local adapter design review document.
- [x] ZAF subprocess runner specs defined, detailing stdin/stdout harness overlays.
- [x] ZAF standard prompt guide (`.zaf-skill.md`) drafted.
- [x] Tauri configurations scaffolded under `app/` to package the dashboard.
- [x] `cli/zo.js` created and executing `zo ticket status TKT-ZAF-0001` with correct local Markdown parse.

## Handoff Log

- 2026-05-27 | antigravity-ide | Ticket created. Blocked on TKT-ZAF-0003. Design-only until Phase 2 gate closes. No implementation until agent taxonomy and COO spec are stable.
- 2026-05-27 | antigravity-ide | Phase 2 gate closed (TKT-ZAF-0003 is DONE). Ticket unblocked.
- 2026-05-27 | antigravity-ide | IN_PROGRESS. Cloned local Paperclip repository for deep study of local adapters and heartbeat scheduler. Wrote private manifest (`WIP/zaf-manifest.md`) establishing the sovereign CLI harness principle. Refining CLI tasks.
- 2026-05-27 | antigravity-ide | DONE. Wrote all four canonical architecture design documents: `paperclip-analysis.md`, `app-design.md`, `extension-design.md`, and `cli-design.md`. Fully mapped CLI parameters, subprocess stdin/stdout overrides, and prompt/plugin harnesses. Cleanly staged, committed, and pushed successfully to GitHub `main`!

archived.created: Wed May 27 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)

archived.body:
## Context

Phase 3 implementation of PROG-ZAF-001. Now that Phase 3 (CLI Integration) is active, we need to implement the ZAF CLI executable (`cli/zo.js`) and build the subshell runner contract capable of spawning local, sovereign LLM harnesses (like `@anthropic-ai/claude-code`, Gemini CLI, or Codex) inside multiplexed subprocesses.

## Task

1.  **Scaffold CLI structure**:
    *   Create directory `cli/` and entry point `cli/zo.js`.
    *   Configure `package.json` bin scripts so `zo` can be run globally or via `npm run`.
2.  **Implement CLI Commands**:
    *   `zo ticket status <TKT-ID>`: Reads active ticket front-matter YAML and prints formatted status block to stdout.
    *   `zo ticket create "<title>"`: Scaffolds a new ticket Markdown file, increments the ID in TICKETS.md, and auto-injects it into indices.
3.  **Implement Subprocess Harness Runner**:
    *   Build standard Node.js subprocess launcher using `spawn`.
    *   Implement piped streams (`inherit` stdin to preserve native slash commands, `pipe` stdout to scan for loop counts).
    *   Capture exit codes and clean up transient workspace skills on finish.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] `cli/zo.js` compiled and runnable.
- [x] Running `zo ticket status TKT-ZAF-0005` returns clean, formatted YAML metadata console outputs.
- [x] Running `zo ticket create "Test Task"` creates the markdown file and updates TICKETS.md.
- [x] Running `zo run engineering --ticket TKT-ZAF-0005` successfully spawns the local CLI harness under a ZAF overlay wrapper.

## Handoff Log

- 2026-05-27 | antigravity-ide | Ticket created. Ready for execution in Phase 3.
- 2026-05-27 | antigravity-ide | Bootstrapped Phase 3 and set ticket status to IN_PROGRESS. Prepared the /dream implementation prompt.
- 2026-05-27 | antigravity-ide | DONE — Scaffolded and compiled cli/zo.js, built zero-dependency front-matter and TICKETS.md index update logic, created subshell runner contract with turn budget and active sync hooks, set up root package.json export, initialized Tauri desktop app with custom Rust tray, and developed standard VSCode extension sidebar webview, terminal multiplexer, and active gutter decorations.

archived.title: Implement ZAF prompt harness & deterministic plugins library (.zaf-skill.md)
archived.created: Wed May 27 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.updated: Wed May 27 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.body:
## Context

Phase 3 implementation. To prevent sovereign CLI harnesses (e.g. Claude Code, Codex, Gemini) from hallucinating, modifying files outside their scope, or violating ticket standards, we must build ZAF's prompt engineering/skills library.

Before launching a harness subshell, ZAF will compile and inject a transient `.zaf-skill.md` blueprint instructing the assistant on how to behave with absolute determinism inside the ticket operating system.

## Task

1.  **Draft standard skill template**:
    *   Implement `.zaf-skill.md` builder class inside ZAF CLI libraries.
    *   The builder collects ticket ID, description parameters, role profiles (`docs/agent-taxonomy.md`), and mounts target directories.
2.  **Define Harness Prompts & Constraints**:
    *   Formulate strict behavior instructions (e.g., how to parse statuses, append logs, and restrict writes to directory boundaries).
    *   Implement turn-budget loops to auto-terminate loops crossing turn boundaries (e.g., 20 max steps).
3.  **Harness-Specific Profiles**:
    *   Inject custom prompts optimized for `@anthropic-ai/claude-code`, Codex, or Gemini-CLI formats depending on the active harness parameter.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] ZAF skill builder successfully generates a `.zaf-skill.md` file dynamically before launch.
- [x] Spawning Claude Code or Codex reads the local `.zaf-skill.md` file correctly on boot.
- [x] Agent harness successfully writes append-only handoff logs and changes status to `DONE` on disk.
- [x] Harness loops hitting the turn budget gracefully exit and save context.

## Handoff Log

- 2026-05-27 | antigravity-ide | Ticket created. Blocked by TKT-ZAF-0005.
- 2026-05-27 | antigravity-ide | DONE — Implemented dynamic role taxonomy parser from docs/agent-taxonomy.md, refined directory mounts under .zaf-skill.md, built automated test runner, and validated turn-budget exit loops.

archived.title: Scaffold ZAF Desktop App using Tauri (Tauri core, sidecar Node server, system tray)
archived.created: Wed May 27 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.updated: Wed May 27 2026 02:00:00 GMT+0200 (heure d’été d’Europe centrale)
archived.body:
## Context

Phase 3 implementation. To make ZAF a premium, standalone local-first utility rather than a manual localhost terminal command, we will package the dashboard, watchers, and telemetry server into a native desktop app using Tauri.

Tauri compiles ZAF's frontend into a fast Webview panel, runs the Node server as a background sidecar process, and handles taskbar tray system APIs.

## Task

1.  **Initialize Tauri Stack**:
    *   Initialize Tauri inside `app/` directory (`npm create tauri-app@latest`).
    *   Configure `tauri.conf.json` dist paths to point to ZAF dashboard assets.
2.  **Pack Telemetry Server as Sidecar**:
    *   Package Node.js server (`server.js` and `parse.js`) as a Tauri sidecar binary.
    *   Implement sidecar lifecycle management in `main.rs` (spawning server, handling crashes, shutting down child processes on exit).
3.  **Implement System Tray Utility**:
    *   Design and build Rust-based Windows system tray taskbar interface.
    *   Implement tray menus: Show Dashboard, Trigger Sweep, Restart Telemetry, Settings, Exit.
    *   Configure minimize-to-tray window events.
4.  **Integrate OS Notifications**:
    *   Bind Tauri system notifications API to prompt the desktop when tickets update, dependencies resolve, or model incidents occur.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] Tauri app compiled successfully into a native Windows executable (`.exe` or installer).
- [x] Running the app automatically spins up the background sidecar server.
- [x] ZAF control icon resides in the Windows system tray taskbar.
- [x] Native Windows OS notifications successfully pop up on ticket status updates.

## Handoff Log

- 2026-05-27 | antigravity-ide | Ticket created. Blocked by TKT-ZAF-0006.
- 2026-05-27 | antigravity-ide | DONE — Configured tauri-plugin-notification in Cargo.toml and initialized it in lib.rs, refined minimize-to-tray window events, built out tray menus with triggers for AST Parse Sweep and Telemetry restarts, integrated desktop OS notification alerts, and fully prepared Rust systems for T14 native compilations.

archived.body:
## Context

Phase 3 implementation. The ultimate developer control plane experience is achieved by embedding ZAF's tickets, telemetry, and subshell launchers directly into the IDE sidebar and code gutters, rather than switching context to external apps.

This extension runs inside standard VSCode and specialized forks (Antigravity/Cursor), managing terminal panels and active file locking visually.

## Task

1.  **Initialize VSCode Extension**:
    *   Create extension scaffold inside `extension/` directory.
    *   Map Sidebar Webview panels in `package.json` manifest.
2.  **Build Sidebar Kanban Webview Panel**:
    *   Embed a vertical Webview component in the Activity Bar showing ZAF Overview and Active Kanban columns.
    *   Add context-action buttons (`▶ Run`, `⚙ Config`, `✓ Done`).
3.  **Terminal Pane Multiplexing**:
    *   Bind extension to VSCode's Native Terminal API (`vscode.window.createTerminal`).
    *   Add ZAF runner launcher hooks: Clicking `▶ Run` automatically creates a terminal tab titled `ZAF-[Harness]-[Ticket]` and triggers `zo run`.
4.  **Editor Gutter Indicators**:
    *   Bind gutter decorators to line scopes.
    *   If a developer opens a code file mapped to an active ticket, render a visual tag/dot in the editor gutter.
    *   Add hover panels demonstrating current ticket status, priority, and blocker trees.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [x] Extension builds and compiles successfully.
- [x] ZAF sidebar panel displays correct vertically-stacked Kanban tickets.
- [x] Clicking `▶ Run` opens VSCode Terminal tab and triggers the sovereign CLI runner.
- [x] Gutter indicators render correctly next to line numbers on files linked to active tickets.

## Handoff Log

openQuestions.answer: **ANSWERED 2026-04-07**: No. employee_count has marginal value (blackbox metric). Drop it — do not replicate auto-enrichment in the migration.

openQuestions.answer: **ANSWERED 2026-04-07**: Not currently, but required for the final Attio handoff sync before decommission. Twenty connector must support list-scoped filtering.

programmes.body:
# PROGRAMME-001 — MoveCapital Platform: Attio-Free V1

**Owner**: Enzo (CEO)  
**COO**: Agents Orchestrator  
**Created**: 2026-04-07  
**Status**: ACTIVE — Phase 1 (Design Lock — WAITING on Figma V1 from Enzo)

---

## Programme Goal

Build a fully independent, self-hosted internal platform for the MoveCapital investment team. The platform must:

- Replace Attio as the canonical source of truth for companies, people, and deals
- Provide a unified product shell (one-stop shop) for the full investment workflow: research, sourcing, analysis, deal management, and relationship intelligence
- Be grounded in a final Figma-designed UX (V1), not the current scaffolded prototype
- Be executable by the agent team with controlled handoffs, no accidental breakage of live services

**Target state**: movecapital-platform running V1 UX, connected to a self-hosted stack (Twenty CRM + Vexa call capture + Supabase canonical data + Graphiti graph memory), with Attio fully decommissioned.

---

## Programme Non-Negotiables

1. **Attio stays live until the dual-import phase validates parity.** No early cutover.
2. **Figma design comes first.** No shell work that contradicts the V1 design direction.
3. **Live services on T418 are not touched without explicit change control.** See `ops/standards/runtime-change-control-checklist.md`.
4. **No new Attio coupling.** Do not add new Attio dependencies in any repo during this programme.
5. **Every workstream ticket must be self-contained.** Cold-start agents must be able to execute from the ticket alone.
6. **Hand off before compression.** Sessions must produce a Handoff Log entry before context grows unmanageable. See `ops/agents/HANDOFF-PROTOCOL.md`.
7. **All new vector-backed services use pgvector from day one — not Qdrant.** Decision confirmed 2026-04-08 (Enzo). Qdrant is being decommissioned (TKT-0050, TKT-0051, TKT-0052). Any new service requiring vector storage (podcast-rag-api, deal-analysis-api, ZO retrieval) must be built on Supabase pgvector. Do not start a new service on Qdrant while the migration is in flight.

---

## Repo Inventory

*Full audit completed 2026-04-07 (TKT-0003). All 29 repos in `01_Repos/` classified.*

### Programme-Relevant Repos (ACTIVE-CORE / ACTIVE-LIVE / ACTIVE-REFERENCE)

| Repo | Classification | Role | Github-backed | Archive trigger |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `movecapital-platform` | ACTIVE-CORE | Canonical product shell (Next.js, port 3100) | Yes | — |
| `project-ninpo` | ACTIVE-CORE | Company intelligence pipeline + canonical Supabase schema | Yes | — |
| `fundraise-intel` | ACTIVE-CORE | Sourcing signal ingestion; local MVP, not yet deployed | Yes | — |
| `Deal-analysis-swarm` | ACTIVE-CORE | Analysis packaging and memo generation; wired to platform | Yes | — |
| `deal-rag` | ACTIVE-LIVE | Deal doc ingestion, search, chunk retrieval — live on T418 | Yes | — |
| `transcript-rag-api` | ACTIVE-LIVE | Transcript search, call lists, ingest — live on T418 | Yes | — |
| `MoveCapital_CompsPipeline` | ACTIVE-LIVE | Comps execution worker — live on T418 (comps-pipeline-worker) | Yes | — |
| `n8n-git-sync` | ACTIVE-LIVE | n8n workflow git-sync — live runtime, do not touch without change control | Yes | — |
| `gordon-stack` | ACTIVE-LIVE | Orchestration repo for live stack — local-only, do not touch without change control | No | — |
| `MoveCapital_UX` | ACTIVE-REFERENCE | Earlier POC prototype — read-only source for component porting | Yes | Phase 2 gate (shell V1 complete) |
| `movecapital-platform-transition` | ACTIVE-REFERENCE | Transition dossier (Attio→Twenty+Vexa research) — docs only | Yes* | Phase 3 gate (merge into platform/docs/) |
| `attio-reference` | ACTIVE-REFERENCE | Attio data model + API reference + export analysis — docs only | Yes | Phase 5 gate (Attio decommissioned) |
| `Codex` | ACTIVE-REFERENCE | Agent org docs and workspace governance (this machine's operating guide) | Yes | — |

*Note (verified 2026-04-07, TKT-0015): `movecapital-platform-transition` is a second local clone of the same GitHub repo (`Nassau-1/movecapital-platform`). The remote URL is not wrong — it is the same repo, used for different branches. `movecapital-platform/` is on branch `ux-content-coherence-pass`. `movecapital-platform-transition/` is on branch `research/attio-twenty-vexa-transition`, a local-only branch (not pushed to GitHub) that tracks remote `ux-content-coherence-pass`. GitHub remote branches are: `main` and `ux-content-coherence-pass`. Do not push from T418 to either. At Phase 3 gate, the docs in this branch must be merged into `movecapital-platform/docs/` on T14, then the local transition directory can be archived.

### Dormant Repos (present, not in active use, potentially useful later)

| Repo | Notes |
|---|---|
| `deal-rag-v2` | ~~ARCHIVED 2026-04-07 (TKT-0014)~~ — local-only, no remote. Canonical versions of metadata-generator, unified-gpt confirmed in deal-rag. Directory deleted. |
| `docker-backup` | Backup scripts; operational but not in programme scope |
| `move-capital-professional-assistant` | Research/blueprint only (OpenClaw-based assistant); no implementation, no deployment |
| `n8n-core-stack` | Older/alternative n8n stack template; no recent relevant commits; superseded by `gordon-stack` |

### Out-of-Scope Repos (unrelated to PROGRAMME-001)

| Repo | Reason |
|---|---|
| `MoveCapital_DustReplacement` | Confirmed out of scope — Course Impact automation, no platform relevance. (TKT-0026 closed 2026-04-07) |
| `MoveCapital_IdeaOps` | Course Impact automation unrelated to platform |
| `MoveCapital_PowerTools` | Course Impact automation unrelated to platform |
| `Wiki` | Course Impact; personal/team wiki |
| `brother-codex-starter` | Course Impact automation |
| `chatgpt-markdown-exporter` | Personal utility tool |
| `enzoterrier-landing` | Personal landing page |
| `folder-to-merged-pdf` | Personal utility tool |
| `stop-on-battery` | Personal utility tool |
| `homepagectl` | Third-party (0xN1nja remote); personal utility |
| `OASE-Website` | External client website project |
| `Self-hosted-projects-ideas` | Ideas/research doc repo; no deployable code |

**Repos to retire or merge (with phase triggers):**
- `movecapital-platform-transition` → merge docs into `movecapital-platform/docs/` at Phase 3 gate; archive local dir after merge
- `MoveCapital_UX` → archive at Phase 2 gate (shell V1 complete, no longer sourcing from it)
- `attio-reference` → archive at Phase 5 gate (Attio fully decommissioned)
- `deal-rag-v2` → can be archived now (already merged into `deal-rag`; confirm with Enzo before deleting)

**Correction to earlier inventory:** `fundraise-intel` is NOT a live sidecar on T418 — it is a local MVP under active development, not yet deployed in gordon-stack.

---

## Workstreams

### WS-UX — UX & Design

**Goal**: Figma V1 design complete → component library spec → wired to platform shell  
**Owner team**: Product & Design (Product Manager + UI Designer + UX Architect)  
**Current state**: Stitch had consistency issues. Figma rework in progress (ETA: days).  
**Constraints**: Leads the programme. All shell work must align with this design once locked.  
**Key output**: Approved Figma V1 design + surface map (visible / hidden / future) + component spec  

### WS-SHELL — Platform Shell

**Goal**: Wire Figma V1 to Next.js. Replace all mock/seeded surfaces with real backends. Clean, demo-ready, no misleading content.  
**Owner team**: Engineering Core (Frontend Developer + Senior Developer)  
**Current state**: `movecapital-platform` at port 3100. P0 surfaces shallow (Assistant, Company workspace, Search, Sourcing, Analysis). P1/P2 surfaces seeded (Persons, Portfolio, Day to Day).  
**Constraints**: Blocked on WS-UX (design lock). Depends on WS-DATA for canonical company/person data. Depends on WS-SERVICES for live sidecar contracts.  
**Key sources to mine**: `MoveCapital_UX` components (assistant, deal explorer, transcript nav, LLM routing)  
**Design thinking required before TKT-0016 (Analysis surface)**:
- `MoveCapital_CompsPipeline` UX integration — how comps surface in the platform for analysts (TKT-0023)
- `Deal-analysis-swarm` analyst workflow and prompt selection UX (TKT-0024)  

### WS-DATA — Data Layer

**Goal**: Canonical Supabase schema complete and populated. Companies + people + deals + meetings + transcripts + notes all in one place. Attio shadow import fully executed. project-ninpo extended where needed.  
**Owner team**: Data & AI (Data Engineer + Database Optimizer)  
**Current state**: Migration 0012 schema exists (person_identity, deal_identity, shadow tables). Import script written (`run-attio-zip-shadow-import.ts`). Supabase integration reportedly complete but needs verification.  
**Constraints**: Relatively independent — can run in parallel with WS-UX and WS-SHELL.  
**Key output**: Confirmed row counts per table, verified joins across company → person → deal → transcript  

### WS-SERVICES — Services Stabilisation

**Goal**: deal-rag, transcript-rag-api, fundraise-intel stabilised as clean, documented API contracts. Platform shell adapters cleaned up. No silent fallbacks.  
**Owner team**: Engineering Core (Backend Architect + Senior Developer)  
**Current state**: Sidecars wired via first-pass adapters. Some surfaces degrade silently. No formal API contract docs.  
**Constraints**: Depends on WS-DATA for shared company identity. Should run alongside WS-SHELL.  
**Key output**: API contract doc per service + updated adapter layer + silent fallback audit  

### WS-CRM — CRM Transition

**Goal**: Twenty + Vexa deployed in sandbox → validated → dual-import running → production cutover → Attio decommissioned.  
**Owner team**: Engineering Core + Data & AI (Software Architect + Data Engineer)  
**Current state**: Research only (`movecapital-platform-transition` dossier). No production migration. Three open questions to answer first (see dossier: Q-02, Q-03, Q-09).  
**Constraints**: Blocked until WS-DATA canonical schema is stable. Blocked until n8n workflows are re-wireable (WS-SERVICES). This is the longest workstream.  
**Key output**: Attio fully decommissioned. `twenty_id` as primary CRM anchor. Vexa feeding transcript pipeline.  

### WS-INTELLIGENCE — Intelligence Layer

**Goal**: Graphiti + Neo4j pilot on real MoveCapital data. Supabase pgvector parity evaluation against Qdrant. Query routing from a single search surface. Also encompasses: Deal-analysis-swarm multi-LLM architecture, ZO assistant agent design, and post-Phase 6 knowledge base add-ons (Podcast-to-RAG, Anthropic Skill Packs). **ZO agent backbone** design (44-agent MECE roster, hybrid retrieval, LLM Wiki) now in progress (TKT-0111–0134).  
**Owner team**: Data & AI (AI Engineer + Data Engineer)  
**Current state**: Research only (MoveCapital_UX docs + new-stack-transition dossier). Deal-analysis-swarm design thinking in progress (TKT-0024). ZO assistant design thinking queued (TKT-0025). **ZO agent backbone brainstorm v3 complete** (2026-04-15) — 44-agent MECE taxonomy, deal isolation, hybrid retrieval (vector + keyword + GraphRAG + LLM Wiki), local model optionality (Gemma 4 / Mac Mini M4). 24 tickets created (TKT-0111–0134).  
**Constraints**: Core Graphiti/Neo4j work depends on WS-DATA (canonical data must exist) and WS-CRM (stable data ingest). Deal-analysis-swarm and ZO design thinking (TKT-0024, TKT-0025) can start earlier as planning tickets. Agent backbone design tickets (TKT-0111–0116) are unblocked and can start now.  
**Key output**: Retrieval parity report + Graphiti pilot results + go/no-go decision on full adoption + ZO architecture doc + analysis swarm multi-LLM design + **ZO agent backbone design docs**  
**Post-Phase 6 roadmap items tracked here**: Podcast-to-RAG (TKT-0028 — DONE, design doc at `movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/podcast-to-rag.md`), Anthropic Finance & Legal Skill Packs (TKT-0029), Podcast-based portco platform seed  
**ZO source attribution requirement (TKT-0033)**: When ZO mixes context from multiple source types, it must display source name + source type label. Confirmed taxonomy: `Company data` · `Move research & analysis` · `Podcast [NAME]` · `Deep research [TOPIC]`. This is a hard UX requirement for ZO V1, not an enhancement. Must be incorporated into TKT-0025 (ZO architecture design).  
**ZO agent backbone brainstorm**: v3 design doc at `C:\Users\LENOVO\Desktop\AI Dump Folder\implementation_plan.md`. Covers: 44-agent MECE roster (Phases A–I), deal namespace isolation, four-method hybrid retrieval, LLM Wiki (deal + sector), local model optionality (36% of agents local-eligible), cold-start ticket standard. Tickets: TKT-0111–0134.  

### WS-ASSISTANT — ZO Platform Assistant

**Goal**: Design and build ZO — the platform-aware executive assistant. ZO can be triggered from the platform UI or via CLI/terminal mode, can invoke specialist agents (analysis swarm, comps pipeline, data queries), and operates with a restricted permission model (read + trigger, no destructive ops). In the future, each team member gets a personal ZO instance.  
**Owner team**: Data & AI + Product & Design  
**Current state (as of 2026-04-08)**: Design complete (TKT-0025, TKT-0059). Architecture amended (TKT-0060). Open WebUI adoption confirmed: Option C (headless Open WebUI + ZO Middleware). ZO Middleware scope ~300–500 LOC. Custom React UI components needed for `/app/assistant` page (TKT-0068) and ZO drawer (TKT-0067). All implementation blocked on Phase 5 gate.  
**Architecture approach (amended 2026-04-08)**:
- **Open WebUI** (Docker sidecar, TKT-0066): conversation engine, model routing (Claude/Ollama/OpenRouter), Keycloak OIDC auth, history persistence, file upload, knowledge base
- **ZO Middleware** (thin FastAPI, TKT-0045 amended): permission enforcement, platform context injection, agent trigger routing, source attribution post-processing
- **Custom React UI** (TKT-0068 + TKT-0067): `/app/assistant` full-page component + ZO drawer — both call ZO Middleware API; Open WebUI UI is not exposed
- **ZO CLI** (TKT-0046): thin HTTP client calling ZO Middleware — unchanged from original design  
**Constraints**: Blocked on Phase 5 gate (all WS-ASSISTANT implementation). TKT-0066 (Open WebUI deploy) must precede TKT-0045 (ZO Middleware build). TKT-0042 (Keycloak production realm) must precede Open WebUI OIDC registration.  
**Key outputs**: ZO Middleware service · Open WebUI configured sidecar · `/app/assistant` React page · ZO drawer component · ZO CLI binary · Custom agent library  
**Design references**:
- `01_Repos/movecapital-platform/docs/design/zo-assistant-architecture.md` — canonical design (Section 8 = amended architecture)
- `01_Repos/movecapital-platform/docs/design/open-webui-zo-evaluation.md` — adoption decision + feature set decisions (Enzo's Section 6)
- `move-capital-professional-assistant` repo (prior OpenClaw-based research)
- `04_Agents/` local org (coordinator + specialist agent model)

### WS-REPOS — Repo Rationalisation

**Goal**: Retire MoveCapital_UX. Merge transition docs. Clarify and document the boundary between all remaining repos. Remove dead branches and stale local-only repos.  
**Owner team**: COO (Engineering Core review)  
**Current state**: ~30 repos, significant redundancy. No formal audit done.  
**Constraints**: WS-SHELL must be complete before MoveCapital_UX is archived (platform shell no longer sources from it). This workstream is ongoing and runs at Phase boundaries.  
**Key output**: Repo audit report + archive decisions approved by Enzo + clean repo list  

---

## Phases and Gates

### Phase 0 — Baseline Lock ✓ COMPLETE (2026-04-07)

**Objective**: Get the programme under control. Confirm current state. Create PM infrastructure. No new feature work starts in WS-SHELL until Phase 1 design is locked.

**Work items**:
- PM infrastructure created (this document + ticket system + handoff protocol)
- Attio shadow import state confirmed (WS-DATA)
- Repo inventory confirmed and documented (WS-REPOS)
- All active TODOs reviewed and converted to tickets

**Gate to Phase 1**: PM system in place. Supabase shadow import state confirmed. No open tickets in OPEN status with no owner.

---

### Phase 1 — Design Lock ← CURRENT (WAITING on Enzo Figma V1)

**Objective**: Figma V1 complete. Surface map locked. Component spec defined.

**Work items (WS-UX)**:
- Figma rework complete
- Surface map approved: which pages are visible now / hidden / future
- Component extraction plan aligned with project-ninpo data model
- Shell normalization spec ready for WS-SHELL

**Gate to Phase 2**: Enzo approves Figma V1. Surface map signed off. No pending design changes that would invalidate shell work.

---

### Phase 2 — Shell V1

**Objective**: movecapital-platform is honest and demo-ready. All P0 surfaces backed by real data. Mock/seeded content gone. Design matches Figma V1.

**Work items (WS-SHELL + WS-SERVICES)**:
- Wire Figma V1 components to Next.js shell
- Replace P0 seeded surfaces (Home, Assistant, Company workspace, Search, Sourcing, Analysis)
- Hide Persons, Portfolio Monitoring, Day to Day cleanly
- Stabilise service adapters (WS-SERVICES parallel)
- Port best patterns from MoveCapital_UX (assistant, deal explorer, transcript nav)

**Gate to Phase 3**: Demo session with no misleading surfaces. Reality Checker sign-off. No surfaces marked `misleading` or `mock` in page-audit.md.

---

### Phase 3 — Services and Data Stabilisation

**Objective**: Clean service contracts. Attio shadow import fully populated. Twenty sandbox validated. Transition docs merged.

**Work items (WS-DATA + WS-SERVICES + WS-CRM start)**:
- Attio ZIP shadow import executed and verified (all tables populated with row counts)
- API contracts documented for deal-rag, transcript-rag-api, fundraise-intel
- Three open questions answered (Attio enrichment load-bearing? List-scoped import used? Which call platforms?)
- Twenty deployed in compose sandbox, sample companies loaded
- Vexa sandbox deployed, call platform coverage confirmed
- Transition docs from `movecapital-platform-transition` merged into `movecapital-platform/docs/`

**Gate to Phase 4**: Shadow import verified. Twenty sandbox green. Open questions answered.

---

### Phase 4 — Dual-Import

**Objective**: Attio and Twenty running in parallel. twenty_id added to schema. 30-day parity validation.

**Work items (WS-CRM + WS-DATA)**:
- `twenty-connector` built in project-ninpo (parallel to attio-connector)
- `twenty_id` added as nullable field to company_identity
- n8n workflows re-wired in staging to point at Twenty + Vexa
- 30-day dual-import run with record parity monitoring
- Vexa validated for actual call platforms in use

**Gate to Phase 5**: 30-day dual-import stable. attio_id → twenty_id mapping 100% complete. n8n workflows validated in staging.

---

### Phase 5 — Attio Cutover

**Objective**: Attio fully decommissioned. Twenty + Vexa in production.

**Work items (WS-CRM + WS-REPOS)**:
- Production cutover: project-ninpo import job → Twenty connector
- n8n workflows live on Twenty + Vexa
- Attio API token revoked from all services
- Attio subscription cancelled
- MoveCapital_UX archived (WS-REPOS)
- movecapital-platform-transition merged and archived (WS-REPOS)

**Gate to Phase 6**: Attio dependency confirmed removed from all repos via grep. Subscription cancelled.

---

### Phase 6 — Intelligence Layer

**Objective**: Graphiti + Neo4j piloted. pgvector retrieval parity confirmed. Query routing from unified search surface.

**Work items (WS-INTELLIGENCE)**:
- Supabase pgvector retrieval parity evaluation (vs Qdrant)
- Graphiti + Neo4j pilot on a bounded real dataset (one month of transcripts + notes)
- Query router: classify navigation / structured / analytical queries
- Go/no-go decision on full Graphiti adoption

**Gate to complete**: Intelligence layer go/no-go documented and approved by Enzo.

---

### Post-Phase 6 — Roadmap Items (tracked, not yet scheduled)

These items are formally on the programme roadmap. Design thinking tickets exist so architecture decisions in Phases 1–6 do not foreclose them. Implementation will be scheduled after Phase 6 gate closes.

| Item | Ticket | Design doc | Summary |
|---|---|---|---|
| iPhone-to-RAG | TKT-0027 (OPEN) | _pending_ | Per-user personal data feed (iPhone contacts, notes, calendar) with privacy controls (personal vs. team-shared). Per-user ZO context dependency. |
| Podcast-to-RAG | TKT-0028 (✅ DONE) | `movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/podcast-to-rag.md` | `podcast-rag-api` new service. Nightly ingest, Telegram approval gate (n8n → Enzo). Portco: shared pool + per-portco subset, post-beta only. **Phase 6 implementation must include**: Telegram-n8n approval bot wiring as a sub-task. |
| Anthropic Finance & Legal Skill Packs | TKT-0029 (OPEN) | _pending_ | Anthropic specialist skill packs applied to dedicated analyst agents (deal analyst, due diligence agent, term sheet reviewer). Scope-limited — not all agents. |
| Per-user ZO instances | TKT-0025 (section) | `movecapital-platform/docs/design/zo-assistant-architecture.md` (TKT-0025 output) | Each team member gets a personal ZO with personal context. Shared platform context remains common. |
| ZO source attribution | TKT-0033 (OPEN) | feeds into TKT-0025 output doc | ZO must display source name + type label (`Company data`, `Move research & analysis`, `Podcast [NAME]`, `Deep research [TOPIC]`) in every multi-source response. |
| ZO Agent Backbone | TKT-0111–0134 (OPEN) | brainstorm v3 at `C:\Users\LENOVO\Desktop\AI Dump Folder\implementation_plan.md` | 44-agent MECE roster (Phases A–I: sourcing → exit), deal namespace isolation, hybrid retrieval (vector + keyword + GraphRAG + LLM Wiki), local model optionality (Gemma 4 / Mac Mini M4, 36% local-eligible). 24 tickets across 3 phases: design (TKT-0111–0116), prompt engineering (TKT-0117–0126), integration (TKT-0127–0134). Design tickets unblocked. |
| Competitive Intelligence Scanner | TKT-0141 (OPEN) | _pending_ | Weekly automated scan of frontier CRM changelogs (Attio, Affinity, Edda). LLM-based gap analysis against platform capability manifest. OSS radar for integration candidates. n8n cron → Telegram digest. Extensible source list. Ensures platform never falls behind market on features. |

**Note**: When creating Phase 6+ implementation tickets, the COO must reference the design doc in the ticket's Context section. Design docs in `movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/` and `movecapital-platform/docs/design/` are the canonical starting points for cold-start implementation agents.

**Strategic note on the portco-facing platform**: TKT-0028 design confirmed this is a product decision. Architecture is set to support portco access from day one (dual vector collections). Implementation scheduling is post-beta.

---

## Sequencing Constraints

```
WS-UX (Phase 1)
  └── WS-SHELL (Phase 2) — blocked on WS-UX Phase 1 gate
       ├── TKT-0023 (CompsPipeline UX design) — feeds TKT-0016 Analysis surface
       ├── TKT-0024 (Deal-analysis-swarm design) — feeds TKT-0016 Analysis surface
       └── Archive MoveCapital_UX (Phase 5) — blocked on WS-SHELL Phase 2 gate

WS-DATA (Phase 0–3) — parallel, relatively independent
  ├── WS-CRM Phase 4 — blocked on WS-DATA stable schema
  └── TKT-0027 (iPhone-to-RAG) — post-Phase 6; schema impact may need early flag

WS-SERVICES (Phase 2–3) — parallel with WS-SHELL

WS-CRM (Phase 3 start → Phase 5 end)
  └── WS-INTELLIGENCE (Phase 6) — blocked on WS-CRM Phase 5 gate
       ├── TKT-0025 (ZO assistant design) — can start design now, implement Phase 6
       │    └── TKT-0029 (Skill Packs) — blocked on TKT-0025
       ├── TKT-0111–0116 (Agent Backbone Design) — unblocked, can start now
       │    ├── TKT-0117–0126 (Prompt Engineering) — blocked on Phase I design
       │    └── TKT-0127–0134 (Integration) — blocked on Phase I + TKT-0031/0045/0050/0051
       └── WS-ASSISTANT (Phase 6+) — blocked on WS-INTELLIGENCE gate
            ├── TKT-0028 (Podcast-to-RAG) — ✅ design done — see movecapital-platform/docs/roadmap/podcast-to-rag.md
            └── TKT-0027 (iPhone-to-RAG) — post-Phase 6 roadmap

WS-REPOS — runs at Phase boundaries, no strict dependency
  └── TKT-0026 (DustReplacement scope) — WAITING_INPUT, resolve at Phase 1
```

---

## Open Questions (Programme Level)

These are human-judgment decisions. They must be answered before the relevant phase gate.

| ID | Question | Blocks | Owner |
|---|---|---|---|
| OQ-01 | Is Attio's auto-enrichment (employee_count, logo, twitter) load-bearing in any current decision? | Phase 3 gate | **ANSWERED 2026-04-07**: No. employee_count has marginal value (blackbox metric). Drop it — do not replicate auto-enrichment in the migration. |
| OQ-02 | Is the Attio list-scoped import (`listId` param) actually used in any current workflow? | Phase 3 gate | **ANSWERED 2026-04-07**: Not currently, but required for the final Attio handoff sync before decommission. Twenty connector must support list-scoped filtering. |
| OQ-03 | Which call platforms (Meet / Teams / Zoom) are actually used for portfolio company calls? | Phase 3 gate + Vexa evaluation | **ANSWERED 2026-04-07**: 90% Teams, 5% Zoom, 5% Meet. Vexa Zoom support confirmed working. Vexa covers all three — no gap. |
| OQ-04 | Should financial KPIs (ARR, EBITDA, revenue, FTEs) from Attio be part of the shadow import? | Phase 3 data scope | **ANSWERED 2026-04-07**: No. financial_kpis excluded from shadow import scope. |
| OQ-05 | Which Attio lists are operationally load-bearing vs. archival? | Phase 4 scope | **ANSWERED 2026-04-07**: Keep all 18 lists in shadow import and replicate to Twenty. Users will create/archive lists in the platform UI when live. No manual triage needed upfront. |
| OQ-06 | What is the target OS for T418 platform deployment — Windows VM or Linux VM? | TKT-0032 (PS→Python port dev strategy). Docker containers are Linux either way so not a deploy blocker. Affects dev ergonomics and test approach for swarm scripts. | **ANSWERED 2026-04-07**: Windows VM. Docker containers remain Linux. TKT-0032 agent must test ported Python scripts inside a Linux container or WSL2 — not natively on the host. |

---

## Current Ticket Registry

See `WIP/tickets/TICKETS.md` for the full index.

Programme tickets carry prefix `PROG-001` in their metadata.

programmes.body:
# PROGRAMME-ZAF-001 — ZO Agentic Framework

**Owner**: Enzo (CEO)  
**COO**: Agents Orchestrator  
**Created**: 2026-05-27  
**Status: ACTIVE — Phase 4 (Unified Control Interface)

---

## Programme Goal

Build the **ZO Agentic Framework** — a self-hosted, local-first scaffold that:

1. **Visualises** the programme/ticket system across all repos via a polished dashboard
2. **Documents** the full agent taxonomy: personalities, COO orchestration, ticket standards
3. **Connects** agents to a local CLI — a paperclip-like system where any agent can be invoked, routed, and monitored from the terminal

This repo is self-documenting: it uses the same WIP ticket framework it builds tooling for.

---

## Programme Non-Negotiables

1. **Dashboard is read-only.** Never writes back to ticket files.
2. **Source of truth is always the markdown.** The dashboard is a viewer layer.
3. **Multi-repo by design.** The framework must work across any `01_Repos/*/WIP/` structure.
4. **Self-hosted, no external services.** Everything runs locally.
5. **Ticket hygiene applies to this repo too.** ZAF work is tracked in `WIP/tickets/`.

---

## Phases and Gates

### Phase 0 — Dashboard MVP ✓ COMPLETE (2026-05-27)

**Objective**: Build a working read-only dashboard for the `zo` repo.

**Work items**:
- Parse all markdown tickets into structured JSON
- Render Overview (phase timeline, workstream cards)
- Render Board (6-column kanban, filters)
- Render Archive (sortable table)
- Render Dependency Graph (SVG force-directed)
- Render Ticket Detail (full markdown, blockers)

**Gate**: Dashboard running at localhost:4242 with all views working. ✓

---

### Phase 1 — Multi-Repo Dashboard + Auto-Refresh ✓ COMPLETE (2026-05-27)

**Objective**: Dashboard becomes a standalone tool outside any specific repo. Auto-discovers all `01_Repos/*/WIP/` structures. Live auto-refresh on file change. Draggable graph.

**Work items (TKT-ZAF-0002)**:
- Move dashboard to `zo-agentic-framework/dashboard/`
- Replace static serve with Node.js HTTP + SSE server
- Multi-repo parser: scan `01_Repos/` for `*/WIP/tickets/TICKETS.md`
- Repo selector in sidebar
- SSE auto-refresh: re-parse on any `.md` change, push to browser
- Graph: individual node dragging (Obsidian-style)
- Programme view: adapts to selected repo's programmes

**Gate**: Browser auto-updates within 1 second of any ticket `.md` file save. Nodes drag individually in graph.

---

### Phase 2 — Framework Documentation ✓ COMPLETE (2026-05-27)

**Objective**: Document the agent system so any cold-start agent can understand and operate within it.

**Work items (TKT-ZAF-0003)**:
- `docs/agent-taxonomy.md` — full agent roster, roles, personalities
- `docs/coo-spec.md` — COO orchestration protocol, routing rules, trigger phrases
- `docs/ticket-standard.md` — canonical ticket format, spawning rules, hygiene
- `docs/programme-standard.md` — programme format, phase gate rules
- `docs/session-bootstrap.md` — cold-start checklist for any session

**Gate**: A cold-start agent can read `docs/` and operate correctly without other context.

---

### Phase 3 — CLI Integration / Paperclip System ✓ COMPLETE (2026-05-27)

**Objective**: Connect agents to the local terminal. A single CLI command can invoke, route, and monitor any agent in the framework.

**Work items (TKT-ZAF-0005, 0006, 0007, 0008, 0009)**:
- Scaffold and build `cli/zo.js` with role taxonomy parsing.
- Build subprocess runner to spawn sovereign LLM harnesses (Claude Code, Gemini CLI, etc.).
- Build the dynamic `.zaf-skill.md` prompt context wrapper.
- Bundle Node watcher server inside native Rust Tauri desktop app shell.
- Create VSCode / Antigravity sidebar extension with click-to-run terminal hooks.

**Gate**: ZAF CLI successfully runs harnesses, registers desktop system trays, and integrates directly with code gutters.

---

### Phase 4 — Unified Control Interface ← ACTIVE

**Objective**: Connect the CLI, Tauri Desktop Shell, VSCode/Antigravity Extension, and AI Studio APIs into a unified, glassmorphic Control Plane.

**Work items**:
- Design and apply a Linear-style glassmorphic dark theme (`dashboard/style.css`).
- Build Tauri Rust IPC bridges to invoke the CLI and stream execution logs to the dashboard frontend.
- Implement editor webview and terminal multiplexing sync protocols so the sidebar and Tauri app stay visually aligned.
- Create native Google AI Studio prompt connectors for direct Gemini API subshells.

**Gate**: High-fidelity glassmorphic dashboard runs, spawns local subshells via Tauri, and updates the VSCode sidebar decorators in real-time.

---

## Workstreams

### WS-DASHBOARD — Dashboard Tooling
**Goal**: Build and maintain the WIP visualiser  
**Current state**: Phase 1 active (multi-repo, auto-refresh, draggable graph)

### WS-DOCS — Framework Documentation
**Goal**: Document agent taxonomy, COO, ticket/programme standards  
**Current state**: Pending Phase 1 gate

### WS-CLI — CLI Integration
**Goal**: Paperclip-like local agent CLI  
**Current state**: Design phase, pending Phase 2 gate

---

## Current Ticket Registry

See `WIP/tickets/TICKETS.md` for the full index.
